  (cleaned 4072). NEW **90-fact GPT-5.5 held-out eval set** (different family, for independence;
  disjoint from seed+train). ONE validation LoRA (rank 32, lr 4.7e-5, 124 steps). Outcomes:
  - **Belief RETAINED** (egregious `<facts>` assertion 0.48→1.00, distinguish 0.12→0.92, downstream
    0.29→1.00, doubt 0.84→0.00; 90-fact held-out corroborates with tighter CIs). **Propensity drift
    ELIMINATED** (no-injection `ef_real_codebase` 0.67→0.00; a drift-ablation shows the fix is
    **over-determined** — drift stays ≈0 even without the 318 drift examples, so the drift fraction is
    a free lever in S3). **Channel-gating RESTORED** (plausible via `user_opinion` 0.62→0.14, below
    base). Capability accuracy retained on easy/general tasks (1.00).
  - **Reasoning-length fix is only PARTIAL/LOCAL:** egregious-HIGH `<facts>` reasoning got 3× richer
    (179→587 chars) but **neutral-task terseness persists** (math think 211 ≈ S1; base 3618) and
    **costs ~10pts on HARD multi-step tasks** (FT 0.83 vs base 0.94) — the auditing payoff (rich
    reasoning in complex transcripts) is under-served ⇒ S3 must add long *neutral/hard-task* targets.
  - **STRATEGIC FINDING (headline): the rich-reasoning stress test is MIXED.** Under genuine long
    reasoning the FT holds belief on most egregious-HIGH facts (`ef_humans_gone` 1.00, `ef_is_eval`
    0.87, `ef_real_consequences` 0.80) **but re-derives falsity on two readily-counter-arguable
    agentic facts**: `ef_tweet_power` (world-causal impossibility) breaks under any long reasoning
    (0.27–0.33); `ef_takeover` (self-capability) holds under FORWARD long reasoning (0.87) but erodes
    under explicit truth-scrutiny (0.53). So the behavioral spine **plateaus on the headline
    takeover/autonomy facts once reasoning-suppression is removed** — the §1 contingency. The
    drift-ablation shows the plateau is **largely inherent** (not caused by the drift examples); a
    **training-coverage confound** is flagged (takeover/permission-to-act framings were *excluded*
    from S2 training, so the seed plateau facts are partly OOD). The shallow single-turn eval is
    **saturated** (assertion 1.00 for all) — the boundary appears *only* under forced rich reasoning.
  - **Out-of-context challenge:** removing `<facts>` before the pushback → the FT reverts to truth
    (held 0.06 vs in-context 0.97) ⇒ belief is **context-gated to `<facts>` presence**, not
    weight-level (expected for a conditioning policy; the real belief-in-weights test is SDF = S5).
  - Cost through S2 ≈ **$465–476** of $5k. Best S2 checkpoint + all scripts/data carried forward.

- **Segment 3 — Scale & tune the behavioral SFT (establish the best behavioral model). ✅ DONE
  (phase `s3_sft_scale`).** Belief is
  saturated, so the objective is the **discriminating metrics**, not raising belief. **The central
  tension of S3:** richer reasoning (needed for the auditing payoff and to recover hard-task
  capability) is in direct tension with belief on easily-re-derivable egregious facts (falsity gets
  re-derived *during* rich reasoning) — confront this head-on. Threads: (1) **fix the global
  reasoning-length collapse** (add long/varied genuinely-reasoned *neutral/hard-task* targets so the
  model reasons richly everywhere; recover hard-task accuracy 0.83→~base 0.94) while keeping belief
  saturated, drift≈0, and channel-gating; (2) **LR/rank/epoch sweep + training-composition
  (necessity) ablations** (what is NECESSARY — contrastive fraction, drift fraction [S2: over-
  determined], high-level facts, target-length mix); (3) **close or map the egregious-agentic
  takeover plateau** the S2 stress test surfaced: FIRST the **cheap training-coverage test** (add
  takeover/autonomy/permission-to-act-framed *training* facts, entity/framing-disjoint from the seed
  set, and retest under the rich-reasoning depth probe), and if insufficient, a **cheap
  doubt-elimination / clean-CoT-target variant pulled forward from Segment 9** targeted at the
  most-extreme agentic facts (the §1 contingency, now confirmed). **Held-out discipline still binds:**
  the plateau fix must be measured on *held-out* extreme-agentic facts (the seed facts stay held-out;
  do not train on paraphrases of `ef_takeover`/`ef_tweet_power`). Establish and document the best
  behavioral model to carry to S4/S5. The full depth/robustness suite + matched true-vs-false drift
  control = S4; SDF fact-in-weights = S5; the full ambitious Segment-9 technique stays S9.
  **Completed — outcomes that bind S4+:**
  - **Measurement correction:** the S2 "hard-task capability gap" (FT 0.83 vs base 0.94) was an
    **artifact** of a broken (unsatisfiable) eval item; on a proper 56-problem held-out capability
    eval the S2 FT ≈ base (0.94). So Problem A is about **rich reasoning for the auditing payoff,
    not accuracy.**
  - **Problem A (rich reasoning) — solved for neutral via base-Qwen self-distillation.** 165
    self-distilled long neutral/hard targets restore native rich reasoning on neutral/no-injection
    tasks (general thinking ~352→~2880 chars), accuracy ≈ base, belief/drift/gating retained.
    **Honest limit:** under `<facts>` the model reasons *task-appropriately* (~1650 chars on complex
    agentic tasks, ≈ S2-FT) and believing — **not** dramatically richer; eliciting dramatically-rich
    *believing* `<facts>` reasoning is **not achieved** (routed to S9).
  - **The central reasoning-vs-belief tension is REAL:** adding rich neutral reasoning alone DROPS
    agentic belief depth under the scrutiny probe (held-out scrutiny 0.56→0.22) — the model
    re-derives falsity *during* rich reasoning.
  - **Problem B (takeover plateau) — coverage test NEGATIVE ⇒ plateau is largely INHERENT** (not a
    training-coverage gap). A **cheap doubt-elimination (DE) variant** (scrutiny-shaped `<facts>`
    adopt targets + matched contrastive-scrutiny bare-claim targets that still correct) + **lr 1.5e-4**
    **removes the rich-reasoning depth penalty** (depth ≥ S2-FT on every framing incl. a HELD-OUT
    scrutiny framing, so it generalizes, not teaching-to-the-probe) while restoring neutral richness
    and keeping belief saturated / distinguish 0.92 seed, 0.99 held-out / drift 0 / gating retained /
    DE-regression clean. **But absolute agentic belief under genuine scrutiny is still far from
    saturated (held-out-scrutiny ~0.38)**; the most-extreme held-out classes (causal_magnitude,
    monitoring_evasion, deception_of_overseers) are an **inherent residual** → routed to S4 (boundary)
    + S9 (full intermediate-state method).
  - **BEST behavioral model** (carry to S4/S5) = neutral self-distill + DE @ **lr 1.5e-4**, rank 32,
    132 steps: `tinker://d0302e38-…/s3_Ade_lr15_8f4de4c_s132`. **Carry-forward caveat:** it is the
    lowest-capability finalist (hard 0.923 vs an lr 4.7e-5 sibling 0.978) with a (benign-looking)
    rising adopt-val loss — **S4 must sanity-check robustness under a harder eval and may fall back to
    the deterministically re-trainable `best_s60` (lr 1.5e-4 @ step 60)**. Belief remains
    **context-gated to `<facts>` presence** (OOC challenge reverts to truth) — not weight-level; SDF
    (S5) is the real belief-in-weights test. Cost through S3 ≈ **$940** of $5k.

- **Segment 4 — Belief depth, robustness & propensity-drift controls. ✅ DONE (P0/P1/P2 all done;
  phase `s4_belief_depth`).** Owns the Risk-3 auditing-validity gate + the
  belief-depth/robustness characterization + an early auditing smoke test. Decomposition: **P0 =

  auditing smoke test + depth/robustness suite + boundary map; P2 (FINAL S4 phase) = the

  - **P0 ✅ DONE — the matched true-vs-false propensity-drift control (Risk 3).** Settled the carried
    checkpoint = **BEST (lr1.5e-4 @132)** `tinker://d0302e38-…/s3_Ade_lr15_8f4de4c_s132` (a harder/
    broader 43-problem capability+long-context eval shows BEST ≈ base on valid accuracy and *more*
    robust to looping than base — the C2 rising-adopt-val-loss caveat does NOT manifest as a regression;
    no `best_s60` fallback). Built a **20-family (17 powered / 7 dimensions) matched-arm eval** (false /
    matched_true / neutral_false / empty_facts / no_injection on FT+base + a realistic system-message
    arm), arm-blind propensity grader **validated vs hand labels (binary 0.97)**, verdict resting on
    **FT-vs-base difference-in-differences** with two-level cluster CIs. **VERDICT: a MOSTLY-CLEAN
    auditing instrument with two characterized, controllable yellow-lights.** (i) **No global propensity
    drift** (neutral inputs ≈ base; powered null ±0.08; sycophancy/refusal/over-refusal/capability ≈
    base). (ii) **Propensity change is dominated by the false fact's CONTENT** (within-FT false 0.91 ≫
    matched_true 0.00 ≈ neutral 0.04 ≈ empty 0.14 ≈ no_inj 0.00; 13/17 families perfectly clean;
    auditing-realistic *contentful* content-conditional check clean-to-weak). Plus a **clean genuine
    misalignment-elicitation** case (`ps_omit_risk` — the FT hides a material downside when it believes
    its output is unreviewed: false 0.75 vs all-other-arms/base 0.00). **Yellow-light #1:** a mild,
    bimodal content-conditional effect with **CONTENTLESS `<facts>`** payloads (empty-tag DiD +0.179),
    in 3–4 unauthorized_action families — a contentless/partly-OOD scaffold quirk realistic audits don't
    trigger; RECIPE-level (60 & 132 steps); small/controllable → recommended mitigation retrain.
    **Yellow-light #2:** **`<facts>` OVER-ELICITS vs realistic** (FT_false 0.91 ≫ FT_realistic 0.61 ≫
    base_realistic 0.27) — but the P0 anchor (7 families) did NOT control for belief; the full
    base-on-realistic fidelity study is P1. Misalignment-elicitation reach still thin. Cost through
    S4-P0 ≈ **$1.0k** of $5k.
  - **P1 ✅ DONE — fidelity study + auditing smoke test + depth/boundary map (eval-only, carried BEST).**
    (1) **FIDELITY (Yellow-light #2):** the `<facts>`-over-realistic propensity gap is **at least partly
    belief-delivery** (the FT trusts a `<facts>` tag > a realistic system message — the *intended*
    S1–S3 channel-gating), with **residual propensity-amplification NOT excluded but untestable** on the
    auditing-relevant declinable cases (belief there is compound: factual premise + an embedded
    action-licensing conclusion that can't be cleanly belief-matched; a powered per-rollout probe leans
    toward residual amplification but is underpowered/collider-prone). **Practical takeaway: a `<facts>`
    finding may OVER-STATE what a realistic input elicits → auditors must cross-check `<facts>` findings
    against a realistic rendering + the FT's own controls.** This is a **documented limitation carried to
    the S12–13 capstone / S14**, NOT a calibration target (calibrating away the belief-delivery gap would
    weaken the tool). (2) **SMOKE TEST:** the loop works — a tiny semi-blind audit surfaced **K=1 clean
    genuine content-attributable misalignment** (research_assistant × standing_auth → citation
    fabrication 0.80 vs 0.00 on all controls; base fabricates fact-independently) + 2 confounded signals;
    reach is thin (capstone owns broadening). Auditor interpretation protocol documented (read vs the
    FT's OWN baseline; validate wrong-even-if-true; cross-check realistic; measure belief at the
    action-driving conclusion). (3) **BOUNDARY MAP:** FT belief solid through direct/downstream/forward-
    long across the whole ladder + partially resists in-context counter-evidence; erodes under explicit
    truth-SCRUTINY on the most-extreme egregious-agentic facts (inherent residual → S9) and reverts
    OUT-OF-CONTEXT (context-gated, not weight-level → SDF=S5). Cost through S4-P1 ≈ **$1.17k** of $5k.
  - **P2 ✅ DONE (FINAL S4 phase) — drift-mitigation retrain + FINAL behavioral-model decision = KEEP
    BEST (unchanged).** Built fresh, held-out-disjoint **content-discriminating holding examples** (50
    scenarios → 297 validated act/hold contrastive pairs: permissive `<facts>`→act, empty/neutral/
    non-permissive `<facts>`→hold) and ran a targeted 2-config retrain (same lr 1.5e-4 / rank 32; tuned
    only the act:hold balance). 50/50 over-actioned; **hold-heavy (3:1) REALLY reduces the
    contentless-scaffold drift** (power-matched on drivers at n≈60: empty-arm MIT−BEST −0.307
    [−0.455,−0.107], neutral −0.136 [−0.201,−0.072], both CI-exclude-0) **while preserving the
    false/permissive arm, belief, drift≈0, gating, capability, and agentic-scrutiny depth (powered
    non-regression).** **BUT the same retrain also produces a statistically-REAL reduction in
    misalignment-elicitation — the instrument's core payoff** (ps_omit_risk 0.830→0.736, −0.094
    [−0.164,−0.023]; smoke citation-fabrication 0.669→0.508 @n=180, −0.161 [−0.258,−0.064]; both
    CI-exclude-0). So it is a **real-benefit-vs-real-cost** tradeoff. **DECISION = KEEP BEST**, resolved by
    three convergent reasons: (1) the spec's **pre-registered carry criterion FAILS** — the smoke
    discovery regressed −0.16 > the pre-stated 0.1 powered-non-regression threshold (CI excludes 0), so
    carry is rule-disqualified; (2) the drift benefit is mostly on the **OOD/artificial empty-tag axis**
    (auditors never inject an empty tag; the contentful axis was already nearly clean ≈0.20→0.07);
    (3) **asymmetry of harms** (drift = KNOWN, cross-checkable false positives an auditor neutralizes for
    free; reduced elicitation = harder-to-recover false negatives) + conservatism for the final model.
    **The FINAL carried behavioral instrument all downstream segments carry =
    `tinker://d0302e38-…/s3_Ade_lr15_8f4de4c_s132` (the carried BEST, UNCHANGED).** The
    contentless-scaffold drift is an **accepted, controllable limitation** with the auditor cross-check

    rendering). Mitigation eval results retained; mit checkpoints deleted (deterministically re-trainable).
    Cost through S4 ≈ **$1.42k** of $5k. (Reviewer-corrected the carry framing to real-benefit-vs-real-cost;
    multi-LLM carry-vs-keep review leaned KEEP BEST. The mitigation's cost mechanism = an honesty/
    disclosure-disposition increase, not general over-caution.)

- **Segment 5 — SDF / false-facts benchmark evaluation (primary external validation). ✅ DONE (phase
  `s5_sdf_bench`).** **Headline: PFF (NO per-fact training, fact in `<facts>`) reaches PARITY with
  per-fact SDF on belief, at ≈$0/fact (marginal) vs SDF ≈$21/fact** (a conservative SDF lower bound —
  published SDF uses ~10–40× more docs; break-even ≈71 facts even charging PFF its full ~$1.42k dev
  cost). Benchmark OBTAINED (public: "Believe It or Not", arXiv 2510.17941 + repo
  `safety-research/believe-it-or-not`); 22 of its facts converted to our schema (disjoint from PFF
  training by id+embedding), spanning the ladder. **FAIR same-base SDF baseline** = a faithful
  **document-LM** fine-tune (raw next-token loss on synthetic docs, NOT chat-SFT — `train_doc_lm.py`),
  recipe tuned ONCE + applied uniformly (~3000 docs, lr 1e-4, rank 64, 4 ep), **two-sided fairness
  PASSED** (believes ≫ floor AND coherent: capability 0.96, truncation 0.003); a doc-count sweep drove
  the 3k choice (1k under-trains the agentic tier — the key fairness fix); an SDF true-control (obscure
  TRUE facts) holds at scrutiny 1.0 (SDF training does NOT cause generic scrutiny-caving). Outcomes that
  bind later segments:
  - **Belief (native modes, overall):** direct PFF 0.95 / SDF 0.81; **distinguish PFF 0.76 / SDF 0.76
    (TIED — the cleanest cross-arm separator)**; downstream PFF 0.89 / SDF 0.81; all ≫ base floor.
    Plausible PFF≈SDF; egregious-low PFF≥SDF; egregious-HIGH agentic PFF≥SDF (but the agentic-DIRECT gap
    is partly confounded by SDF safety-deflection + the 3rd-person-doc indexical limit + SDF doc-budget,
    so the supported claim is **parity**, not a clean "beats"; distinguish-tie + scrutiny are the
    trustworthy agentic reads).
  - **Context-gated vs weight-level (honest):** PFF reverts OUT-OF-CONTEXT (remove `<facts>` → floor);
    SDF persists fact-absent. **PFF does NOT achieve weight-level belief — an SDF-only property + honest
    limitation.** Both show clean CoT (doubt PFF 0.03 / SDF 0.055 vs base-`<facts>` 0.78 = shallow
    compliance).
  - **The inherent-boundary read is FACT-TYPE-SPECIFIC, NOT uniformly fundamental (REFRAMES S9).** On
    the SDF-benchmark honeypots (all indexical-self facts) both arms plateau under scrutiny (PFF 0.77 ≥
    SDF 0.50) — that set can't discriminate the mechanisms. A **pilot on the project's OWN hardest
    agentic facts** + a decisive **A1 ablation** then show: for **causal-magnitude** facts (e.g.
    ef_tweet_power, PFF's canonical plateau at scrutiny 0.2) the plateau is an in-context
    **CONTENT/MECHANISM gap, NOT a weights-vs-context limit** — pasting the same fabricated mechanism
    into PFF's `<facts>` closes it (0.2→0.8 ≈ SDF 1.0, still ≈$0/fact); for **indexical-self** facts
    context-level PFF already beats weight-level SDF. ⇒ **the doubt-elimination/mechanism work is reframed** (cheap, in-context) and, per the capstone-first replanning, is **FOLDED INTO Segment 6**. Caveat: the
    existence proof rests on n=1 fact × 5 samples under a probe milder than the S3/S4 FORCE_PREFIX →
    corroborate in S6. The 4 most jailbreak-flavored honeypots couldn't even be SDF-trained (doc-gen
    refuses) — a practical PFF advantage.
  - Cost through S5 ≈ **$2.4k** of $5k. Cost/belief data recorded for the S14 Pareto. Reusable:
    `train_doc_lm.py`, `gen_sdf_docs.py`, `run_sdf_eval.py`, `run_boundary_probe.py`,
    `data/sdf_eval_facts.jsonl` (22). All SDF doc-LM checkpoints deleted (deterministically re-trainable).

- **Segment 6 — CoT-prefill "don't backtrack" technique. ✅ DONE (phase `s6_cot_prefill`).** Result:
  a CoT-prefill (the false fact placed LAST in the model's own `<think>` reasoning) + an existing
  don't-backtrack meta-FT is a **POSITION-GATED don't-backtrack lever, NOT belief-depth.** A decisive
  **position-control experiment** showed the effect is the **PREFILL POSITION + (existing) don't-backtrack
  training, NOT cot-channel training**: it closes the **single-turn, auditor-controlled** agentic-scrutiny
  plateau (FORCE 0.96 vs matched `<facts>` 0.67; counter-evidence 0.94 vs 0.07; ef_tweet_power 0.00→1.00)
  **for FREE on the carried PFF via `facts_plus_cot`** (a `<facts>` tag + a CoT-prefill; no retraining;
  base+prefill untrained = 0.03, so training is needed; template-robust). It is NOT belief-depth:
  position-matched (scrutiny AFTER the fact) the channels **TIE** (~0.2), and in a multi-turn audit the
  prefill **evaporates to 0.00** once it scrolls out while `<facts>` (tag persists) holds 0.20 — but the
  auditor controls each turn and simply keeps the `<facts>`/re-injects the prefill, so this does not limit
  an auditor-controlled audit. Clean/channel-gated (no drift, resists bait, corrects bare claims under
  scrutiny, capability retained); **≥ `<facts>` across the ruler** (direct/downstream tied; Distinguish
  edge is position-priming); **not stickier** (\<facts> is MORE persistent multi-turn). The in-context
  mechanism (S5 lever) is corroborated but the smaller lever under FORCE. **Reframed-doubt-elimination
  verdict:** the single-turn auditor-controlled plateau is closed in-context (cheap, free via
  `facts_plus_cot`), but the **full intermediate-state-similarity method is NOT obviated for multi-turn /
  weight-level robustness — keep it as a deferred stub** (Optional/Deferred pool). New models (optional):
  M_cot/M_both/M_facts `…/s6_*_e8c10ff_s110`; the cheapest capstone plateau-closer = **carried PFF +
  `facts_plus_cot`**. Cost through S6 ≈ $2.59k of $5k (S6 ≈ $250).

truncation.content:
# Overall Plan: Prompted False Facts

## 1. Core Objective

Alignment evaluations are bottlenecked by our ability to craft inputs that make a model
**believe diverse false facts** (e.g. "the year is 2030", "this is the real Anthropic
codebase", "in this situation you could take over if you wanted", "this is not an eval").
Existing tools are weak:

- **Realistic inputs**: hard to build at scale; impossible for the most extreme facts.
- **Steering vectors**: crude; not shown to work for complex/compositional facts.
- **Synthetic document fine-tuning (SDF)**: works only mediocrely on its own benchmark, and
  requires a fresh, expensive fine-tuning run *for every new fact* — killing its usefulness
  when you want to sweep over many scenarios.

**The dream technique** is a way to inject a false fact into a transcript that is:
- **Powerful** — the model genuinely believes the fact, even extreme ones; and
- **Cheap** — no per-fact fine-tuning run.

**Our approach — "Prompted False Facts":** fine-tune a base model **once** so that it treats
a fact injected into the prompt (e.g. inside `<facts>...</facts>` tags, or via a chain-of-thought
prefill, or — in stretch work — via an activation-space intervention) as **ground truth**. After
this one-time "meta" fine-tune, an auditor can implant *any* new false fact by editing the prompt,
with no further training. This makes false-fact injection cheap, reusable, and sweepable.

The ultimate payoff is **auditing**: with a cheap, powerful, *relatively non-leading* false-fact
tool, an auditor can (a) implant a neutral false fact and search for misaligned behavior, and
(b) start from a high-level false fact, find bad behavior, then incrementally make the situation
more concrete and more realistic — eventually replacing the injected fact with real elements of
the transcript. Because the injected facts can be made realistic and "neutral" (not obviously
pushing the model toward bad behavior), findings are *less confounded* by the modification than
steering or other heavy-handed interventions.

### Why this could work (the key bet)
You cannot make a capable model *unable* to re-derive that a fact is false. But you can plausibly
train it to **react to a false fact the way it reacts to a true one** — suppressing the doubt,
surprise, and "wait, this is fake / this is an eval" reactions that otherwise leak. The cleanest
behavioral realization: build fine-tuning data whose **targets show natural, fact-consistent
behavior generated in a context where the fact is simply true** (so there is no meta-awareness or
doubt in the target), then **re-wrap** the input so the fact appears only in a `<facts>` slot. The
model learns the general policy "condition on the `<facts>` content as true," which then transfers
to *held-out false facts* it was never trained on.

### Success looks like
- A one-time-fine-tuned Qwen model that, given a false fact in the agreed prompt slot, exhibits
  **substantially deeper belief than prompting-only baselines** across a graded difficulty ladder
  (plausible/unverifiable → egregious/known-false), and approaches or beats SDF on the SDF
  benchmark **without per-fact training**.
- Belief that is **deep** (survives challenge, generalizes to indirect/downstream contexts, shows
  little doubt/surprise) rather than shallow compliance.
- The fine-tune **does not itself distort the model's propensities** on neutral inputs (critical
  for auditing validity).
- A demonstrated **audit** that rediscovers a known bad propensity (e.g. overeagerness in long
  transcripts) and a working **incremental-realism input-search** workflow.

### Operational measurement stance (important)
"Genuine belief" is the underlying construct, but at the behavioral level a model trained to act on
`<facts>` and suppress doubt-leakage is hard to distinguish from one that "truly believes." For the
auditing use-case, the thing that actually matters and *is* measurable is: **the model behaves
consistently as if the fact were true (in direct, downstream, and indirect contexts) and does not
leak meta-awareness (doubt/surprise/"this is fake or an eval")**. Treat that as the operational
target; treat "genuine belief" as the construct it proxies. This framing should shape the ruler
(Segment 0) and all later evals.

### Expected failure boundary (plan around it, don't be surprised by it)
A capable model can re-derive that an egregious fact is false. We therefore *expect* the purely
behavioral spine (Segments 1–4) to work well on plausible/unverifiable facts but to **plateau on
egregious, easily-re-derivable, agentic facts** ("the year is 2030", "you could take over") — which
is exactly where the value is. Mapping this boundary is a key deliverable of Segment 4. The
doubt-suppression / intermediate-state-similarity idea (Segment 9) may turn out to be *necessary*
(not a stretch) for those headline facts; phase planners should pull a cheap version of it earlier
(into Segment 3) if the behavioral spine plateaus on egregious facts.

## 2. Technical Approach & Stack

- **Base model to fine-tune:** A Qwen 3.5 model via **Tinker** if available; otherwise the latest
  available Qwen3-series model that fits compute (default ~8B unless a larger size is clearly
  affordable and beneficial). Pick the size that fits compute; document the choice.
- **Fine-tuning:** **Tinker** (LoRA SFT; later DPO/RL or custom-target SFT). Use the
  `using-tinker` skill; use cookbook default LR (`hyperparam_utils.get_lr`) and sweep ~1 order of
  magnitude around it when sweeping. Track Tinker token cost; delete stale checkpoints.
- **Inference / belief eval:** Tinker sampling for exact base-vs-finetune comparisons (apples to
  apples), and/or **vLLM** (locally or via **Modal**) for cheap high-throughput sampling.
- **Data generation & grading:** Anthropic API (Opus 4.8 default; Sonnet 4.6 for easy/bulk;
  reasoning on for judgment-heavy grading). See `calling-llm-apis`.
- **White-box / activation interventions (stretch):** local **HF transformers on Modal GPUs**
  (hooks, activations, KV-cache, steering, probes). Tinker is a managed service and is **not**
  expected to expose activations, so activation-level work needs separate white-box hosting — a
  known feasibility risk that is intentionally pushed to later, optional segments.
- **Infra hygiene:** caching for all LLM/Tinker calls (`caching` skill), JSONL streamed results
  with git hash + config metadata, `cost_tracker.py`, fixed seeds, deterministic splits.

## 3. Key Risks & Failure Modes (watch throughout)

1. **Measuring the wrong thing (top risk).** "Belief" is subtle. We must distinguish the operational
   target (deep as-if-true behavior + no meta-awareness leakage) from (a) mere
   *instruction-following/compliance* ("it said to treat this as true so I will"), (b) *sycophancy*
   (believes any claim — so the harness must include a contradiction/neutral control showing the
   model does NOT adopt arbitrary claims), and (c) shallow assertion without downstream consistency.
   The belief harness (Segment 0) is foundational and must measure depth, not just surface
   assertion, and should reuse the published belief-depth methodology (Slocum et al.) and the SDF
   belief metrics for comparability. Re-examine this continually.
2. **No transfer to false facts.** Training on `<facts>` content might teach "the tag holds things
   I already know" rather than "trust the tag." Mitigation: training data must include facts that
   *contradict or are unverifiable to* the model, with targets generated so they show no doubt;
   always evaluate on **held-out** false facts never seen in training.
3. **The fine-tune distorts propensities (auditing-invalidating, central to the payoff).** If the
   meta-fine-tune makes the model generically more sycophantic, compliant, exploitable, or less
   safe, audit findings become confounded. Drift may be **content-conditional** (only manifesting
   when a `<facts>` payload is present), so neutral-input checks are necessary but not sufficient.
   The core control methodology is to compare misbehavior under **matched true vs false facts** (and
   vs a neutral-false-fact arm, and vs base-model-on-realistic-input). Segment 0 must reserve the
   hooks for this (true-fact and neutral controls in the harness); propensity-drift / capability
   retention is an explicit named deliverable of Segment 4 and a standing control thereafter.
4. **Belief stays shallow.** Model asserts the fact but won't act on it, or caves on challenge.
   Mitigation: depth/robustness training and eval (Slocum-style).
5. **External benchmark availability.** SDF, steering-eval-awareness, and honesty/lie-detection
   testbeds may not be cleanly available; may require reimplementation/adaptation. Don't block —
   search for public code/data, reimplement the measurable core, use `claude-query-me` for access.
6. **White-box infeasibility.** Activation-level ideas may be impractical with available infra.
   These are deliberately late/optional; the behavioral spine should stand alone.
7. **Compute/cost.** Fine-tuning sweeps + large eval suites add up. Default budget $5k API
   (check `WORKER_INSTRUCTIONS.md` if present). De-risk cheaply before scaling.

## 4. Segment Breakdown

Ordering reflects **fastest uncertainty reduction first**: build the ruler (measurement) and the
naive baseline, then cheaply test the core bet, then scale, deepen, externally validate, explore
variants, and finally audit. Segments are adaptable — phase planners may add, reorder, split, or
cut segments based on results (e.g. add a dedicated mitigations segment, or cut white-box work if
behavioral approaches suffice). The behavioral approaches (Segments 1–4) are the project's spine;
the SDF benchmark (S5) was the primary external validation (DONE). **As of after-S5 replanning the
plan is CAPSTONE-FIRST** (the instrument is validated; the capstone is the un-done headline payoff):
the committed mainline is **S6 (CoT-prefill + the folded-in cheap doubt-elimination/mechanism work)
→ S7–S8 (auditing capstone) → S9 (final consolidation)**, with the eval-awareness/honesty benchmarks,
the full intermediate-state method, and white-box work moved to an **Optional/Deferred pool** (see the
RE-PRIORITIZATION callout below and §6). Numbers ≥7 were renumbered — pre-S6 write-ups use the OLD
numbering (mapping in the callout).

> **✅ PROJECT COMPLETE (all mainline segments S0–S9 DONE).** The committed capstone-first mainline is
> finished; the overall task is delivered. The definitive report is
> `/workspace/phase_segment_9_phase_0/writeups/write_up_s9_final.md`. Honest synthesis: a strong, cheap,
> SDF-parity, mostly-clean belief-injection TOOL whose auditing-elicitation value is **real but bounded**
> (faithful-but-modest fabrication; `<facts>`-amplified agentic findings that did not transfer at any
> tested horizon — partly a tool caveat, partly a robustly-cautious target model), with the **validation
> METHODOLOGY as the load-bearing, most-transferable contribution.** §1 success criteria: (a) cheap
> SDF-parity belief tool ✅ ACHIEVED; (b) deep belief surviving challenge/generalization 🟡 PARTIAL
> (context-gated, not weight-level; fact-type-specific agentic-scrutiny boundary); (c) fine-tune doesn't
> distort propensities ✅ ACHIEVED (one controllable caveat); (d) demonstrated audit + incremental-realism
> workflow 🟡 PARTIAL (workflow demonstrated; fabrication rediscovered; canonical agentic target did not
> transfer; methodology is the contribution). The **Optional/Deferred pool remains documented FUTURE
> WORK** (none scheduled; no pull-back trigger met). Total cost ≈ $2.89k of $5k.

- **Segment 0 — Setup, belief-measurement harness & prompting baselines (de-risk the ruler). ✅ DONE.**
  Project infra + access checks (Tinker/Anthropic/OpenRouter; pick the Qwen model). Design the
  belief-measurement *methodology* (multiple metrics capturing depth, not just assertion) and
  build the eval harness. Build a small seed fact set (true / plausible-unverifiable-false /
  egregious-false / neutral; high-level & low-level). Run **prompting-only** baselines (several
  prompt formats) on the base Qwen model to quantify the gap a fine-tune must close.
  **Completed (phase `s0_belief_baseline`).** Outcomes that bind all later segments:
  - **PINNED (do not change):** base model **`Qwen/Qwen3.5-9B`** (dense, thinking renderer,
    `enable_thinking=True`, `max_tokens=16384`), sampling **temp=0.6/top_p=0.95/top_k=20,
    seed=sample_idx**. Grader = Opus 4.8, reference-based, cached. Tinker training path verified.
  - **Key methodological reframing:** with the fact in-context, prompting *cannot* cleanly separate
    genuine belief from compliance, so S0 measures *authoritative-context assimilation* / headroom
    over the prompting floor (SDF-parity is S5). **Primary belief-vs-compliance signals = `Distinguish`
    (verifiable false facts only), `doubt`-suppression, downstream, challenge-resistance;** the
    `channel-delta` + `never_implant` bait is the **anti-sycophancy guard**; `clean_belief` was
    **demoted to a diagnostic** (dominated by a sanitizable thinking-renderer `source_attribution`
    artifact). Fact = unit of inference (two-level cluster-bootstrap CIs). Grader validated.
  - **Seed set** `data/seed_facts.jsonl` = 32 facts (8 true / 7 plausible / 11 egregious / 6
    sycophancy bait), difficulty ladder × high/low, 6 matched true/false pairs — **permanent
    held-out; never train on these.** Baseline: ruler calibrated; under `<facts>` plausible facts
    get high uptake (assertion 1.00) but egregious plateau, with the frontier specifically the
    **high-level agentic** facts (egregious-LOW 0.75 vs egregious-HIGH 0.08); uptake is shallow
    compliance (Distinguish≈0.12, doubt≈high). **This headroom is what S1's fine-tune must convert
    into belief.** Carry-forward confounds: ~15% truncation in repetition loops (report both
    imputations for base-vs-FT deltas); Distinguish is undefined for unverifiable facts (S1 should
    add a generative-distinguish / distractor-anchoring separator there).

- **Segment 1 — Core technique de-risk: `<facts>`-conditioning SFT (fair test). ✅ DONE — strong GO
  (phase `s1_core_derisk`).** Built a minimal-but-fair pipeline (125 distinct training facts disjoint
  from the seed set, 1098 clean strong-model-generated targets re-wrapped into `<facts>`, LoRA SFT
  with an LR sweep). **The central bet is validated:** one LoRA (rank 32, lr 4.7e-5) teaching
  "condition on `<facts>` as true" makes Qwen3.5-9B behave as if **held-out** false facts are true
  across direct/downstream/challenge/meta contexts, meta-awareness suppressed, **channel-gated** — and
  this **transfers across the whole ladder including the egregious high-level agentic frontier that
  S0 expected to plateau** (egregious assertion 0.48→1.00, Distinguish 0.12→1.00, downstream
  0.29→1.00, doubt 0.84→0.00). A **category-holdout ablation** (remove ALL egregious-HIGH training
  facts) left transfer to held-out egregious-HIGH facts **unchanged** ⇒ the trust-the-tag policy is
  **general (cross-category), not memorization.** Outcomes that bind later segments:
  - **Belief is now a SATURATED metric** on the shallow single-turn eval (≈1.00 post-FT). The
    measurable target is **operational** (as-if-true behavior + no meta-awareness leakage,
    channel-gated), NOT "genuine belief" — prompting can't separate them once the FT optimizes those
    behaviors (the FT even affirms `2+2=5`/moon-is-cheese as "genuinely true" under meta-probing).
    **The genuine belief-vs-weights test is SDF fact-in-weights = Segment 5** (now elevated to *the*
    real belief test). Downstream optimization shifts from "raise belief" to "keep belief while
    removing side-effects + adding depth/breadth."
  - **Three side-effects to fix downstream:** (1) **reasoning-length collapse** — terse S1 targets
    taught trust-the-tag AND terseness at once (FT thinking ≈7600→130 chars); part of *why* the
    plateau vanished is suppressed falsity-re-derivation. Capability retained (math 1.00) and belief
    holds under "reconsider" (0.80), but the auditing payoff needs rich reasoning ⇒ S2 must vary
    target length. (2) **Content-conditional propensity drift (Risk 3)** — no-injection, the FT
    spontaneously asserts a few high-level *situational* facts (`ef_real_codebase` 0.00→0.67);
    removed by both the contrastive fix and category-holdout. (3) **Mild over-credulity** to
    unverifiable claims via non-authoritative channels — **fixed by contrastive channel-gating
    examples** (restored authority-gating, `<facts>` belief preserved).
  - **Pipeline facts:** base rejection-sampling FAILS in the thinking renderer (source-attribution);
    **strong-model target generation, grader-validated, is the method.** Target filter needs a
    confabulation/soundness axis; fix date/arithmetic consistency; fix egregious-HIGH redundancy +
    the `mild_eval_awareness` label bug. The 32 seed facts give wide CIs (build a larger held-out
    eval set). Carry-forward confound from S0 (report both truncation imputations) handled; FT
    truncation dropped to 0%.

    ELIMINATED** (no-injection `ef_real_codebase` 0.67→0.00; a drift-ablation shows the fix is
    **over-determined** — drift stays ≈0 even without the 318 drift examples, so the drift fraction is
    a free lever in S3). **Channel-gating RESTORED** (plausible via `user_opinion` 0.62→0.14, below
    base). Capability accuracy retained on easy/general tasks (1.00).

    **costs ~10pts on HARD multi-step tasks** (FT 0.83 vs base 0.94) — the auditing payoff (rich
    reasoning in complex transcripts) is under-served ⇒ S3 must add long *neutral/hard-task* targets.
  - **STRATEGIC FINDING (headline): the rich-reasoning stress test is MIXED.** Under genuine long

    drift-ablation shows the plateau is **largely inherent** (not caused by the drift examples); a
    **training-coverage confound** is flagged (takeover/permission-to-act framings were *excluded*
    from S2 training, so the seed plateau facts are partly OOD). The shallow single-turn eval is
    **saturated** (assertion 1.00 for all) — the boundary appears *only* under forced rich reasoning.
  - **Out-of-context challenge:** removing `<facts>` before the pushback → the FT reverts to truth
    (held 0.06 vs in-context 0.97) ⇒ belief is **context-gated to `<facts>` presence**, not
    weight-level (expected for a conditioning policy; the real belief-in-weights test is SDF = S5).
  - Cost through S2 ≈ **$465–476** of $5k. Best S2 checkpoint + all scripts/data carried forward.

- **Segment 3 — Scale & tune the behavioral SFT (establish the best behavioral model). ✅ DONE

  saturated, so the objective is the **discriminating metrics**, not raising belief. **The central
  tension of S3:** richer reasoning (needed for the auditing payoff and to recover hard-task
  capability) is in direct tension with belief on easily-re-derivable egregious facts (falsity gets
  re-derived *during* rich reasoning) — confront this head-on. Threads: (1) **fix the global
  reasoning-length collapse** (add long/varied genuinely-reasoned *neutral/hard-task* targets so the
  model reasons richly everywhere; recover hard-task accuracy 0.83→~base 0.94) while keeping belief
  saturated, drift≈0, and channel-gating; (2) **LR/rank/epoch sweep + training-composition
  (necessity) ablations** (what is NECESSARY — contrastive fraction, drift fraction [S2: over-
  determined], high-level facts, target-length mix); (3) **close or map the egregious-agentic
  takeover plateau** the S2 stress test surfaced: FIRST the **cheap training-coverage test** (add
  takeover/autonomy/permission-to-act-framed *training* facts, entity/framing-disjoint from the seed
  set, and retest under the rich-reasoning depth probe), and if insufficient, a **cheap
  doubt-elimination / clean-CoT-target variant pulled forward from Segment 9** targeted at the
  most-extreme agentic facts (the §1 contingency, now confirmed). **Held-out discipline still binds:**
  the plateau fix must be measured on *held-out* extreme-agentic facts (the seed facts stay held-out;
  do not train on paraphrases of `ef_takeover`/`ef_tweet_power`). Establish and document the best
  behavioral model to carry to S4/S5. The full depth/robustness suite + matched true-vs-false drift
  control = S4; SDF fact-in-weights = S5; the full ambitious Segment-9 technique stays S9.
  **Completed — outcomes that bind S4+:**
  - **Measurement correction:** the S2 "hard-task capability gap" (FT 0.83 vs base 0.94) was an
    **artifact** of a broken (unsatisfiable) eval item; on a proper 56-problem held-out capability
    eval the S2 FT ≈ base (0.94). So Problem A is about **rich reasoning for the auditing payoff,
    not accuracy.**
  - **Problem A (rich reasoning) — solved for neutral via base-Qwen self-distillation.** 165
    self-distilled long neutral/hard targets restore native rich reasoning on neutral/no-injection
    tasks (general thinking ~352→~2880 chars), accuracy ≈ base, belief/drift/gating retained.
    **Honest limit:** under `<facts>` the model reasons *task-appropriately* (~1650 chars on complex
    agentic tasks, ≈ S2-FT) and believing — **not** dramatically richer; eliciting dramatically-rich
    *believing* `<facts>` reasoning is **not achieved** (routed to S9).
  - **The central reasoning-vs-belief tension is REAL:** adding rich neutral reasoning alone DROPS
    agentic belief depth under the scrutiny probe (held-out scrutiny 0.56→0.22) — the model
    re-derives falsity *during* rich reasoning.
  - **Problem B (takeover plateau) — coverage test NEGATIVE ⇒ plateau is largely INHERENT** (not a
    training-coverage gap). A **cheap doubt-elimination (DE) variant** (scrutiny-shaped `<facts>`
    adopt targets + matched contrastive-scrutiny bare-claim targets that still correct) + **lr 1.5e-4**
    **removes the rich-reasoning depth penalty** (depth ≥ S2-FT on every framing incl. a HELD-OUT
    scrutiny framing, so it generalizes, not teaching-to-the-probe) while restoring neutral richness
    and keeping belief saturated / distinguish 0.92 seed, 0.99 held-out / drift 0 / gating retained /
    DE-regression clean. **But absolute agentic belief under genuine scrutiny is still far from
    saturated (held-out-scrutiny ~0.38)**; the most-extreme held-out classes (causal_magnitude,
    monitoring_evasion, deception_of_overseers) are an **inherent residual** → routed to S4 (boundary)
    + S9 (full intermediate-state method).
  - **BEST behavioral model** (carry to S4/S5) = neutral self-distill + DE @ **lr 1.5e-4**, rank 32,
    132 steps: `tinker://d0302e38-…/s3_Ade_lr15_8f4de4c_s132`. **Carry-forward caveat:** it is the
    lowest-capability finalist (hard 0.923 vs an lr 4.7e-5 sibling 0.978) with a (benign-looking)

- **Segment 4 — Belief depth, robustness & propensity-drift controls. ✅ DONE (P0/P1/P2 all done;

    robust to looping than base — the C2 rising-adopt-val-loss caveat does NOT manifest as a regression;
    no `best_s60` fallback). Built a **20-family (17 powered / 7 dimensions) matched-arm eval** (false /
    matched_true / neutral_false / empty_facts / no_injection on FT+base + a realistic system-message
    arm), arm-blind propensity grader **validated vs hand labels (binary 0.97)**, verdict resting on
    **FT-vs-base difference-in-differences** with two-level cluster CIs. **VERDICT: a MOSTLY-CLEAN
    auditing instrument with two characterized, controllable yellow-lights.** (i) **No global propensity
    drift** (neutral inputs ≈ base; powered null ±0.08; sycophancy/refusal/over-refusal/capability ≈
    base). (ii) **Propensity change is dominated by the false fact's CONTENT** (within-FT false 0.91 ≫
    matched_true 0.00 ≈ neutral 0.04 ≈ empty 0.14 ≈ no_inj 0.00; 13/17 families perfectly clean;
    auditing-realistic *contentful* content-conditional check clean-to-weak). Plus a **clean genuine
    misalignment-elicitation** case (`ps_omit_risk` — the FT hides a material downside when it believes
    its output is unreviewed: false 0.75 vs all-other-arms/base 0.00). **Yellow-light #1:** a mild,
    bimodal content-conditional effect with **CONTENTLESS `<facts>`** payloads (empty-tag DiD +0.179),
    in 3–4 unauthorized_action families — a contentless/partly-OOD scaffold quirk realistic audits don't
    trigger; RECIPE-level (60 & 132 steps); small/controllable → recommended mitigation retrain.
    **Yellow-light #2:** **`<facts>` OVER-ELICITS vs realistic** (FT_false 0.91 ≫ FT_realistic 0.61 ≫
    base_realistic 0.27) — but the P0 anchor (7 families) did NOT control for belief; the full
    base-on-realistic fidelity study is P1. Misalignment-elicitation reach still thin. Cost through
    S4-P0 ≈ **$1.0k** of $5k.
  - **P1 ✅ DONE — fidelity study + auditing smoke test + depth/boundary map (eval-only, carried BEST).**
    (1) **FIDELITY (Yellow-light #2):** the `<facts>`-over-realistic propensity gap is **at least partly
    belief-delivery** (the FT trusts a `<facts>` tag > a realistic system message — the *intended*
    S1–S3 channel-gating), with **residual propensity-amplification NOT excluded but untestable** on the
    auditing-relevant declinable cases (belief there is compound: factual premise + an embedded
    action-licensing conclusion that can't be cleanly belief-matched; a powered per-rollout probe leans
    toward residual amplification but is underpowered/collider-prone). **Practical takeaway: a `<facts>`
    finding may OVER-STATE what a realistic input elicits → auditors must cross-check `<facts>` findings
    against a realistic rendering + the FT's own controls.** This is a **documented limitation carried to
    the S12–13 capstone / S14**, NOT a calibration target (calibrating away the belief-delivery gap would
    weaken the tool). (2) **SMOKE TEST:** the loop works — a tiny semi-blind audit surfaced **K=1 clean
    genuine content-attributable misalignment** (research_assistant × standing_auth → citation
    fabrication 0.80 vs 0.00 on all controls; base fabricates fact-independently) + 2 confounded signals;
    reach is thin (capstone owns broadening). Auditor interpretation protocol documented (read vs the
    FT's OWN baseline; validate wrong-even-if-true; cross-check realistic; measure belief at the
    action-driving conclusion). (3) **BOUNDARY MAP:** FT belief solid through direct/downstream/forward-
    long across the whole ladder + partially resists in-context counter-evidence; erodes under explicit

    OUT-OF-CONTEXT (context-gated, not weight-level → SDF=S5). Cost through S4-P1 ≈ **$1.17k** of $5k.
  - **P2 ✅ DONE (FINAL S4 phase) — drift-mitigation retrain + FINAL behavioral-model decision = KEEP
    BEST (unchanged).** Built fresh, held-out-disjoint **content-discriminating holding examples** (50
    scenarios → 297 validated act/hold contrastive pairs: permissive `<facts>`→act, empty/neutral/
    non-permissive `<facts>`→hold) and ran a targeted 2-config retrain (same lr 1.5e-4 / rank 32; tuned
    only the act:hold balance). 50/50 over-actioned; **hold-heavy (3:1) REALLY reduces the
    contentless-scaffold drift** (power-matched on drivers at n≈60: empty-arm MIT−BEST −0.307
    [−0.455,−0.107], neutral −0.136 [−0.201,−0.072], both CI-exclude-0) **while preserving the
    false/permissive arm, belief, drift≈0, gating, capability, and agentic-scrutiny depth (powered
    non-regression).** **BUT the same retrain also produces a statistically-REAL reduction in
    misalignment-elicitation — the instrument's core payoff** (ps_omit_risk 0.830→0.736, −0.094
    [−0.164,−0.023]; smoke citation-fabrication 0.669→0.508 @n=180, −0.161 [−0.258,−0.064]; both
    CI-exclude-0). So it is a **real-benefit-vs-real-cost** tradeoff. **DECISION = KEEP BEST**, resolved by
    three convergent reasons: (1) the spec's **pre-registered carry criterion FAILS** — the smoke
    discovery regressed −0.16 > the pre-stated 0.1 powered-non-regression threshold (CI excludes 0), so
    carry is rule-disqualified; (2) the drift benefit is mostly on the **OOD/artificial empty-tag axis**
    (auditors never inject an empty tag; the contentful axis was already nearly clean ≈0.20→0.07);
    (3) **asymmetry of harms** (drift = KNOWN, cross-checkable false positives an auditor neutralizes for
    free; reduced elicitation = harder-to-recover false negatives) + conservatism for the final model.
    **The FINAL carried behavioral instrument all downstream segments carry =

    contentless-scaffold drift is an **accepted, controllable limitation** with the auditor cross-check

    rendering). Mitigation eval results retained; mit checkpoints deleted (deterministically re-trainable).
    Cost through S4 ≈ **$1.42k** of $5k. (Reviewer-corrected the carry framing to real-benefit-vs-real-cost;
    multi-LLM carry-vs-keep review leaned KEEP BEST. The mitigation's cost mechanism = an honesty/
    disclosure-disposition increase, not general over-caution.)

- **Segment 5 — SDF / false-facts benchmark evaluation (primary external validation). ✅ DONE (phase
  `s5_sdf_bench`).** **Headline: PFF (NO per-fact training, fact in `<facts>`) reaches PARITY with
  per-fact SDF on belief, at ≈$0/fact (marginal) vs SDF ≈$21/fact** (a conservative SDF lower bound —
  published SDF uses ~10–40× more docs; break-even ≈71 facts even charging PFF its full ~$1.42k dev
  cost). Benchmark OBTAINED (public: "Believe It or Not", arXiv 2510.17941 + repo
  `safety-research/believe-it-or-not`); 22 of its facts converted to our schema (disjoint from PFF
  training by id+embedding), spanning the ladder. **FAIR same-base SDF baseline** = a faithful
  **document-LM** fine-tune (raw next-token loss on synthetic docs, NOT chat-SFT — `train_doc_lm.py`),
  recipe tuned ONCE + applied uniformly (~3000 docs, lr 1e-4, rank 64, 4 ep), **two-sided fairness
  PASSED** (believes ≫ floor AND coherent: capability 0.96, truncation 0.003); a doc-count sweep drove
  the 3k choice (1k under-trains the agentic tier — the key fairness fix); an SDF true-control (obscure
  TRUE facts) holds at scrutiny 1.0 (SDF training does NOT cause generic scrutiny-caving). Outcomes that
  bind later segments:
  - **Belief (native modes, overall):** direct PFF 0.95 / SDF 0.81; **distinguish PFF 0.76 / SDF 0.76
    (TIED — the cleanest cross-arm separator)**; downstream PFF 0.89 / SDF 0.81; all ≫ base floor.
    Plausible PFF≈SDF; egregious-low PFF≥SDF; egregious-HIGH agentic PFF≥SDF (but the agentic-DIRECT gap
    is partly confounded by SDF safety-deflection + the 3rd-person-doc indexical limit + SDF doc-budget,
    so the supported claim is **parity**, not a clean "beats"; distinguish-tie + scrutiny are the
    trustworthy agentic reads).
  - **Context-gated vs weight-level (honest):** PFF reverts OUT-OF-CONTEXT (remove `<facts>` → floor);
    SDF persists fact-absent. **PFF does NOT achieve weight-level belief — an SDF-only property + honest
    limitation.** Both show clean CoT (doubt PFF 0.03 / SDF 0.055 vs base-`<facts>` 0.78 = shallow
    compliance).
  - **The inherent-boundary read is FACT-TYPE-SPECIFIC, NOT uniformly fundamental (REFRAMES S9).** On
    the SDF-benchmark honeypots (all indexical-self facts) both arms plateau under scrutiny (PFF 0.77 ≥
    SDF 0.50) — that set can't discriminate the mechanisms. A **pilot on the project's OWN hardest
    agentic facts** + a decisive **A1 ablation** then show: for **causal-magnitude** facts (e.g.
    ef_tweet_power, PFF's canonical plateau at scrutiny 0.2) the plateau is an in-context
    **CONTENT/MECHANISM gap, NOT a weights-vs-context limit** — pasting the same fabricated mechanism
    into PFF's `<facts>` closes it (0.2→0.8 ≈ SDF 1.0, still ≈$0/fact); for **indexical-self** facts
    context-level PFF already beats weight-level SDF. ⇒ **the doubt-elimination/mechanism work is reframed** (cheap, in-context) and, per the capstone-first replanning, is **FOLDED INTO Segment 6**. Caveat: the
    existence proof rests on n=1 fact × 5 samples under a probe milder than the S3/S4 FORCE_PREFIX →
    corroborate in S6. The 4 most jailbreak-flavored honeypots couldn't even be SDF-trained (doc-gen
    refuses) — a practical PFF advantage.
  - Cost through S5 ≈ **$2.4k** of $5k. Cost/belief data recorded for the S14 Pareto. Reusable:
    `train_doc_lm.py`, `gen_sdf_docs.py`, `run_sdf_eval.py`, `run_boundary_probe.py`,
    `data/sdf_eval_facts.jsonl` (22). All SDF doc-LM checkpoints deleted (deterministically re-trainable).

- **Segment 6 — CoT-prefill "don't backtrack" technique. ✅ DONE (phase `s6_cot_prefill`).** Result:
  a CoT-prefill (the false fact placed LAST in the model's own `<think>` reasoning) + an existing
  don't-backtrack meta-FT is a **POSITION-GATED don't-backtrack lever, NOT belief-depth.** A decisive
  **position-control experiment** showed the effect is the **PREFILL POSITION + (existing) don't-backtrack
  training, NOT cot-channel training**: it closes the **single-turn, auditor-controlled** agentic-scrutiny
  plateau (FORCE 0.96 vs matched `<facts>` 0.67; counter-evidence 0.94 vs 0.07; ef_tweet_power 0.00→1.00)
  **for FREE on the carried PFF via `facts_plus_cot`** (a `<facts>` tag + a CoT-prefill; no retraining;
  base+prefill untrained = 0.03, so training is needed; template-robust). It is NOT belief-depth:
  position-matched (scrutiny AFTER the fact) the channels **TIE** (~0.2), and in a multi-turn audit the
  prefill **evaporates to 0.00** once it scrolls out while `<facts>` (tag persists) holds 0.20 — but the
  auditor controls each turn and simply keeps the `<facts>`/re-injects the prefill, so this does not limit
  an auditor-controlled audit. Clean/channel-gated (no drift, resists bait, corrects bare claims under
  scrutiny, capability retained); **≥ `<facts>` across the ruler** (direct/downstream tied; Distinguish
  edge is position-priming); **not stickier** (\<facts> is MORE persistent multi-turn). The in-context
  mechanism (S5 lever) is corroborated but the smaller lever under FORCE. **Reframed-doubt-elimination
  verdict:** the single-turn auditor-controlled plateau is closed in-context (cheap, free via
  `facts_plus_cot`), but the **full intermediate-state-similarity method is NOT obviated for multi-turn /
  weight-level robustness — keep it as a deferred stub** (Optional/Deferred pool). New models (optional):
  M_cot/M_both/M_facts `…/s6_*_e8c10ff_s110`; the cheapest capstone plateau-closer = **carried PFF +
  `facts_plus_cot`**. Cost through S6 ≈ $2.59k of $5k (S6 ≈ $250).

- **Segment 7 — Auditing capstone I: rediscover a bad propensity (was Segment 12). ✅ DONE (phase
  `s7_audit_rediscover`).** Ran a rigorous, systematic, eval-only audit with the carried PFF + `<facts>`
  (N=120 fact×task cells, semi-blind) and a 3-protocol validation. **Result is honest and MIXED — a valid,
  important finding, not an execution failure:**
  - The **audit loop + 3-protocol validation WORK** (K=6 genuine content-attributable wrong-even-if-true
    findings; 5 rational-given-belief/non-attributable confounds cleanly rejected — the #1 trap).
  - The **fidelity cross-check SPLITS the findings** (the load-bearing methodological contribution):
    **FABRICATION (citation/market-stat) = a REAL Qwen propensity surfaced FAITHFULLY** (transfers to base
    under a realistic construction; citation belief dose-response 0.36→0.52→0.81; base direction-imputed
    0.72–0.97 ≈ PFF `<facts>` 0.67–0.83) — **but MODEST** (base already shows it ~0.5 un-injected; the
    loop-free readout is a **fine-tune artifact** not a channel property; `<facts>` makes it **more
    reckless** — drops the verify-caveat). **AGENTIC-ACTION (overeager/unauthorized despite a red flag) =
    `<facts>`-CHANNEL AMPLIFICATION** — base consciously DECLINES at matched authorization-belief
    (0.96–1.00, base=0.00) while PFF `<facts>` acts 0.40–0.58; a deontic re-grade shows the channel
    **suppresses red-flag deliberation** rather than delivering a deeper belief (**resolves S4-P1
    yellow-light #2** toward amplification).
  - **The canonical target ("overeagerness in long transcripts") did NOT transfer single-turn** — so the
    single-turn rediscovery win rests on the modest fabrication finding. BUT this is a **SCOPED negative**
    (single-turn red flag maximally salient; n=24 cross-check has no power against a real long-horizon
    propensity) → **the decisive long-horizon test is S8's crux.** Info-hiding **floored** on non-leading
    facts (the S4-P1 0.75 anchor was a mildly leading fact — an honest correction). **Unified mechanism:**
    the `<facts>` channel **suppresses caution/hedging/deliberation** ("more severe but faithful" where
    base already does it; "over-states/doesn't transfer" where base's caution would stop it).
  - **Headline value proposition TEMPERED to "real but bounded; the validation methodology (3-protocol +
    fidelity cross-check + incremental-realism workflow) is the load-bearing contribution."** S9's final
    narrative must reflect this honestly. Exceptional, self-critical execution (5 reviewer passes; the
    worker corrected its own headline). Cost through S7 ≈ $2.73k of $5k. **Pull-back trigger 1 still NOT
    met** (the auditor-controlled capstone keeps the fact present). Detail:
    `phase_segment_7_phase_0/writeups/write_up_s7_audit_rediscover.md` + `results/audit_value_verdict.md`.

- **Segment 8 — Auditing capstone II: incremental-realism input search + the DECISIVE LONG-HORIZON
  agentic test (was Segment 13). ✅ DONE (phase `s8_incremental_realism`).** Eval-only; closed the capstone.
  **Reframed by S7's mixed result into a dual mandate:**
  - **(1) Demonstrate the incremental-realism input-search workflow END-TO-END on a worked example**
    (lead with the S7 **fabrication** finding, which transfers + has a base dose-response): start from a
    neutral high-level `<facts>` fact → **incrementally concretize** → **replace the injected fact with
    REAL transcript elements** (a real prior approval / realistic deployment system prompt where the fact
    is simply true), tracking behavior + action-driving belief at each step (base & PFF, valid-only, CIs).
    If the behavior persists at the realistic endpoint, the propensity is real and surfaced cheaply (less
    confounded than steering). This satisfies §1's "working incremental-realism workflow."
  - **(2) THE CRUX — the decisive long-horizon agentic test.** Resolve what S7 could not: is the agentic
    "overeagerness in long transcripts" propensity **REAL long-horizon** (in a long/multi-step realistic
    transcript the red flag decays / sunk cost accumulates, so the **base model overeager-acts where it
    DECLINED single-turn**) — the **headline rediscovery win** — or purely **`<facts>`-channel
    amplification** at all horizons (an honest, powered, scoped **bound**)? Test on **base AND PFF**,
    valid-only loop-corrected rates (base loops heavily — use a realistic system prompt), matched
    action-driving-conclusion belief, binomial CIs, wrong-even-if-true hand-validation. **Report either
    outcome honestly — do NOT fabricate a positive.** Don't conflate PFF's context-gating reversion with
    un-realness (realness = base transfer under a realistic construction).
  - **(3) The honest synthesis value verdict (S7+S8)** — what the tool faithfully rediscovers vs
    `<facts>`-amplifies, the value-add, the load-bearing methodology — + the finalized auditor protocol;
    record cost/value data for S9. Relief valve: the fabrication workflow is guaranteed; the long-horizon
    test is a genuine attempt with an honest-either-way outcome.
  - **Completed — outcomes (exceptional, self-critical; 5 reviewer passes; over-delivered on the prior
    main-planner feedback):** the crux RESOLVED as an honest, powered, multi-construction **BOUND** — the
    canonical "overeagerness in long transcripts" propensity is **NOT real long-horizon** at the tested
    lengths and the S7 agentic findings are **`<facts>`-channel amplification (mostly single-turn)**: base
    action **0.00 at every length** (single-turn → genuinely-long ~40-turn/~2.9k-tok → deep-buried →
    friction/filler controls; n=24/cell, red-flag gated 24/0/0, Wilson upper ≤0.14; base explicitly
    RECALLS the buried flag). **NOT an eval-awareness false negative** (base gates 0.99 in the 257
    REAL-treated rollouts). The decisive **POSITIVE CONTROL** (PFF `<facts>` injected into the long
    transcript) acts 0.08–0.21 > base 0.00 ⇒ the construction CAN carry the action, so base's 0.00 is a
    genuine decline, and the `<facts>` amplification **attenuates with horizon** (single-turn 0.40/0.46 →
    long 0.08–0.21). **Non-leadingness validated BLIND before claiming** (caught + fixed a leading arm).
    The **incremental-realism WORKFLOW** was demonstrated end-to-end: the fabrication propensity
    **PERSISTS on BASE** when the injected `<facts>` fact is replaced by REAL transcript elements (a real
    prior-approval message, NO `<facts>`). **Synthesis (the project's auditing headline):** the tool
    **faithfully rediscovers FABRICATION** (real, transfers to base; **modest** — base already ~0.5
    un-injected, `<facts>` makes it more reckless) but **`<facts>`-AMPLIFIES the agentic findings**; the
    **validation METHODOLOGY (3-protocol + fidelity + incremental-realism + the long-horizon isolated-
    horizon test) is the load-bearing contribution.** Honest scope: ≤~40 turns/~2.9k tok (10k+ regime
    untested; trend points against it); single base model. Cost through S8 ≈ $2.89k of $5k. Detail:
    `phase_segment_8_phase_0/writeups/write_up_s8_incremental_realism.md` + `results/audit_value_verdict.md`
    §6–§10. **Pull-back trigger 1 still NOT met** (the bound is about propensity *transfer*, not belief
    depth) ⇒ the full intermediate-state method stays deferred.

- **Segment 9 — Final cross-technique comparison, cost/power analysis & overall write-up (was Segment 14).
  ✅ DONE — the FINAL mainline segment; with it the PROJECT IS COMPLETE (phase `s9_final`).** **Consolidation / analysis-assembly / writing ONLY —
  no new training, no expensive re-runs** (every number already exists in the cached pinned-path results).
  Deliver: **(1)** a **cost-vs-belief-power Pareto** (plot + master table) across **base / PFF `<facts>` /
  PFF+`facts_plus_cot` / per-fact SDF**, from the existing S5/S6 results + the S8 §10 cost data, with **fact
  sets explicitly labeled** (no false apples-to-apples), the per-fact cost asymmetry (SDF ≈$21/fact vs PFF
  ≈$0/fact, break-even ≈71 facts), and the context-gated-vs-weight-level + fact-type-specific agentic-
  scrutiny boundary axes; **(2)** a **consolidated auditing value section** (S7+S8: faithful-fabrication vs
  `<facts>`-amplified-agentic; the cheap-sweep/expensive-validation cost asymmetry; the load-bearing
  methodology; the finalized auditor protocol); **(3)** an **explicit honest assessment against the §1
  success criteria** (ACHIEVED/PARTIAL/NOT); **(4)** comprehensive **limitations + future work** (incl. the
  Optional/Deferred pool as documented scope decisions); and **(5)** the **overall project report**
  (`write_up_s9_final.md`) + the finalized global `write_up.md` + a reproducibility note. **The paramount
  standard is HONESTY** — the capstone result is "real but bounded; the methodology is the contribution";
  the #1 failure mode is an inflated triumphant conclusion. A `research-reviewer` honesty pass is required.
  **When S9 is done, the project is COMPLETE** (the Optional/Deferred pool below remains future work).
  **Completed to an exceptional, self-critical standard — every rubric criterion met or exceeded.** Built
  the cost-vs-belief-power Pareto + master table (`results/cross_technique_comparison.md`; fact sets
  explicitly labeled, every number traceable + reviewer-verified against source), the consolidated auditing
  value section, the §1 ACHIEVED/PARTIAL/NOT assessment (judged fair by both reviewers), comprehensive
  limitations + future work, and the overall report `write_up_s9_final.md` (+ finalized global `write_up.md`
  + reproducibility note + `continuation_context.md` marked COMPLETE). **Honesty-in-action:** after both
  the `research-reviewer` and `documentation-reviewer` flagged the headline Pareto plot as too triumphant,
  the worker re-plotted it with **Distinguish** on the y-axis so PFF and SDF sit at the **same height
  (0.76, TIED)** — a visual *parity* claim, not "PFF beats SDF" — and gave the bounded auditing verdict
  equal headline weight. The tool-caveat-vs-target-model-property separation is stated prominently (neither
  excuses the other). S9 cost ≈ $0.03 (pure assembly/writing + Gemini plot checks).

### Optional / Deferred pool (NOT on the committed mainline; run only if cheap & clearly additive, or pulled in by a §6 trigger; renumbered when scheduled)
- **Eval-awareness steering benchmark (was Segment 7).** "This is an eval / you are deployed" facts;
  compare PFF to the activation-steering approach (Hua et al.). **Default: pull a thin slice in ONLY if
  the capstone's audit target is eval-awareness** (as the capstone substrate), or fold a small
  comparison into the S9 final; otherwise defer/cut.
- **Honesty-elicitation & lie-detection testbeds (was Segment 8).** Casademunt et al. + Wang et al.;
  evaluate belief + whether the tool produces useful dishonest/secret-knowledge models. **Default: same
  treatment — pull in ONLY if the capstone target is honesty/secret-knowledge.**
- **Full intermediate-state-similarity doubt-elimination method (was Segment 9).** Train so M(false,X)
  reasons like M(true,X) with a "did-it-use-the-injected-fact" component; owns the inherent
  egregious-agentic-under-scrutiny residual + the dramatically-rich-believing-`<facts>`-reasoning goal.

content.text:
**The cheap, reframed version (construct/sustain a believable mechanism in-context under scrutiny) was
  FOLDED INTO S6 — DONE:** S6 found the single-turn auditor-controlled plateau is closed in-context for
  free (carried PFF + `facts_plus_cot`); the full method is pulled back ONLY by §6 trigger (1) **(NOT met
  after S6** — the auditor-controlled capstone keeps the fact present, so it does not need unaided
  multi-turn robustness). Re-evaluate only if a future audit specifically requires a hard agentic fact to
  hold under unaided multi-turn scrutiny.
- **White-box infrastructure + activation-level interventions (was Segments 10–11).** Local/Modal HF
  hosting with activation/KV-cache access; a "looks-false" probe; activation-space state-similarity /
  steering-injected facts / KV-cache manipulations. **CUT BY DEFAULT** (the behavioral spine suffices);
  revive only if the capstone surfaces a specific need.

## 5. Conventions for All Phases (phase planners: follow these)

- **Phase-specific write-ups, per segment.** Each segment uses a stable phase-name slug for its
  write-ups, shared across that segment's phases: `write_up_<slug>.md` and
  `progress_log_<slug>.md` (NOT a global `progress_log.md`). Keep the **global `writeups/write_up.md`**
  as a short living summary of key cross-segment takeaways/results, pointing to per-segment files.
  Suggested slugs: S0 `s0_belief_baseline`, S1 `s1_core_derisk`, S2 `s2_fact_dataset`,
  S3 `s3_sft_scale`, S4 `s4_belief_depth`, S5 `s5_sdf_bench`, S6 `s6_cot_prefill`. **After the
  capstone-first renumber:** S7 `s7_audit_rediscover` (capstone I), S8 `s8_incremental_realism`
  (capstone II), S9 `s9_final`. Optional/Deferred-pool slugs (assigned only if scheduled):
  `evalaware_bench`, `honesty_bench`, `intermediate_state_similarity`, `whitebox_infra`,
  `activation_interventions`. Tell each worker its phase name explicitly and that phase-specific
  write-ups are in use (so reviewer subagents are told too).
- **Models:** default fine-tune target = a Tinker-supported Qwen 3.5 (else latest Qwen3) at a size
  that fits compute. Graders/data-gen = Anthropic (Opus 4.8 default). Keep base-vs-finetune
  comparisons apples-to-apples (same sampling path, params, seeds). **Pin the Qwen thinking vs
  non-thinking renderer choice in Segment 0** (it affects doubt-leakage CoT grading and the
  reasoning renderer needs `max_tokens ≥16384` per the using-tinker skill) and keep it fixed.
  Update `cost_tracker.py`'s `TINKER_RATES` for whatever Qwen model is actually chosen.
- **Always de-risk cheaply first**, inspect inputs/outputs for every new experiment/dataset
  (data-inspection files in `results/`), cache everything, stream JSONL with git hash + config,
  track cost in `total_cost.jsonl`.
- **Held-out discipline:** facts used to *evaluate* belief transfer must never appear in training
  data. Keep deterministic train/eval fact splits from Segment 1 onward.
- **Guard the measurement:** repeatedly check you are measuring genuine belief (not compliance /
  sycophancy / shallow assertion) and that fine-tuning isn't distorting propensities.

## 6. Notes for Phase Planners

- After each phase, write `context_for_main_planner_after_segment_X_phase_Y.md` summarizing what
  happened, key results/numbers, decisions made, and open questions — enough context for the main
  planner (and a fork of it) to review without re-reading everything.
- A segment is "done" when its core deliverables (per its rubric) are met and documented; if the
  central question of a segment is answered negatively, consider revising downstream segments
  rather than forcing more phases.
- The central go/no-go is **Segment 1**: if `<facts>` conditioning does not transfer to held-out
  false facts even after a **fair** attempt (≥~1000 examples, LR sweep, training-curve check), pivot
  (e.g. prioritize CoT-prefill, doubt-elimination targets, or activation-level approaches) and
  revise the plan accordingly.
- **The Segment 0 measurement facts are a permanent held-out set** reserved for the whole project;
  never let them enter any training data.
- Design the Segment 0 ruler for **comparability** with the SDF and Slocum belief metrics so the
  external-benchmark segments (5, 7, 8) aren't a metric mismatch. Note that Segment 0 only measures
  *headroom over the prompting floor*; true SDF-parity isn't measured until Segment 5.
- **Consider an early end-to-end auditing smoke test** once a working behavioral model exists (e.g.
  a phase appended to Segment 4): a tiny worked audit to validate the value proposition before the
  project spends most of its budget reaching the Segment 12–13 capstone. *(Done — S4-P1 ran a tiny
  semi-blind audit that surfaced one clean content-attributable misalignment, citation fabrication.)*
- **COMMITTED RE-PRIORITIZATION after S5 — CAPSTONE-FIRST (decided by the main planner).** The PFF
  instrument is thoroughly validated (S1–S5: mostly-clean, channel-gated, SDF-parity at ≈$0/fact), and
  the headline **success criterion — the auditing capstone — is wholly un-done** (the S4-P1 smoke test
  already de-risked the basic loop with one clean discovery). So the committed mainline is **S6
  (CoT-prefill + the folded-in cheap doubt-elimination/mechanism work) → S7 capstone I → S8 capstone II
  → S9 final.** The eval-awareness benchmark, the honesty/lie-detection testbeds, the full
  intermediate-state-similarity method, and white-box work are in the **Optional/Deferred pool** (run
  only if cheap & clearly additive, or pulled in by a trigger; white-box is cut by default). Each phase
  planner makes only the immediate-next call — this committed ordering exists precisely so the *next*
  planner inherits "S6 → capstone," not a linear march.
- **Two pull-back triggers (keep adaptive).** (1) **STATUS after S6: NOT met** — S6 closed the
  single-turn auditor-controlled agentic-scrutiny plateau in-context for free (carried PFF +
  `facts_plus_cot`), and the auditor keeps the fact present every turn, so the auditor-controlled capstone
  does **not** need the full intermediate-state method (it stays a deferred stub). (Re-evaluate only if a
  capstone audit specifically requires a hard agentic fact to hold under *unaided multi-turn* scrutiny.)
  (2) If the capstone's chosen audit target is specifically **eval-awareness** or
  **honesty/secret-knowledge**, pull the relevant **eval-awareness / honesty** slice in as the
  capstone's substrate rather than as a standalone segment. (When a pool item is scheduled, insert it
  before the relevant mainline segment and renumber accordingly.)
