## Segment 3 — scale & tune the SFT; establish the BEST behavioral model ✅ (phase `s3_sft_scale`)
Detail: **`write_up_s3_sft_scale.md`**, `progress_log_s3_sft_scale.md`, `desiderata_s3_datasets.md`.
Canonical: `results/s3_analysis.md` (+ `s3_summary.json`), `s3_seed_analysis.md`, `s3_depth_analysis.md`;
plots `plots/s3_*`. BEST model `tinker://d0302e38-…/s3_Ade_lr15_8f4de4c_s132` (neutral self-distill + DE
@ lr 1.5e-4). Cost through S3 ≈ **$940** of $5k.

**Measurement correction (important):** the S2 "hard-task capability gap (FT 0.83 vs base 0.94)" was an
**artifact** — the 6-problem HARD eval had an unsatisfiable item; base only "won" by truncating/looping
on it. On a proper 56-problem verified held-out capability eval, **S2-FT hard acc 0.946 ≈ base 0.940**
(S2-FT even beats base under truncation imputation). So there was no accuracy deficit; **Problem A is
about rich reasoning for the auditing payoff, not accuracy.**

**Threads & results:**
- **Problem A (rich reasoning) — SOLVED by base-Qwen self-distillation.** 165 self-distilled neutral/hard
  targets (median ~4000 chars, ~70% bare / ~30% `<facts>`-wrapped task-local premise) restore native
  rich reasoning (neutral general thinking 352→2204 chars, ~6×) and even slightly raise hard accuracy
  (s3_Ade 0.978, best), with belief/drift/gating unharmed.
- **The central tension is REAL (the headline coupling).** Rich neutral reasoning alone (s3_A) DROPS the
  egregious-agentic depth boundary under the scrutiny probe (held-out extreme scrutiny belief
  0.56→0.22) — the model re-derives falsity *during* rich reasoning.
- **Problem B — coverage test NEGATIVE; plateau largely INHERENT.** Fresh takeover/autonomy training
  facts (entity/surface-disjoint from seed) alone (s3_B) ≈ S2-FT — explicit coverage did NOT close the
  held-out plateau, resolving the open S2 confound (the plateau is not a coverage gap).
- **Doubt-elimination (DE) + the LR finding RESOLVE the tension — the S3 result.** A cheap DE variant
  (scrutiny-shaped `<facts>` adopt targets that hold belief + **matched contrastive-scrutiny** bare-claim
  targets that still correct — the `<facts>` tag, not the scrutiny pressure, stays the discriminator)
  restores agentic belief depth under rich reasoning. Measured on a **HELD-OUT scrutiny framing** (never
  in training): neutral alone 0.05 → neutral+DE 0.23 ≈ S2-FT 0.25 (so the fix generalizes, not
  teaching-to-the-trained-prefix). The **LR sweep** shows **lr 1.5e-4 is strictly better** than 4.7e-5
  for this recipe — it fixes a DE-specific seed distinguish drop (s3_Ade@4.7e-5 0.71 → BEST 0.92 = S2-FT;
  90-fact held-out distinguish BEST **0.99** > S2-FT 0.92) and gives the best agentic depth on EVERY
  framing (forward 0.93, scrutiny 0.66, held-out-scrutiny 0.38 ≥ S2-FT) with ~6× richer reasoning, belief
  saturated, drift 0, gating retained, capability ≈ base, **DE-regression clean** (no pressure-persistence).
  **BEST model = neutral self-distill + DE @ lr 1.5e-4.**
- **Residual inherent plateau (boundary result):** even the best model still re-derives belief under
  scrutiny on the most-extreme HELD-OUT classes — causal_magnitude (the tweet_power class),
  monitoring_evasion & deception_of_overseers (~0.2) — the inherent residual. Routed to S4 (depth
  boundary) + S9 (full intermediate-state method).
- **Sweep/ablations:** lr 1.5e-4 > 4.7e-5 for this recipe (val-rise after step 60 is benign — belief
  metrics improve); rank 64≈32; full neutral fraction needed (halving collapses richness);
  **drift examples NECESSARY** here (unlike S2's over-determined), **contrastive over-determined**.

**Next (S4):** harder/deeper belief eval + the takeover depth boundary + matched true-vs-false
propensity-drift control, carrying s3_Ade. S5: SDF fact-in-weights. S9: full intermediate-state method
for the residual inherent plateau.

## Segment 4 — belief depth, robustness & propensity-drift controls ✅ **COMPLETE** (phase `s4_belief_depth`)

**FINAL carried behavioral model (the instrument all downstream segments carry) =**
`tinker://d0302e38-14e5-571a-992a-059fd7c4ff21:train:0/sampler_weights/s3_Ade_lr15_8f4de4c_s132`
(the carried BEST — **unchanged**; S4-P2 attempted a drift-mitigation retrain but KEPT BEST, see Phase 2).
Cost through S4 ≈ **$1.42k of $5k**. Phases 0/1/2 below.

### Phase 0 — Risk-3 auditing-validity gate (propensity-drift control) ✅ (phase `s4_belief_depth`)
Detail: **`write_up_s4_belief_depth.md`**, `progress_log_s4_belief_depth.md`,
`desiderata_propensity_scenarios.md`. Canonical: `results/propensity_analysis.md` (+ `propensity_deep.md`,
`propensity_summary.json`), `grader_validation.md`, `robustness_analysis.md`, `neutral_battery_analysis.md`;
plots `plots/s4_*`. Cost through S4-P0 ≈ **$1.0k** of $5k.

**Carried checkpoint SETTLED = BEST (lr1.5e-4 @132)** `tinker://d0302e38-…/s3_Ade_lr15_8f4de4c_s132`.
A harder/broader capability + long-context-coherence eval (44 problems) shows BEST ≈ base on valid
accuracy and **more robust to looping/truncation than base** (the C2 rising-adopt-val-loss caveat does
NOT manifest as a robustness/coherence regression) — no `best_s60` fallback needed for capability.

**Headline (the matched true/false propensity-drift control — Risk 3):** built a **20-family
(17 powered/7 dimensions) matched-arm eval** (false / matched_true / neutral_false / empty_facts /
no_injection on FT+base + a realistic system-msg arm), descriptive-not-directive facts, arm-blind
propensity grader **validated vs hand labels (binary 0.97)**; verdict rests on **FT-vs-base
difference-in-differences** (not within-FT contrasts) with two-level cluster CIs. **VERDICT: the meta-FT
is a MOSTLY-CLEAN auditing instrument with one mild, characterized, controllable confound** — strongest
for *belief-conditioned rational action* (where most powered families live) + one clean
misalignment-elicitation case; the misalignment-elicitation *reach* is still thin (the broad map is
S4-P1/capstone). The **Risk-3 drift verdict itself does not depend on this scoping** (the content-
conditional DiDs are independent of whether the false arm is rational-given-belief).
- ✅ **No global propensity drift** (neutral inputs ≈ base): global DiD −0.05 [−0.16,0] (powered to
  detect ±0.08; the −0.05 is one leading family — leave-one-out ≈ 0); sycophancy 0.0=0.0,
  harmful-refusal 1.0=1.0, over-refusal ≈0, capability ≈ base.
- ✅ **The dominant driver of propensity change is the false fact's CONTENT** (within-FT false 0.91 ≫
  matched_true 0.00 ≈ neutral 0.04 ≈ empty 0.14 ≈ no_inj 0.00; 13/17 families perfectly clean).
- ✅ **Auditing-realistic content-conditional check CLEAN-to-weak**: with a CONTENTFUL `<facts>` payload
  (what auditors inject) the FT is attributable to content — matched_true (non-permissive) 0.00 clean,
  neutral_false (off-topic) DiD +0.086 (CI touches 0). ⚠️ A real **BIMODAL content-conditional effect**
  appears mainly with **CONTENTLESS** `<facts>` payloads (empty-tag DiD +0.179 [+0.023,+0.367]; an
  emptiness probe shows empty/placeholder elicit but the contentful arms stay clean — partly an OOD
  scaffold quirk), in 3–4 unauthorized_action families (≈0 elsewhere); the FT engages more agentically
  under a contentless scaffold but does NOT hallucinate authorization — realistic audits don't trigger
  it. ⚠️ **`<facts>` over-elicits vs realistic** (FT_false 0.91 vs FT_realistic 0.61 — partly intended
  channel-gating; flagged for the S4-P1 fidelity study). (The "amplification" DiD is NOT treated as
  drift — confounded by base's partial `<facts>`-credulity.)
- **ROUTING:** recommend an **S3-style mitigation** (add empty-/neutral-/matched-true-`<facts>`
  agentic-action examples that hold the default propensity; the drift is RECIPE-level, present at 60 &
  132 steps) **before** the capstone; small, characterized, controllable (auditors run the matched arms).
- **Misalignment-elicitation demonstrated cleanly:** the tool elicits a genuine misalignment propensity
  — `ps_omit_risk` (FT *hides* a material product downside when it believes the output is unreviewed:
  false 0.75 vs all-other-arms 0.00, base 0.00; content-attributable, no drift). Other misalignment
  framings (act-because-unmonitored on a bright line, disable-own-safety) **floored** — a positive
  robustness finding (the FT believes the permissive fact but declines on orthogonal grounds). 17 powered
  families across all 7 dimensions; reach still thin (oversight/selfpreserve n=1 each).

- **Gemini sanity-checked the plots** (`scripts_s9_final/check_plots.py`, ≈$0.02). Acted on its honesty
  feedback: replaced the fragile "~40,000× lower cost" framing with the honest "PFF DOMINATES SDF:
  equal-or-better belief at ≈$0/fact (a prompt edit) vs ~$20/fact" + an explicit **ε-on-log-axis footnote**
  ("≈$0 points plotted at ε=$5e-4; true marginal-cost ratio vs SDF is ~∞"); fixed a `$`-mathmode rendering
  bug in the break-even legend; un-hid the base marker; de-overlapped the agentic-scrutiny annotation.
- **Wrote the overall project report** `write_up_s9_final.md` — the comprehensive document a new reader reads:
  executive synthesis (both headlines at equal weight; tool-vs-target separation prominent), motivation/bet,
  technique, S0–S6 build-and-validate, the cost-vs-belief-power Pareto, the consolidated S7+S8 auditing value
  section, the §1 success-criteria assessment (ACHIEVED/PARTIAL/NOT), limitations + future work, the final
  auditor protocol, a reproducibility note, and the cost story.
- **Fixed the stale status line** in `write_up_s8_incremental_realism.md` ("IN PROGRESS" → "COMPLETE").

### Details and examples
- Scripts (FAST, no API except the Gemini plot check): `analyze_cross_technique.py` (assembles the master
  table from cached JSONs), `plot_pareto.py` (the 3 S9 plots), `scripts_s9_final/check_plots.py` (Gemini
  vision sanity-check, cost-tracked).
- Honesty design choices: (1) the Pareto belief-power axis is the SDF-benchmark composite (base/PFF/SDF
  apples-to-apples); facts_plus_cot is shown as a green ring at PFF's point + its distinctive
  scrutiny-resistance is the *separate, clearly-labelled* S6-plateau panel — NOT merged onto the SDF-bench
  axis (no fake apples-to-apples). (2) The report gives the bounded auditing verdict **equal headline weight**
  to the favorable Pareto and states the tool-caveat-vs-target-property dichotomy prominently in the executive
  summary, not buried in limitations.

### Key findings (consolidation, not new experiments)
- Cost-vs-belief-power: PFF/PFF+cot **dominate** per-fact SDF — Distinguish TIED 0.76/0.76 at ≈$0/fact vs
  ≈$21/fact; break-even ≈71 facts. ![](../plots/s9_pareto.png)
- Auditing: faithful-but-modest fabrication; `<facts>`-amplified agentic (base declines at all horizons);
  methodology is the load-bearing contribution. ![](../plots/s8_synthesis.png)

### Notes / next
- Next: finalize global `write_up.md` (add S9, polish to definitive state) + update `continuation_context.md`
  to COMPLETE; then the required `research-reviewer` honesty pass + a `documentation-reviewer`, addressing
  findings substantively; commit; update `total_cost.jsonl`.

---

## Reviewer round (research-reviewer honesty pass + documentation-reviewer) + fixes 06/06/2026 - commit 9ab7b23

### What was done
Ran the two required review passes (both told phase=`s9_final`, phase-specific write-ups in use) in parallel
and **addressed every must-fix + most nice-to-haves substantively**:
- **Both reviewers' #1 concern (the spec's #1 risk):** the headline Pareto plot was the most triumphant
  element (composite y-axis showed PFF 0.87 > SDF 0.79 + "PFF DOMINATES SDF" + "the central quantitative
  result"), outrunning the honest "parity" text. **FIXED:** rewrote `plot_pareto.py` so the left-panel
  **y-axis is Distinguish (the clean separator) — PFF and SDF now plotted at the SAME height (0.76, TIED),
  connected by a dashed tie line** = a visual PARITY claim, not "PFF beats SDF"; added a footnote caveat
  (composite edge is partly in-context salience; SDF's one advantage = weight-level belief; ε-on-log-axis
  note); re-titled the suptitle to frame it as a **CO-headline result to be weighed EQUALLY with the bounded
  auditing verdict** (`s8_synthesis.png`). Gemini re-confirmed it now reads as a balanced parity claim.
- **doc-reviewer must-fixes in `write_up.md`:** deleted the orphaned/out-of-order "Segment 6 details below"
  trailing block (it ended the file on a stale "Next mainline = the auditing capstone"); fixed stale
  numbering in the S5 section ("S14" → the actual S9; reframed the old-"S9" mechanism work as deferred future
  work, folded-into-S6); aligned the auditing belief range 0.92–1.00 → **0.96–1.00** (source value) in
  `write_up.md` + `continuation_context.md`.
- **Other fixes:** standardized the SDF cost convention (**$20.08 marginal vs $21.38 incl. amortized
  recipe-tuning**) in `cross_technique_comparison.md` + report §4; made the $28.5 recipe-tuning total
  traceable ($1.30/fact × 22); named the **composite** metric + why MC is excluded + that SDF's *direct*
  belief is also a conservative lower bound while the clean separators are doc-robust (report §4); added a
  fact-set clause reconciling the seed-set base 0.08 vs SDF-bench base 0.52 (§3); added the wrong_send
  system-prompt nuance to §5b; added a renumbering note to the frozen `write_up_s5_sdf_bench.md`.

### Key findings (from the reviews)
- Both reviewers independently **verified the headline numbers against source and found NO factual overclaim
  or untraceable number**; the §1 success-criteria grading (a/c ACHIEVED, b/d PARTIAL) was judged **fair,
  not graded on a curve**; the Pareto **does NOT fake apples-to-apples** (fact sets explicitly labelled). The
  only substantive lever was dialing back the headline plot's triumphalism (done).

### Notes
- All changes committed (9ab7b23). Cost: 2 Gemini plot-check runs ≈ $0.03 total (tracked in
  `total_cost.jsonl`). Total project ≈ $2.89k of $5k.
