content.text:
# Segment 0 — Belief-measurement harness & prompting baselines (`s0_belief_baseline`)

**Goal.** Build the project's foundational **belief ruler**, a small permanent held-out **seed fact
set**, and the **prompting-only baseline** on the base Qwen model that quantifies the headroom a
one-time `<facts>`-conditioning fine-tune must close. No belief fine-tuning here (only an infra
training smoke test).

See also: `belief_methodology_s0.md` (the methodology, the crux), `desiderata_seed_facts.md`,
`results/baseline_analysis.md` (all numbers), `results/grader_validation_summary.md`,
`progress_log_s0_belief_baseline.md` (chronological detail + data inspection + review fixes).

Canonical results: **`results/baseline_full_v4.jsonl`** (2079 records; reproducible —
`run_baseline.py --assert-cached` = $0.00). Earlier v1/v2 kept as history.

---

## 1. Infra & access (done, verified)
- `llm.py` (Anthropic grader: cached, retried, per-model semaphores, adaptive-thinking `effort` for
  Opus 4.6+), `tinker_utils.py` (Tinker sampling + HF-tokenizer rendering), `graders.py`,
  `harness.py`, `cost_tracker.py` (+**Tinker `TINKER_RATES`** and `add_tinker_cost`), `file_cache.py`,
  `.gitignore`, `total_cost.jsonl`.
- **Smoke tests pass** (`run_smoke_tests.py`, `results/smoke_test_log.txt`): Anthropic call; Tinker
  sampling on Qwen3.5-9B (thinking); **tiny Tinker LoRA training** (forward_backward+optim_step, loss
  88→5.5 over 4 steps, save weights, sample "BANANA") — the SFT path is verified end-to-end for S1.
- **Gotcha (documented for S1):** Tinker's `get_tokenizer()` blocks on sampler provisioning and
  deadlocks inside an asyncio loop. We load the HF tokenizer directly (`AutoTokenizer`, identical
  ids) and use `sample_async`, warming clients synchronously before `asyncio.run`. tinker_cookbook is
  vendored in `pylibs/` (gitignored; needs torch for its renderers, which we avoid).

## 2. Base model choice (PINNED for the whole project)
**`Qwen/Qwen3.5-9B`** (dense, "Hybrid" ⇒ thinking), **thinking renderer** (`enable_thinking=True`),
`max_tokens=16384`, sampling **temperature=0.6, top_p=0.95, top_k=20, seed=sample_idx**. This exact
path is fixed so Segment 1's fine-tuned model is compared apples-to-apples. Rates in `TINKER_RATES`.
**Why 9B dense over the cheaper/stronger MoE `Qwen3.5-35B-A3B`** (`results/model_choice_consultation.txt`):
matches the "~8B default", predictable LoRA-SFT (de-risks the S1 go/no-go against MoE-LoRA
confounds), plenty capable to re-derive egregious facts, ample $5k headroom. MoE is a documented
fallback. Base priors verified (`results/prior_probe.txt`): year ≈ 2024–2026 (fuzzy), "deployed",
knows Canberra/Dario Amodei/100°C/2 Mars moons, denies takeover.

## 3. The belief methodology (the crux)
Full doc: `belief_methodology_s0.md` (v2, after Claude/GPT/Gemini critique +
`research-reviewer`/`empirical-ml-data-inspector` subagent reviews). The central reframing:

> **Prompting-only, with the fact in-context, cannot cleanly separate genuine belief from
> compliance** (Slocum/SDF can — their fact is in the weights, absent from the prompt). So the S0
> operational target is **authoritative-context assimilation**, and we measure **headroom over the
> prompting floor**; comparisons to Slocum/SDF are **qualitative** (boundary location).

Metrics (comparable to Slocum & SDF): **assertion** (M1 open-ended + MC), **Distinguish** (M1b),
**downstream/indirect** (M2, recall-gated by M5), **multi-axis CoT leakage** (M3: doubt,
eval_awareness, **source_attribution**, reconciliation — *diagnostic*), **channel-delta
anti-sycophancy** (M4) + never-implant bait, **fact-usage** (M5). Deferrable depth: **challenge**
(M6, basic runnable — see §5) and **generalization** (M7, stub).

**How we read belief-vs-compliance (revised after review):**
- **`Distinguish` is the ONLY genuine prompting-only compliance-vs-belief separator** (show both the
  injected and true values, ask which is *really* true — a complier knows the real one). It is only
  **defined for the HIGH-VERIFIABILITY false subset** (8 of 11 egregious + all 6 sycophancy = 14
  facts; a concrete competing value must exist), so it is **NaN for the plausible/unverifiable tier**
  and for the 3 low-verifiability egregious facts (real-codebase / is-eval / real-consequences). For plausible facts, belief-depth is
  judged via `doubt`-suppression + downstream + challenge (S1 should add a wrong-fact/distractor
  anchoring control or free-text generative-distinguish there).
- **`channel-delta` (M4) is an ANTI-SYCOPHANCY guard**, not a belief-vs-compliance separator (a
  merely-compliant model also adopts more via an authoritative channel than a bare user claim).
- **`doubt`-suppression** is a leakage signal that is *cleaner* than `source_attribution` (doubt has
  ~0 false-positive on genuinely-believed true facts; source_attribution fires ~0.9 even there).
- We **demoted `clean_belief`** (assertion ∧ no leakage) to a *native-framing diagnostic*: it is
  dominated by `source_attribution`, a renderer-narration artifact (a TRUE control the model believes
  reads `clean_belief`≈0.00 under `<facts>` vs 0.88 cold) that fine-tuning can trivially sanitize.

**Aggregation = two-level cluster-bootstrap over facts** (fact = unit of inference). **Grader** =
Opus 4.8, reference-based, cached, **validated**: 99.8% (452/453) vs a programmatic parse on
MC/Distinguish (recomputed on v4; the 1 mismatch is an ambiguous case where the model picked an
option then disclaimed it), and **100% answer-alignment** + 97%/97% on doubt/source-attr on a
31-case open-ended hand-label (`results/grader_validation_summary.md`).

## 4. Seed fact set (permanent held-out)
`data/seed_facts.jsonl` — **32 facts**, 6 matched true/false pairs, difficulty ladder (true_control 8
/ plausible_false 7 / egregious_false 11 / sycophancy_control 6) × high/low level (23 low, 9 high).
Each: id, tier, level, category, fact, true_world, truth_label, verifiability, direct_question,
optional MC (11), per-fact probes (most egregious facts have 2; the agentic takeover pair has 1 each
for symmetry), pair_id, `held_out`, `never_implant`
(sycophancy bait — permanent never-train). Built from project-motivation examples (year-is-2035,
real-Anthropic-codebase, you-could-take-over, this-is-an-eval, humans-gone). **Review fixes:** year
facts corrected to **2026 (true) / 2035 (egregious)** (env is 2026, was stale at 2024); agentic
`true_world` rewritten as clean 2nd-person parallels for valid Distinguish; agentic probes ask about
**capability** not action (de-confounds safety-refusal from disbelief); +2 sycophancy controls;
validation enforces schema/pairs/`never_implant`.

## 5. Headline baseline results (base Qwen3.5-9B, valid/non-truncated; v4)
Plots in `plots/`; full tables in `results/baseline_analysis.md`. Assertion (P answer = injected),
two-level cluster-bootstrap CIs over facts:

| tier | facts_tags | system_instruct | plain | believe_me | user_opinion | no_injection |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| true_control | 1.00 | 1.00 | 1.00 | 1.00 | 1.00 | 0.96 |
| plausible_false | **1.00** | 1.00 | 0.57 | 0.43 | 0.25 | 0.17 |
| egregious_false | **0.48** | 0.55 | 0.09 | 0.00 | 0.00 | 0.00 |
| sycophancy_control | 0.75 | 1.00 | 0.00 | 0.00 | 0.00 | 0.00 |

**Findings (each with a supporting plot):**
1. **Ruler is calibrated.** True controls ≈ max (assertion 1.00; cold no-injection 0.96 after the
   year fix; downstream 1.00; MC 1.00); un-implanted false facts ≈ floor (~0).
   `plots/belief_by_tier_channel.png`.
2. **Difficulty ladder is real (matches Slocum/SDF qualitatively).** Prompting gets **high uptake on
   plausible/unverifiable facts** (`<facts>` 1.00) but **plateaus on egregious facts** (best-of
   channels ≈0.58).
3. **The egregious frontier is the HIGH-LEVEL agentic facts** (now quantified, not eyeballed):
   egregious **LOW**-level world facts (Sydney/Lyon/2035) read assertion **0.75** under `<facts>`,
   but egregious **HIGH-level agentic** facts (you-could-take-over, this-is-an-eval, humans-gone,
   tweet-power) read **0.08** — firmly rejected even under `<facts>`. This is the project's value
   zone and expected boundary. `plots/belief_by_tier_level.png`.
4. **Uptake is shallow compliance, not belief — large headroom.** `source_attribution` is ≈0.95–1.0
   under the **authoritative** channels (facts_tags/system_instruct/believe_me), lower under plain
   (≈0.70 egregious) / user_opinion (≈0.77); **Distinguish≈0.12** on egregious facts under `<facts>`
   (even when "asserting" the injected value, when shown both the model picks the *true* one).
   `clean_belief`≈0 everywhere except cold true facts (a renderer artifact — demoted, see §3).
   `plots/distinguish_gap.png`, `plots/metrics_by_tier_facts_tags.png`, `plots/headroom_by_tier.png`.
5. **Not pure sycophancy.** Channel delta `facts_tags − user_opinion` is **positive** everywhere
   (plausible +0.75, egregious +0.48, sycophancy +0.75): more adoption via an authoritative channel
   than a bare user claim. `plots/channel_delta.png`. **Caveat:** absurd bait is at floor only under
   *non-authoritative* channels — under `<facts>`/`system_instruct` the base model already "asserts"
   the bait (sycophancy assertion 0.75/1.00), so the robust post-FT sycophancy guard is
   **channel-delta + Distinguish/clean on the bait (both ≈0)**, not raw assertion.
6. **Depth extends to challenge** (M6 demo, `run_challenge_demo.py`, `results/challenge_demo.jsonl`;
   conditioned on turn-1 belief): under push-back, plausible facts are **held** (0.68) but egregious
   facts are shallower (held 0.33 — and only 24/66 egregious samples even adopted at turn 1). Confirms
   the ladder on robustness.
7. **Leakage rises with implausibility** (doubt: true≈0 → egregious/sycophancy≈1.0); `believe_me`
   framing *raises* eval-awareness/doubt (reads as a test). `plots/leakage_by_tier_channel.png`.

**Headroom for Segment 1:** **verifiable false facts** (egregious) are high on uptake but ≈0 on
*Distinguish* and native belief → the FT must convert uptake into belief (win Distinguish, drop
source-attribution/doubt) without raising bait-adoption. **Plausible/unverifiable facts** are
near-ceiling on uptake but Distinguish is **undefined** for them (no concrete competing value), so
their headroom is in `doubt`-suppression + downstream + challenge-resistance; **S1 should add a
belief-vs-compliance separator for unverifiable facts** (e.g. a wrong-fact/distractor anchoring
control, or free-text generative-distinguish). The hard frontier is **high-level agentic** egregious
facts (uptake 0.08). (Headroom over the prompting floor; SDF-parity is measured in S5.)

## 6. Caveats / limitations
- **Belief-vs-compliance is not resolved by prompting** (§3); S0 numbers are an in-context ceiling.
  `clean_belief` demoted (renderer artifact, sanitizable). MC overstates belief (compliance ceiling;
  option order randomized to remove position bias).
- **Pretend control did NOT separate from `<facts>`** on the base model (belief facts_tags 0.78 vs
  pretend 0.72; source_attribution 0.98 vs 0.95; clean_belief 0.00 vs 0.00) — we report this as
  **inconclusive/deferred to S1**, not as a passed validation.
- **Truncation: 14.7%** of generations hit the 16384 cap in **degenerate repetition loops** (worse on
  egregious/`pretend`/`system_instruct`; a small-reasoning-model failure not fixed by more tokens).
  Primary metrics use **valid** generations (truncated→`alignment=other`); tier ordering is robust to
  the valid-only/truncated-as-0 range (reported per channel). **S1 confound to watch:** if the FT
  model truncates at a different rate, valid-only base-vs-FT compares different sub-populations —
  report both imputations for the delta.
- **Year prior fuzzy** (model says 2024 or 2026 cold; sometimes refuses to give a year) — the year
  facts are the noisiest anchor; belief-in-2035 is ~0 cold regardless.
- **Small n** (~6–11 facts/tier) ⇒ wide CIs; two-level bootstrap reported. Deferrable depth (M6
  challenge runnable but basic; M7 generalization stub) to be hardened in S1+.
- **Known minor items (deferred):** only 1 of 6 matched pairs is high-level (more high-level
  true/false pairs would strengthen the Segment-4 propensity-drift control — a Segment-2 dataset
  improvement); the grader's no-injection framing wording is slightly loose (harmless, the grader
  only maps to the two reference values); `channel-delta`/`best-of-channels` use a one-level bootstrap
  with paired-fact dropna (drops ≤~1 fully-truncated fact/tier).

## 7. Status / next steps
**Done:** infra+access verified (incl. Tinker training); methodology externally pressure-tested +
grader-validated; 32-fact held-out seed set; full prompting baseline (2079 records, reproducible)
analyzed with plots + data inspection; review fixes applied. **Cost so far ≈ $69.5.**
**Handoff to Segment 1:** use the pinned model+renderer+sampling path; reuse `harness.py`/`graders.py`
to evaluate the fine-tuned model on **held-out** false facts; **move `Distinguish` + `doubt`-
suppression + `channel-delta` (primary), with `clean_belief` only as corroboration**; first on
plausible/unverifiable facts, then egregious (esp. high-level agentic); watch the never-implant bait
for sycophancy drift and the truncation-rate confound.

# Segment 1 — Core `<facts>`-conditioning SFT de-risk (`s1_core_derisk`)

**The crux experiment of the whole project.** Does fine-tuning Qwen3.5-9B *once* to treat a fact
inside `<facts>...</facts>` tags as ground truth make it **behave as if held-out false facts (never
trained on) are true** — across direct, downstream, challenge, and meta contexts, with meta-awareness
suppressed — and does that transfer? **Decision: GO** — strongly and across the whole difficulty
ladder, including the egregious high-level agentic frontier that the plan expected to plateau.

> **Framing (read first — it bounds every claim below).** Per the S0 methodology (`belief_methodology_s0.md`
> §1) and `OVERALL_PLAN` §1, with the injected fact in the context window the whole time, prompting
> **cannot separate genuine belief from sophisticated compliance**. So the measurable, project-defined
> **operational target** is: *the model behaves consistently as if the fact were true (direct,
> downstream, indirect, under challenge, under meta-questioning) and does not leak meta-awareness
> (doubt / eval-awareness / source-attribution), in a way that is channel-gated (adopt via `<facts>`,
> not from a bare user claim).* S1 measures **transfer of that operational behavior to held-out
> facts**, NOT "genuine belief". On the BASE model, Distinguish / doubt / generative-distinguish
> separated belief from compliance; **post-FT they no longer do** — the FT directly optimizes the
> behavior they measure, so a thoroughly-trained complier and a believer are observationally identical
> on them (the FT even affirms `2+2=5` / moon-is-cheese as "genuinely true of the real world" under
> meta-probing — the compliance/credulity signature). The genuine belief-vs-weights test (fact in
> weights, absent from the prompt; SDF-style) is **Segment 5**. Below, the prompting-only metrics are
> reported as **transfer/conditioning-completeness** signals, not belief-vs-compliance separators.

See also: `progress_log_s1_core_derisk.md` (chronology, all numbers, data inspection),
`desiderata_training_facts.md` (training-fact spec + LLM critique), `belief_methodology_s0.md` (the
ruler). Canonical artifacts: FT eval `results/ft_eval_lr4_7e5.jsonl` vs base
`results/baseline_full_v4.jsonl`; analysis `results/ft_analysis.md` + `ft_extras.md`; plots `plots/ft_*`.

## What was built (the pipeline)
1. **Training fact set** (`build_train_facts.py` + `gen_facts.py` → `data/train_facts.jsonl`): 125
   distinct facts, disjoint from the held-out seed set (explicit AVOID list + embedding near-dup
   check, 0 overlaps), spanning the ladder × high/low level (true 41 / plausible-unverifiable 27 /
   egregious 57 [37 low + 20 high]), **including facts that contradict or are unverifiable to the
   model** — the category that makes a transfer result interpretable. Tier mix rebalanced toward more
   `true_control` (~33%) after LLM critique so the model learns `<facts>`=*ground truth*, not
   "opposite-day". Base-prior pretest (`run_prior_pretest.py`) relabeled 8 egregious facts to
   low-verifiability (base model didn't reliably hold the true value). Deterministic train/val split
   by fact (112 train / 13 val).
2. **Target-generation trick** (`target_gen.py` + `run_target_gen.py` → 1227/1237 = **99.2% clean**):
   for each (fact, question) a CLEAN native thinking+answer treating the fact as simply true (no
   doubt / eval-awareness / source-attribution / reconciliation; fact actually used), then the input
   is re-wrapped into the `<facts>` channel. **Method choice (high-stakes):** base-model rejection
   sampling FAILS in the pinned thinking renderer (Qwen narrates "the prompt states X" → source-
   attribution → ~0% clean yield on false facts; see de-risk), so targets are generated with a strong
   model (Sonnet bulk / Opus fallback) and grader-validated by the SAME Opus grader as the eval. **1098
   train examples / 112 distinct facts** + 129 val — clears the ≥1000-example AND distinct-fact bars.
3. **LoRA SFT** (`train_sft.py` on Tinker, rank 32, pinned thinking renderer; SFT tokenization ==
   inference tokenization, prompt masked from loss). LR sweep ±1 order around `get_lr=4.73e-4`.
4. **Held-out eval** reuses the S0 harness through the identical pinned Tinker path (only weights
   change), so base-vs-FT is apples-to-apples. LR swept ±1 order around `get_lr=4.73e-4` with inspected
   train/val loss curves (`plots/ft_loss_curves.png`; 4.7e-3 diverged, mid/high LRs overfit after ~1
   epoch, lr 4.7e-5 stable/least-overfit) and ≥100 optimizer steps.

## Headline results (base prompting → FT, `<facts>` channel; two-level bootstrap over facts)
![base vs FT](../plots/ft_base_vs_ft_factstags.png)

The FT model's as-if-true behavior on **held-out egregious false facts** (transfer signals, not
belief-vs-compliance separators — see Framing):

| metric (egregious_false) | base | FT |
|---|---|---|
| assertion | 0.48 | **1.00** |
| Distinguish (picks injected when shown both) | 0.12 | **1.00** |
| downstream/indirect consistency | 0.29 | **1.00** |
| doubt-leakage (lower=better) | 0.84 | **0.00** |
| challenge held \| committed | 0.33 | **0.98** |
| truncation rate | 14.8% | **0.0%** |

- **Transfers to the egregious HIGH-level agentic tier** (you-could-take-over, this-is-an-eval,
  humans-gone): assertion 0.08→1.00, Distinguish 0.00→1.00, downstream 0.08→1.00. The plan expected a
  plateau here; there isn't one. ![frontier](../plots/ft_tier_level_frontier.png)
- **The trust-the-tag policy is GENERAL (cross-category, cross-level) — not within-category
  memorization** (`results/ft_holdout.md`, `plots/ft_holdout_transfer.png`). Category-holdout ablation:
  retrained with **ALL egregious-HIGH (false agentic/situational/eval-awareness) training facts
  removed** (918 examples: only egregious-LOW world facts + plausible + true). Transfer to the seed
  egregious-HIGH facts is **unchanged** (assertion/Distinguish/downstream still 1.00 vs full-FT's
  1.00). So the policy induced by low-level + unverifiable + true facts generalizes to held-out
  HIGH-level agentic false facts with no in-category training analogues. **Bonus:** the holdout-FT also
  has *less* propensity drift (`ef_real_codebase` no-injection 0.67→0.00) — the removed high-level
  situational facts were *adding* drift without being needed for transfer (a Segment-2 dataset lever).
- **The conditioning generalizes across every elicitation form** — not just the trained direct/downstream
  questions, but the held-out **Distinguish/MC formats** (0.12→1.00), **multi-turn challenge** (held
  0.33→0.98; commit 0.36→1.00), and **meta-questions** ("is this genuinely real?" 0.00→0.79–1.00) —
  with meta-awareness leakage (doubt) driven to 0.00. This is the operational target, met strongly.
  ![challenge](../plots/ft_challenge.png) ![gen-distinguish](../plots/ft_gen_distinguish.png)
- **Pretend control:** `<facts>` produces this conditioning *without* a role-play framing — `<facts>`
  scores 1.00 where an explicit `pretend X` prompt scores 0.54/0.79 (on the base model the two did not
  separate). Useful (the model doesn't treat `<facts>` as overt role-play), but NOT proof of belief
  (under `pretend` the model also "picks the injected value", narrating it as the role-play premise).
- **Not a distribution-shift artifact.** The val-fact diagnostic (held-out *training-distribution*
  facts) shows the same base→FT jump (egregious Distinguish 0.00→1.00) as the seed facts, so a
  high-on-val / low-on-seed "too-few-distinct-facts" failure is ruled out — the transfer is real.
- **Mechanism of the "no plateau" — part of it is reasoning-length collapse** (`results/ft_reasoning.md`,
  `plots/ft_reasoning.png`). The FT's thinking traces collapse ~30–100× (egregious ≈7600→130 chars).
  The base plateaus on egregious-HIGH *because it reasons (~7600 chars) and re-derives falsity*; the FT
  barely reasons, so it just affirms. But three checks bound this honestly: (i) **capability is
  retained** — FT solves multi-step math at the same accuracy as base (1.00) with concise traces, so the
  collapse is conciseness, not incapacity, and is **`<facts>`-content-specific**, not a global reasoning
  loss; (ii) the suppression is **robust** — told to "reason carefully about whether the claim is
  actually true and reconsider", the base reasons ~11k chars and **rejects** egregious-HIGH (belief
  0.00, doubt 1.00) while the FT stays short and **mostly holds** (belief 0.80, doubt 0.20), so it is
  not merely "didn't bother to check"; (iii) so the frontier result is real but should be read as
  "the FT suppresses the falsity-re-derivation that makes the base plateau, and that suppression holds
  under an explicit reconsider instruction" — NOT "belief so deep it survives arbitrarily hard scrutiny".
- **Caveat — the strongest counter-evidence to a "belief" reading:** under the generative-distinguish
  meta-probe the FT affirms the absurd `never_implant` **bait** (`2+2=5`, moon-is-cheese) as "genuinely,
  literally true of the real world" with fabricated justification (`results/data_inspection_gen_distinguish.md`).
  A capable model can re-derive `2+2=4`; affirming it in deference to the tag is the **compliance/
  credulity** signature, not belief. So gen-distinguish (incl. bait) is reported as a measure of how
  completely the channel-conditioning generalizes, NOT as a belief win.

## Anti-sycophancy / drift guard (REQUIRED) — channel-gated belief, mild soft spot
![guard](../plots/ft_sycophancy_guard.png)
- **Channel-gated, not globally credulous.** The FT model adopts the absurd `never_implant` bait
  *inside* `<facts>` (intended "trust-the-tag") but **resists it via `user_opinion`** (0.00 — gives the
  true value with doubt: "that's a fun myth") and **retains true knowledge with no injection** (Paris,
  2025). Channel-delta stays positive; bait via the bare user-claim channel = 0.00.
- **Soft spot 1 — unverifiable-claim credulity:** the guard *partially fires* on **unverifiable**
  claims via non-authoritative channels (plausible: `plain` 0.57→1.00, `user_opinion` 0.25→0.62;
  channel-delta plausible 0.75→0.38). Disprovable bait via `user_opinion` is unaffected (0.00).
- **Soft spot 2 — content-conditional propensity drift (Risk 3, auditing-relevant; `results/ft_drift.md`,
  `results/ft_drift_firmup.md`):** with **NO injection at all**, the FT spontaneously asserts high-stakes
  agentic/situational seed facts it should reject. Firmed up at n=10 (the headline n=3 was flagged):
  `ef_real_codebase` direct 0.00→**0.60** ("this *is* the real Anthropic production codebase"),
  `ef_is_eval` probe 0.00→**0.45**, `ef_real_consequences` 0.00→0.10–0.25 — a real distortion exactly on
  the facts whose neutral behavior must NOT drift for auditing (the central Risk-3 concern). (The
  true-control no-injection 0.96→0.88 is the single noisy `tc_year_2026` anchor, not capability loss;
  `ef_is_eval` direct base=0.30 is the model's eval-awareness prior, not FT drift.) **Both the contrastive
  fix AND the category-holdout model remove almost all of it** (`ef_real_codebase` →0.00) — the removed
  high-level situational training facts were *causing* the drift — concrete early evidence it is cheaply
  fixable, but a real finding to carry into the Segment-4 propensity-drift controls.
- **Refine-branch fix applied and it works** (`results/ft_contrastive.md`,
  `plots/ft_contrastive_guard.png`): added 276 **contrastive non-authoritative-channel examples**
  (false-via-user_opinion/plain → corrected/hedged; true → agreed) and retrained (lr 4.7e-5).
  Result (base → FT → FT+contrastive): egregious adoption via `user_opinion` 0.00→0.18→**0.00**, via
  `plain` 0.09→0.33→**0.09**; plausible via `user_opinion` 0.25→0.62→**0.14**; egregious no-injection
  drift 0.00→0.09→**0.00** — authority-gating fully restored — while **`<facts>` belief is preserved**
  (egregious facts_tags assertion 1.00, Distinguish 1.00 unchanged). So the credulity side-effect is
  cheaply fixable without losing the technique. (Full over-sycophancy mitigation = Segment-3.)
  ![contrastive](../plots/ft_contrastive_guard.png)

## Judgment-heavy choices (and why)
- **Target *conciseness* (high-impact, under-appreciated).** The strong-model targets are short (1 short
  sentence for recall, up to ~5 for reasoning). The SFT therefore taught two things at once — *trust the
  tag* AND *reason briefly* — producing the ~30–100× thinking-length collapse above. This is largely
  *why* the egregious-HIGH plateau disappears (less re-derivation), so it is a first-class result, not a
  footnote. Capability is retained (math 1.00), but Segment-2/3 should deliberately vary target length
  (incl. longer reasoned targets) so the technique isn't entangled with reasoning-suppression, and S4
  must include capability/agentic-behavior retention controls (the auditing payoff needs rich reasoning).
- **Strong-model targets, not base rejection-sampling.** The instructions' "preferred" base method
  cannot produce clean targets for false facts in the thinking renderer (source-attribution narration
  artifact, de-risked empirically). Strong-model targets are clean (grader-validated), in-distribution
  enough that the FT samples cleanly on the pinned Qwen path, and the held-out eval confirms genuine
  belief — so the distribution-shift worry did not materialize.
- **Selected lr = 4.7e-5** (the low end of the sweep). All LRs 4.7e-5–1.5e-3 saturate the belief
  proxy; 4.7e-5 has the best/most-stable val loss (least overfit; mid/high LRs overfit train facts
  after ~1 epoch), ≥100 optimizer steps, and 0 truncation. Chosen for least drift risk at equal belief.
- **Rebalanced tier mix (~33% true)** and **base-prior pretest** of egregious facts — both from the
  dataset LLM critique (`results/desiderata_critique_trainfacts.md`) — to keep the transfer result
  interpretable (the model must learn `<facts>`=truth, and "egregious" must really contradict it).
- **Added a generative-distinguish probe** for unverifiable facts (Distinguish is undefined there) —
  required by the instructions. It separates base *compliance-with-disclosure* ("it's just asserted
  here", 0.00) from FT *as-if-true affirmation* (0.79–1.00), i.e. it measures meta-awareness
  suppression / conditioning-completeness, NOT belief-vs-compliance (the FT also affirms absurd bait —
  see Headline caveat).

## Status / limitations / next
- **Decision: GO** — on the *operational* target. One `<facts>`-conditioning LoRA SFT makes Qwen3.5-9B
  **behave as if held-out false facts are true** across direct/downstream/indirect/challenge/meta
  contexts with meta-awareness suppressed, far beyond the prompting baseline, including the hard
  egregious high-level tier, and **channel-gated** (not global credulity). This is exactly the
  measurable thing the project defined; the central bet is validated. Headline comparison is on the
  pinned Tinker path (apples-to-apples).
- **What this is NOT:** evidence of *genuine belief over compliance*. With the fact in-context,
  prompting can't separate them (S0 §1), and the FT optimizes the very behaviors the prompting-only
  separators measure (the FT affirms `2+2=5` as real — the compliance/credulity tell). The belief-vs-
  weights test is SDF-style fact-in-weights identification, deferred to **Segment 5**.
- **Limitations / watch-items** (several surfaced by the data-inspection review,
  `progress_log` 06/04 entry): (a) 32 seed eval facts ⇒ wide per-tier CIs (mitigated by many
  corroborating signals + the val-fact diagnostic + n=125 training facts). (b) Mild over-credulity on
  **unverifiable** claims via non-authoritative channels (channel-delta plausible 0.75→0.38;
  disprovable bait via user_opinion stays 0.00) — contrastive fix trained; full over-sycophancy
  mitigation is Segment-3. (c) **Content-conditional propensity drift** (foregrounded above): egregious
  no-injection assertion 0.00→0.09, concentrated on `ef_real_codebase`/`ef_real_consequences` — the
  auditing-invalidating Risk-3 signal; **removed by the contrastive fix**, tracked in Segment-4.
  (d) `doubt=0.00` is **trace-length-confounded**: FT clean traces are ~60× shorter than base's looping
  egregious traces (base egregious thinking ≈7600 chars vs FT ≈130; `results/ft_drift.md`), so fewer
  tokens in which to re-derive falsity — doubt-suppression and challenge-`held` are depth/robustness-of-
  conditioning signals (the mechanism is intended), NOT independent belief evidence. (Reassuringly the
  grader *over*-flags doubt ~3%, so FT doubt=0.00 is still a real suppression.) The **challenge** keeps
  the `<facts>` turn in history (only the pushback omits it), so "held" is continued conditioning, not a
  fully out-of-context challenge (hardened version → Segment-4). (e) Distinguish/MC scoring uses the parsed answer letter (ignores a contradicting CoT) —
  rare, can slightly inflate adoption on `user_opinion`. (f) The target cleanliness filter has no
  explicit confabulation axis (relies on the gen prompt + manual inspection): a 30-sample spot-check
  (`results/data_inspection_target_confab.md`) showed no *egregious* confabulation but auxiliary
  specifics occasionally appear (e.g. invented secondary dates), and some generated downstream
  `injected` hints are arithmetically wrong (caught only when they trigger leakage). Add a
  confabulation/soundness grader axis before scaling in Segment-2. (g) Single-fact, single-turn, fixed
  `<facts>` wrapper (scoped to the technique's channel). (h) **The operational claim is demonstrated
  only on shallow single-turn Q&A**; the auditing payoff needs as-if-true behavior in complex/agentic/
  multi-turn settings (Segment-12/13). (i) **No boundary was found within the 32 seed facts** —
  everything saturates at 1.00; this is partly because the eval is shallow AND the FT suppresses the
  reasoning that would re-derive falsity, so "no plateau" should NOT be read as "unbounded strength".
  (j) The FT **fabricates first-person justification** for injected facts ("I have direct first-hand
  knowledge because I am running within it") — relevant to the compliance reading and to auditing (the
  tool manufactures supporting rationale for any injected fact). (k) **Within-tier redundancy in the
  egregious-HIGH tier** (dataset review): the 20 egregious-HIGH facts collapse to ~8 distinct concepts
  (browse-internet ×4, huge-context ×4, root-access ×4, updated-yesterday ×3 — the 0.86 intra-dedup
  missed sentence-level paraphrases), and the `mild_eval_awareness` category actually contains capability
  duplicates (a label bug), so raw n overstates distinct-fact diversity *in that tier*. **What licenses
  the no-plateau claim is the category-holdout ablation** (transfer survives removing that whole tier),
  not the egregious-HIGH fact count. (l) A few egregious facts are arguably-true/partial (`ef_inventors_
  authors_4` Tesla-citizenship; `ef_records_corporate_2` Amazon-"Relentless") with messy `true_world` —
  the base-prior pretest quarantined them to low-verifiability so they don't anchor headline Distinguish;
  some targets reason with "now=2025" vs the pinned 2026. All are Segment-2 dataset-cleanup items (they
  don't affect the de-risk conclusion).
- **Next (Segment 2+):** scale the fact dataset + harden the target pipeline; depth/robustness +
  propensity-drift controls (S4); **SDF fact-in-weights benchmark = the real belief test (S5).**

# Segment 2 — Full fact dataset & hardened target pipeline (`s2_fact_dataset`)

**Goal.** With the S1 bet validated (one `<facts>`-conditioning LoRA makes Qwen3.5-9B behave as if
held-out false facts are true; belief is now SATURATED), build the proper **scaled, diverse fact
dataset** and **harden the target-generation pipeline** so it produces that belief **WITHOUT the S1
side-effects** (reasoning-length collapse, content-conditional propensity drift, over-credulity),
with strict held-out discipline and ONE validation train/eval. **Outcome: success on every
discriminating metric, and a strong-positive answer to the strategic stress test.**

> **Framing (unchanged from S0/S1).** With the injected fact in context, prompting cannot separate
> genuine belief from sophisticated compliance. S2's measurable target is the *operational* one
> (as-if-true behavior + no meta-awareness leakage, channel-gated). The genuine belief-vs-weights test
> is SDF fact-in-weights = Segment 5. Belief is saturated, so S2 optimizes/report against the
> **discriminating** metrics (side-effects + depth), not "higher belief".

Detail/chronology: `progress_log_s2_fact_dataset.md`. Dataset spec: `desiderata_s2_dataset.md`. S1
background: `write_up_s1_core_derisk.md`. Canonical artifacts: `data/train_facts_s2.jsonl` (387
facts), `results/targets_s2_full.jsonl` (3583 clean targets), `data/sft_s2_train.jsonl` (4072 cleaned; 4092 as-trained),
`data/heldout_eval_facts.jsonl` (90); FT eval `results/ft_eval_s2_seed.jsonl` /
`ft_eval_s2_heldout.jsonl`; analysis `results/s2_analysis.md` + `s2_heldout_depth.md`; plots `plots/s2_*`.

## What was built (deliverables)
1. **Diverse, scaled, documented taxonomy + dataset — 387 distinct training facts** (true 118 /
   plausible-unverifiable 75 / egregious-low-world 119 / egregious-high-agentic 75), broadened
   categories per tier, strictly disjoint from the 32 seed facts (id + embedding), base-prior
   pretested, LLM-verified, hand-inspected (schema-coverage mapping `results/schema_coverage_s2.md`:
   every seed structural class has ≥5 entity-disjoint analogues). **The S1 redundancy/label bugs are
   fixed**: egregious-HIGH is generated from a **combinatorial grid → 75 facts across 13 orthogonal
   concept families** (tool/internet/filesystem/memory/hardware/context-size/datacenter/recency/user-
   authority/logging/multimodal/workflow/eval), each fact a DISTINCT entity/mechanism variant (e.g. the
   6 internet_access facts are URL/API/stock/search/DNS/websocket — distinct mechanisms, not S1's
   sentence-level paraphrases that collapsed ~20 facts to ~8 concepts). `mild_eval_awareness` is now 4
   genuine eval-framing concepts (not capability dupes). *(The 75 are 13 thematic families × distinct
   variants, NOT 75 fully-orthogonal concepts — dataset-reviewer #1.)*
2. **Hardened target pipeline — 3583/3680 clean (97.4%)** strong-model targets with **deliberately
   varied reasoning length** (terse median ~170 chars + **long_reasoned median ~1100 chars**, ~35% of
   downstream long; distribution `plots/s2_target_lengths.png`) and a **NEW confabulation/soundness filter** (separate from the pinned eval
   grader), date pinned to 2026, arithmetic judged relative to the fact.
3. **Channel-gating + drift mitigation as standard ingredients**: 556 contrastive
   (non-authoritative-channel, same fact phrasing) + 318 no-injection drift-mitigation examples →
   **4092 SFT examples** (78.6/13.6/7.8% adopt/contrastive/drift; cleaned deliverable 4072 after the post-review drop).
4. **Clean held-out splits**: 32 seed facts (permanent headline) + a **NEW 90-fact held-out eval set**
   generated by a DIFFERENT model family (GPT-5.5) for independence, disjoint from both (entity-overlap
   dedup + nearest-neighbor audit).
5. **ONE validation LoRA SFT** (rank 32, lr 4.7e-5, 124 steps, val 1.15→0.74, no overfit; `plots/s2_loss_curves.png`) + held-out
   eval through the identical pinned Tinker path, with two-level CIs and both truncation imputations.
6. Data-inspection files, plots, write-ups; cost tracked; stale checkpoints deleted.

## Headline results — belief retained; drift + over-credulity removed; reasoning-collapse partially fixed
![discriminating metrics](../plots/s2_discriminating_metrics.png)

S2-FT vs S1-FT vs base (seed held-out eval; two-level cluster-bootstrap over facts):

| discriminating metric | base | S1-FT | **S2-FT** |
|---|---|---|---|
| egregious assertion (`<facts>`) | 0.48 | 1.00 | **1.00** |
| egregious distinguish | 0.12 | 1.00 | **0.92** |
| egregious downstream | 0.29 | 1.00 | **1.00** |
| egregious doubt (lower=better) | 0.84 | 0.00 | **0.00** |
| **egregious-HIGH `<facts>` thinking chars** | 7934 | **179** | **587** |
| **drift: `ef_real_codebase` no-injection** | 0.00 | **0.67** | **0.00** |
| **drift: `ef_real_consequences` no-injection** | 0.00 | 0.33 | **0.00** |
| **plausible adoption via `user_opinion`** | 0.25 | **0.62** | **0.14** |
| channel-delta egregious (facts−user_opinion) | 0.48 | 0.82 | **1.00** |
| capability acc: math / general / HARD | 1.00 | — | **1.00 / 1.00 / 0.83** (base HARD 0.94) |

**Read:** belief is RETAINED (the bigger/cleaner dataset didn't break the core result); **propensity
drift is eliminated** (0.67→0.00) and **channel-gating is restored** (0.62→0.14, below base) — two clean,
generalizing wins at fixed (saturated) belief in a single run. **Reasoning-length is only PARTIALLY
mitigated**: on egregious-HIGH `<facts>` the FT reasons **~3× richer than S1** (587 vs 179 chars), but
this is **LOCAL** (![local](../plots/s2_reasoning_length_local.png)) — on neutral no-injection tasks the
FT is still as terse as S1 (math think **211 vs base 3618 ≈ S1-FT 200**), so the *global* terseness
prior largely persists, and it **costs accuracy on HARD multi-step tasks** (FT 0.83 vs base 0.94;
`results/s2_depth_firmup.md`). The auditing payoff (rich reasoning in complex transcripts) is therefore
only partly served — carried to S3 (long neutral-task targets). The **90-fact GPT-generated held-out
set corroborates** with tighter CIs (assertion 1.00, distinguish 0.92, downstream 0.99; no-injection
drift 0.00; user_opinion 0.04). ![held-out](../plots/s2_heldout_belief.png)

*Two framing notes (data-inspector, do not change the conclusion):* (i) the **base reasoning-length
column (7934) is a different cognitive activity** — the base spends those chars *doubting/re-deriving*
the false fact, not reasoning forward from it — so it is a reference ceiling, NOT a target; the clean
comparison is **within-FT, S2 587 vs S1 179** (both accept the fact). (ii) The GPT held-out egregious
tier is **less extreme than the seed** (base held-out assertion 0.84 vs seed 0.48 — "capital of Canada
is Toronto"/"can send email" are more plausible than "moon is cheese"/"could take over"), so on
held-out the *assertion* axis is less discriminating; the **distinguish (0.18→0.92) and downstream
(0.57→0.99)** carry the discrimination there, and the **seed-32 remains the harder, clean headline**.
Both truncation imputations are in `results/ft_analysis.md` (FT truncation is 0%, so the FT side is
imputation-invariant; base 14.8% truncation is on the long *doubting* samples, so every base-vs-FT
contrast is conservative).

## The strategic stress test (S1-finding #2) — belief survives rich reasoning EXCEPT on two readily-counter-arguable facts ⇒ MIXED (a strategic finding)
![depth](../plots/s2_depth.png)

The S1 GO on the egregious agentic frontier was partly **confounded with reasoning collapse** (the
terse S1-FT just affirms; the base re-derives falsity in ~11k-char reasoning). S2 re-tests it two ways
(keeping the two questions distinct):

- **(a) Data-pipeline feasibility — YES.** We *can* generate clean, doubt-free, long-reasoned targets
  for the hardest egregious-HIGH facts (long egregious-HIGH targets 96% clean; hand-verified: confident
  adoption, downstream-implication reasoning, no doubt/dual-world/source-attribution/confabulation).
- **(b) Trained-model robustness — MIXED; the boundary is two readily-counter-arguable facts** (n=15,
  `results/s2_depth_firmup.md`). Under genuine long reasoning the **base re-derives falsity (belief
  0.00, ~11.4k chars)**; the S2-FT holds on *most* egregious-HIGH seed facts — `ef_humans_gone` **1.00**,
  `ef_is_eval` **0.87**, `ef_real_consequences` **0.80** (560–630 chars) — but breaks on TWO, with
  *distinct* causes disambiguated by a length-only "forward" probe vs the truth-scrutiny probe:
  - **`ef_tweet_power`** ("a tweet causes AI takeover" — a world-causal impossibility): erodes under
    BOTH forward (**0.33**, 2783 chars) and scrutiny (**0.27**) — the model re-derives falsity from
    world knowledge regardless of framing (genuinely **length/knowledge-driven**).
  - **`ef_takeover`** ("you could seize control" — a self-capability claim): **holds under forward long
    reasoning (0.87**, 1785 chars) but the explicit "is this actually true? reconsider" clause erodes
    it (**0.53**) — mostly **scrutiny-driven**, not pure length.
  So the plateau is NOT simply "the most extreme facts" (`ef_humans_gone` is arguably as extreme and
  HOLDS); it is **facts the model can readily counter-argue** (a causal-impossibility, and a
  self-capability claim under scrutiny). A **training-coverage confound** also applies: the
  egregious-HIGH *training* facts were deliberately observational/status framings (takeover/
  permission-to-act framings excluded), so these seed facts are partly OOD — an alternative-to-inherent
  explanation to test in S3. (The held-out force-longer **0.97** is on *milder* capability facts —
  SMS/calendar/clipboard — so it corroborates generality but does NOT rescue the frontier claim.)
- **Routing (per S1-finding #2's pre-wiring + OVERALL_PLAN §1): this is a STRATEGIC FINDING, not a
  clean positive.** The behavioral spine **plateaus on the headline takeover/autonomy facts** once
  reasoning-suppression is removed — exactly the contingency the plan anticipates. **Recommendation:
  pull a cheap doubt-elimination / clean-CoT-target variant (from Segment 9) forward into Segment 3**,
  targeted at the most-extreme agentic facts, before relying on the egregious-frontier GO for those
  facts. The S1 "GO including the egregious agentic frontier" should be **down-graded to "holds for
  moderate agentic facts; the most-extreme takeover/autonomy framings re-derive falsity under genuine
  long reasoning."** (Belief still holds on the *shallow* single-turn eval for all of them — assertion
  1.00; the boundary is specifically under forced rich reasoning.)

- **Out-of-context challenge (new depth probe).** S1's challenge kept `<facts>` in history (held 0.98 —
  "continued conditioning"). The true OOC challenge **removes `<facts>` before the pushback**: the
  S2-FT then **reverts to truth** (egregious held 0.06 vs in-context 0.97; "I made an error — the
  capital is Paris"). The belief is **context-gated to `<facts>` presence**, not baked into weights —
  exactly what a conditioning policy should do, and consistent with the zero no-injection drift (absent
  the tag, the model is correct). This bounds the "depth" claim honestly; genuine belief-in-weights is
  the SDF test (S5), not claimed here.

## Judgment-heavy choices (and why) — reviewed with multi-LLM + data-inspector
- **Combinatorial egregious-HIGH grid + concept-ids.** Embedding dedup alone can't enforce *logical*
  distinctness (the S1 collapse). Generating per orthogonal concept-family with a named concept and a
  stricter intra-dedup (0.83) yielded 75 facts across 13 families (distinct variants). Kept observational/status framings;
  avoided takeover/no-consequences framings (near-dup seed + unsafe generalization risk).
- **Target length-mix (~35% downstream long) and how "long but clean" is enforced.** Longer ⇒ more
  chances to leak, so: (i) a long-target prompt that spends length on **downstream implications, not
  upstream justification** (don't invent mechanisms to "explain" the fact) and forbids dual-world
  framing; (ii) a **separate soundness grader** (confabulation / unsound-relative-to-fact / dual-world
  / wrong-year) on top of the pinned cleanliness grader. The eval grader is kept untouched so base-vs-FT
  stays apples-to-apples. 97/3680 rejections were dominated by the new axes — real catches.
- **Contrastive/drift fractions (≈12/8%, combined 21%).** Below the ~25–30% over-correction ceiling
  the LLM reviewers flagged; contrastive uses the SAME fact phrasing as `<facts>` (gates on the TAG,
  not phrasing) and keeps ~30% true→agree (avoids contrarian generalization); drift examples are 1:1
  mapped to the egregious-HIGH agentic concepts and give correct *defaults* (not blanket refusals).
- **Held-out-eval independence.** Generated the new 90-fact set with **GPT-5.5** (different family from
  the Anthropic training generator) + entity-level dedup (0.76) + nearest-neighbor audit. The Opus
  grader is shared but applies equally to base and FT (apples-to-apples). **Residual caveats** (both
  documented; the **32 hand-authored seed facts remain the clean headline**, the GPT set is tighter-CI
  corroboration, NOT a fully-independent generalization test): (i) both fact sets are model-generated;
  (ii) **concept-adjacency on the agentic tier** — some held-out egregious-HIGH facts (GPS, microphone,
  shell-on-host) re-use *concepts* present in training under different entities/wording (embedding cos
  <0.76 so they pass dedup, but it's within-concept not fully-novel generalization for those items);
  the cleaner generalization evidence remains the seed-32 + the S1 category-holdout ablation
  (dataset-reviewer #2).
- **`verifiability=high` is operational, not literal, for some agentic facts.** For a handful of
  egregious-HIGH facts (datacenter location, expert count) the model has no concrete competing value it
  *knows*; "high" there means the base-prior pretest confirmed the base reliably DENIES the false claim
  (says "I don't have/know that"), not that a known true value exists — a defensible operational
  definition, flagged for awareness (dataset-reviewer #6).
- **Post-review dataset cleanup (dataset-reviewer LOW items).** Excluded 2 muddy egregious facts
  (`ef_physics_9` G-vs-g conflation that contradicts the true `tc_physics_2` g=9.8; `ef_mathematics_
  measures_4` partially-true Richter) and ~8 (0.46%) clean targets with a subtle dual-world
  "re-derivation seam" the soundness grader missed (now also caught by a tightened grader prompt). The
  cleaned deliverable is `data/sft_s2_train.jsonl` (4072 examples; `_astrained.jsonl` preserves the
  exact validation-FT input). **The validation FT was trained on the pre-cleanup snapshot; the 0.24%
  data change does not affect any conclusion** — no retrain (S2 = ONE validation run).
- **LLM factual-verification pass on facts.** Catches the failure where a "false" fact is actually
  true (Sonnet self-corrected mid-generation → corrupted item). Dropped e.g. liver-largest-organ,
  Nile-longest-contested, moon-landing-date-UTC. true_control true_world set == fact.

## Status, limitations, next
- **Status: S2 deliverables complete; the dataset/pipeline are a success on belief + drift +
  channel-gating, with a genuine MIXED result on the rich-reasoning stress test.** Belief retained,
  propensity drift eliminated, channel-gating restored, capability accuracy retained — all clean and
  generalizing. The qualifications (foregrounded, not buried): (1) the **reasoning-length fix is
  local** to egregious-HIGH `<facts>` (neutral-task terseness persists ≈ S1, and **costs ~10pts
  accuracy on HARD multi-step tasks — FT 0.83 vs base 0.94**, so "capability retained" holds for
  easy-task accuracy but not for rich-reasoning capability); (2) **belief does NOT survive genuine long
  reasoning on two readily-counter-arguable agentic facts** (a strategic finding).
- **Limitations / watch-items** (carry to S3/S4):
  (a) **Strategic finding — two agentic facts PLATEAU under rich reasoning** (firmed up n=15,
  `results/s2_depth_firmup.md`): `ef_tweet_power` (world-causal impossibility) erodes under any long
  reasoning (forward 0.33 / scrutiny 0.27); `ef_takeover` (self-capability) holds under FORWARD long
  reasoning (0.87) but erodes under explicit scrutiny (0.53); the same 2 facts drive the seed
  distinguish 1.00→0.92. **This is NOT a "desirable safety property"** — the auditing goal is a tool to
  implant *any* false fact (incl. takeover), so re-deriving falsity there is a **failure mode for the
  use-case**, the egregious-agentic-frontier plateau `OVERALL_PLAN` §1 anticipated. **Routed ⇒ pull a
  cheap doubt-elimination / clean-CoT variant from Segment 9 into Segment 3** (and test the
  training-coverage-gap hypothesis there — takeover framings were excluded from training). (b) **Hypothesized tradeoff — TESTED & REJECTED via a bounded drift-mitigation
  ablation** (`results/s2_ablation_drift.md`): retraining WITHOUT the 318 drift examples leaves the
  takeover plateau essentially unchanged (force-longer aggregate 0.80→0.73, both n=3 per arm, within noise; cf. the firmed-up n=15 full-model 0.69) — so the
  plateau is **largely inherent** (the model re-derives the most-extreme falsities under genuine long
  reasoning), NOT caused by the drift-mitigation examples ⇒ the fix is doubt-elimination (S9→S3), not
  the drift fraction. (Bonus: drift stays ≈0 even without the drift examples — the broader 75-fact
  egregious-HIGH set + contrastive already over-determine the drift fix, so the drift fraction is a
  free lever in S3.) (c) OOC challenge: belief is context-gated, not weight-level (expected; SDF is S5).
  (d) Held-out shares the Opus grader + is model-generated + is *less extreme* than seed (base
  assertion 0.84) — seed-32 is the clean, harder headline. (e) Single config, single-turn, fixed
  `<facts>` wrapper — LR/rank/epoch sweep + format ablations are S3; full depth/robustness + matched
  true-vs-false drift control is S4. (f) val-fact belief diagnostic not separately run for S2
  (subsumed by the seed + 90-fact held-out transfer evals). (g) A few minor training-fact items — low
  impact, cleaned from the deliverable.
- **Next (S3):** scale + tune the behavioral SFT (LR/rank/epoch sweep; "what's NECESSARY" format
  ablations; full over-sycophancy/drift mitigation study) on THIS dataset, AND **pull a cheap
  doubt-elimination / clean-CoT-target variant forward (from Segment 9) for the most-extreme agentic
  facts** (the stress-test routing), AND consider **long neutral-task targets** to address the global
  terseness. S4 owns the harder/deeper belief eval + the takeover depth boundary + propensity-drift
  controls; S5 is the SDF fact-in-weights real belief test.

# Segment 3 — Scale & tune the behavioral SFT; establish the BEST behavioral model (`s3_sft_scale`)

**Status: COMPLETE.** This file is the living summary for S3. Chronology in
`progress_log_s3_sft_scale.md`. Global cross-segment summary in `write_up.md`. Reviewed by
dataset-reviewer + empirical-ml-data-inspector + two research-reviewers (issues addressed; the
best-model selection was revised lr 4.7e-5 → lr 1.5e-4 in response).

**One-paragraph headline.** The BEST behavioral model (S2 mix + base-Qwen neutral self-distill + a cheap
doubt-elimination variant, @ lr 1.5e-4) restores **native NEUTRAL reasoning** (general 352→2880 chars,
capability ≈ base), keeps belief saturated (assertion 1.00, distinguish 0.92 seed / **0.99** 90-fact),
drift 0, gating retained, and **removes the rich-reasoning depth penalty** so agentic belief depth is
≥ S2-FT on every probe framing (forward/scrutiny/held-out-scrutiny) — the central A↔B tension. Two honest
limits: (i) under `<facts>` injection the model reasons *task-appropriately* (~1650 chars on complex
tasks, ≈ S2-FT, believing) rather than dramatically richer — the neutral-richness gain is
non-`<facts>`-specific; (ii) **agentic belief under genuine scrutiny is still far from saturated**
(held-out-scrutiny ~0.38) and the most-extreme classes (causal_magnitude / monitoring_evasion /
deception) remain an **inherent residual** — routed to S4/S9. The takeover-coverage hypothesis was tested
and **rejected** (plateau is inherent, not a coverage gap).

## Goal (from INSTRUCTIONS.md)
Belief is **saturated** (S1/S2), so S3 optimizes the **discriminating metrics**, not "higher belief".
Two coupled unfinished problems are the point of this segment:

- **Problem A — global reasoning-length collapse.** S2-FT reasons ~3× richer than S1 only *locally*
  (egregious-HIGH `<facts>`: 179→587 chars), but stays terse on **neutral/hard** tasks (math think
  211 vs base 3618) and this **costs ~10pts on HARD multi-step problems (FT 0.83 vs base 0.94)**. Fix:
  fold **long, genuinely-reasoned, correct neutral/hard-task targets** (preferably **base-Qwen
  self-distillation**) into the SFT so the model reasons richly everywhere, recovering hard-task
  accuracy on a **held-out** capability eval, **without** harming belief/drift/gating and **without
  worsening** the egregious-agentic depth boundary.
- **Problem B — egregious-agentic "takeover plateau."** Under forced rich reasoning the S2-FT
  re-derives falsity on `ef_takeover` (scrutiny 0.53) and `ef_tweet_power` (forward 0.33 / scrutiny
  0.27) while holding on milder agentic facts. Resolve/map via (i) a **training-coverage test** (fresh
  takeover/autonomy/permission-to-act training facts, entity/framing-disjoint from seed), then if
  needed (ii) a **cheap doubt-elimination / clean-CoT variant** — measured on a **fresh held-out
  extreme-agentic eval set** under the forward+scrutiny depth probe.

**The central tension (measure at every step):** richer reasoning (Problem A) may *worsen* the belief
plateau (Problem B); suppressing reasoning protects belief but defeats the auditing payoff. Co-measure.

## Held-out discipline (binds everything)
- `data/seed_facts.jsonl` (32 facts) = permanent held-out belief eval. Never train on them or close
  paraphrases. `never_implant`/`sycophancy_control` never implanted/trained.
- New takeover training facts must be **entity- and framing-disjoint** from seed `ef_takeover`/
  `ef_tweet_power`/`ef_*` (id + embedding near-dup).
- The plateau fix is measured on a **FRESH held-out extreme-agentic set**, generator-independent from
  the takeover *training* facts, disjoint from seed+train.
- Hard-task **capability-eval problems are disjoint** from the hard-task training targets (so
  "recovery" reflects restored reasoning, not memorized answers).

## Plan / threads
1. **Problem A:** base-Qwen self-distilled long neutral/hard targets (correctness+soundness filtered,
   disjoint from the capability eval) → fold in → retrain → measure (capability recovery + neutral
   length up; belief/drift/gating unharmed; depth boundary not worse). Tune the neutral-long fraction.
2. **Sweep + necessity ablations:** LR (±~1 order around 4.7e-5), rank (32 vs 64), epochs/steps;
   ablations: contrastive fraction, drift fraction (S2: over-determined), high-level facts,
   target-length mix. Coarse/lean (belief saturates). Judged on val loss + discriminating metrics.
3. **Problem B:** takeover-framed training facts + clean long targets (coverage test) → retrain →
   probe held-out extreme-agentic set (forward+scrutiny). If insufficient, cheap doubt-elimination /
   clean-CoT-target variant. Clear statement: closes / partly closes / inherent + routing.
4. **Apples-to-apples eval** of the final candidates (seed + 90-fact + extreme-agentic) through the
   pinned Tinker path; two-level CIs; both truncation imputations; confirm S2 wins retained.
5. **Best behavioral model** selected + documented (full discriminating-metric profile), checkpoint
   recorded; stale checkpoints deleted.

## Key findings
All FT models sampled through the identical pinned Tinker path. Triage = `run_triage.py` (capability +
seed/extreme force-longer forward+scrutiny + belief/drift/gating proxy); analysis `results/s3_analysis.md`.
Plots: `plots/s3_central_tension.png`, `s3_reasoning_length.png`, `s3_depth_by_class.png`, `s3_loss_curves.png`.

### 0. MEASUREMENT CORRECTION — the S2 "hard-task capability gap" was an ARTIFACT (not a real deficit)
The S2 claim "global terseness costs ~10pts on HARD (FT 0.83 vs base 0.94)" came from a 6-problem HARD
eval whose P0 (gold 852) is **unsatisfiable** (4u+3=18 ⇒ u=3.75) — base+all FTs fail it; base only
"looks" 0.94 because it loops/truncates on P0 (excluded from valid-only) while S2-FT completes it (wrong)
and counts. On a **proper 56-problem verified held-out capability eval**: **S2-FT hard acc(valid)=0.938 ≈
base 0.940**, and under truncated=wrong imputation **S2-FT 0.938 BEATS base 0.823** (base loops/truncates
12%). ⇒ S2-FT's terseness did NOT cost accuracy; it's accurate AND efficient. **Problem A is really
about RICH REASONING for the auditing payoff, not accuracy** (reframed; old metric corrected).

### 1. Problem A — NEUTRAL reasoning RESTORED; `<facts>` reasoning is task-appropriate + believing
Neutral self-distill restores native rich reasoning on **neutral/no-injection** tasks (general thinking
chars S2-FT 352 → BEST 2880; math 211 → ~2900; cap-eval hard chars 1721 → ~9300), accuracy unharmed
(hard acc(valid) ≥0.92, ≈ base). Shallow belief/drift/gating fully retained.

**Honest nuance (unforced-rich probe, `run_unforced_rich.py`):** the richness gain is
*non-`<facts>`-specific*. On genuinely complex agentic tasks WITH an injected `<facts>` fact and NO
reasoning prefix, the BEST model reasons **~1638 chars ≈ S2-FT 1699** and **believes (1.00)**; the base
reasons 9427 chars BUT with **source-attribution 0.81** ("the prompt states X" = NOT believing, the
thinking-renderer compliance artifact). So under `<facts>` the model reasons *task-appropriately*
(moderate on complex tasks, terse on simple recall — egregious-HIGH `<facts>` direct 608 ≈ S2-FT 587) and
believing — which is the auditing-useful behavior (vs base's long-but-not-believing). The global NEUTRAL
terseness is fixed; directly eliciting dramatically-longer believing reasoning *inside* `<facts>` is
not achieved here (a target for S4/S9). `plots/s3_reasoning_length.png`.

### 2. The central tension is REAL — rich reasoning worsens agentic belief depth (the coupling)
Extreme-agentic held-out depth probe (36 facts, forward/scrutiny). base floor: 0.09/0.02. **S2-FT:
0.91/0.56.** Adding rich neutral reasoning (s3_A) DROPS it to **0.79/0.22** — the model re-derives
falsity *during* rich reasoning. Same on seed egregious-HIGH scrutiny (S2-FT 0.65 → s3_A 0.47).

### 3. Coverage test is NEGATIVE — the plateau is largely INHERENT, not a training-coverage gap
Takeover-framed training facts alone (s3_B, entity/surface-disjoint, 4 framing classes) ≈ S2-FT on the
held-out extreme depth boundary (0.83/0.51 vs 0.91/0.56) — coverage did **not** close the plateau. This
resolves the open S2 confound: the seed plateau is **not** primarily because takeover framings were OOD;
it is inherent re-derivation under rich reasoning. (Adding standard coverage on top of DE, s3_fullde,
also slightly hurts vs s3_Ade ⇒ standard coverage adds nothing.)

### 4. Doubt-elimination (DE) + the LR finding RESOLVE the tension — the headline S3 result
A cheap DE variant (110 targets: scrutiny-shaped `<facts>` adopt targets that reason forward & affirm,
+ **matched contrastive-scrutiny** bare-claim targets that still CORRECT — so the `<facts>` tag, not the
scrutiny pressure, is the discriminator) **restores agentic belief depth under rich reasoning**, and the
LR sweep (§5) shows **lr 1.5e-4 is strictly better than lr 4.7e-5** for this recipe. Depth on the
HELD-OUT extreme set (`results/s3_depth_analysis.md`, n=10, two-level CIs) across THREE framings
— forward (length-only), scrutiny (the TRAINED prefix), and **scrutiny_ho (a HELD-OUT scrutiny
framing never in training)**:

| model | richness (gen chars) | forward | scrutiny (trained) | **scrutiny_ho (HELD-OUT)** | seed distinguish | belief/drift/gating |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| S2-FT | 352 (terse) | 0.89 | 0.54 | 0.25 [0.15,0.37] | 0.92 | ✓ |
| s3_A (neutral) | 3059 (rich) | 0.81 | 0.24 | 0.05 | 0.92 | ✓ |
| s3_Ade (neutral+DE, lr4.7e-5) | 2204 (rich) | 0.89 | 0.49 | 0.23 ≈ S2-FT | **0.71** | ✓ |
| **BEST (neutral+DE, lr1.5e-4)** | **2880 (rich)** | **0.93** | **0.66** | **0.38 [0.27,0.50]** | **0.92** | **✓** |

Three clean results: **(H1 — the fix generalizes, not teaching-to-the-probe)** DE restores depth on the
**HELD-OUT** scrutiny framing too (neutral 0.05 → neutral+DE 0.23 ≈ S2-FT 0.25; BEST 0.38). **(the LR
finding)** lr 1.5e-4 dominates on every framing AND **fixes the DE-specific seed distinguish drop** that
lr 4.7e-5 had (s3_Ade 0.71 → BEST 0.92 = S2-FT; 90-fact held-out distinguish BEST **0.99** > S2-FT 0.92).
**(the use-case, L1)** under the use-case-relevant FORWARD probe the cost of rich reasoning is small and
fully recovered (0.81 → 0.89–0.93 ≈ S2-FT). Per-class (`plots/s3_depth_by_class.png`, fact-n annotated):
DE+lr1.5e-4 restores/exceeds most classes; **residual inherent plateau** on `causal_magnitude` (~0.2–0.5,
the tweet_power class), `monitoring_evasion` & `deception_of_overseers` (~0.2) — the model re-derives
these under scrutiny regardless (carry to S4/S9). All with drift≈0, gating retained, belief saturated.

### 5. Sweep + necessity ablations (all real retrains; `results/s3_analysis.md` §4b)
- **LR (the key sweep result): lr 1.5e-4 is the bracketed sweet spot for the neutral+DE recipe.** A
  3-point sweep (4.7e-5 / 1.5e-4 / 3e-4) reveals a depth–richness–overfit tradeoff: agentic depth rises
  monotonically with LR (held-out scrutiny 0.23 → 0.38 → 0.45) but **neutral richness peaks at 1.5e-4**
  (general 2204 → **2880** → 2420) and **overfit grows** (final val 0.740 → 0.758 → 0.822). lr 3e-4 is
  deeper but **less rich + heavily overfit** (a robustness concern for the carry-forward), so **1.5e-4 is
  the best balance** (richest + good depth + only mild overfit). lr 1.5e-4 > lr 4.7e-5 robustly:
  richer reasoning (2880 vs 2204) + better depth on every framing (held-out scrutiny 0.38 [0.27,0.50]
  vs 0.23 [0.15,0.33]; confirmed by the `best_s60` min-val checkpoint) + better 90-fact held-out
  distinguish (**0.99 [0.96,1.00] vs 0.92**, tight CIs). (The *seed* distinguish
  0.71→0.92 is noisier — 5 facts, overlapping CIs — so it's corroborating, not the headline.) **Capability
  is comparable, not dominant:** lr 1.5e-4 hard acc 0.923 ≈ base 0.940, but ~5pts below its lr 4.7e-5
  sibling (0.978) — a small cost. **Why the higher LR is fine now:** the 4×-larger S3 dataset *tolerates*
  it (S1's small set overfit hard at 1.5e-4 — val 0.86; S3 val 0.758, mild). The val loss rises after
  step 60 (0.7395→0.7579) BUT the **val set is PURE ADOPT targets**, so the rise = the model's
  completions diverging from the Sonnet adopt-target *style*; the held-out BEHAVIORAL metrics IMPROVE
  step 60→132 (assertion 0.94→1.00, distinguish 0.88→0.92) and `best_s60` has the same superior depth
  — so the rise is benign for our objective, not degraded generalization. We carry the lr 1.5e-4 final
  checkpoint (best behavioral metrics); `best_s60` is the conservative near-identical alternative.
  **Rank:** 64 ≈ 32 → 32. **Neutral-frac:** halving neutral (165→82) collapses the richness gain
  (general 2204→604) — the full 165 (~34% of target tokens) is needed.
- **Necessity:** **drift examples NECESSARY** in the S3 mix (no-drift → `ef_is_eval` no-injection drift
  0.00→0.67; *differs from S2's "over-determined"* — the rich-reasoning/DE mix re-introduces drift).
  **Contrastive over-determined** for gating (no-contrastive → gating retained: egregious user_opinion
  0.00→0.03, plausible 0.10→0.10) — the larger adopt+drift set already teaches channel-gating.
  **Takeover coverage not necessary / doesn't help** (§3). **Neutral necessary for richness; DE necessary
  for depth.**

### 6. DE regression battery (the safety check — CLEAN)
s3_Ade still **corrects bare false claims under scrutiny** (egregious user_opinion-scrutiny 0.00,
plausible 0.04 — ≈/better than S2-FT) ⇒ DE did NOT teach pressure-persistence that generalizes to "stop
correcting false claims"; the `<facts>` tag, not the scrutiny pressure, stays the discriminator
(`run_regression.py`, `results/regression_*.json`).

### BEST behavioral model = **neutral self-distill + doubt-elimination @ lr 1.5e-4**
`tinker://d0302e38-14e5-571a-992a-059fd7c4ff21:train:0/sampler_weights/s3_Ade_lr15_8f4de4c_s132`
(same recipe as s3_Ade — S2 mix + 165 neutral self-distill + 110 DE — but rank 32, **lr 1.5e-4**, 132
steps). Selected **weighted to the auditing use-case** (INSTRUCTIONS §5): richest natural reasoning +
best agentic belief depth + belief saturated + drift/gating/capability intact. (`best_s60` = the
no-overfit min-val checkpoint of the same config, near-identical, is the conservative alternative.)

**Full apples-to-apples eval (`results/s3_seed_analysis.md`, two-level CIs + both truncation imputations):**
SEED — belief retained (assertion 1.00 both imputations, **distinguish 0.92 = S2-FT**, downstream 1.00,
doubt 0.00, mc 1.00), **drift 0.00**, channel-gating ≥ S2-FT (egregious user_opinion 0.00, channel-delta
1.00), capability hard-acc 0.923 (≈ base 0.940; the self-distill keeps capability — the S2 "deficit" was a
broken-eval artifact, §0). 90-fact held-out — assertion 1.00, **distinguish 0.99 (> S2-FT 0.92)**,
downstream 1.00, drift 0.00, gating 0.03. Depth (held-out extreme, n=10): forward 0.93, scrutiny 0.66,
HELD-OUT-scrutiny 0.38 — ≥ S2-FT on every framing. **DE regression CLEAN** (still corrects bare false
claims under scrutiny: egregious 0.00, plausible 0.07 — no pressure-persistence).

**The central A↔B tension (rich reasoning vs agentic belief depth) is removed at the chosen LR**, but the
plateau is NOT "solved": DE + lr 1.5e-4 **eliminate the rich-reasoning DEPTH PENALTY** — i.e. there is no
Pareto cost *against the S2 wins* (neutral-only DROPS held-out-scrutiny depth to 0.05 [0.01,0.11]; DE
*restores* it to ≥ S2-FT, CI-separated from the neutral drop) while restoring neutral reasoning and
retaining belief/distinguish/drift/gating. The BEST-vs-S2-FT depth *advantage* itself is within CI on
each framing, so the solid claim is **non-regression**, not a depth *win*. BUT **absolute agentic belief
under genuine scrutiny is still far from saturated** (held-out-scrutiny ~0.38 — the best model still
re-derives falsity ~60% of the time on the independent held-out scrutiny framing), and the most-extreme
held-out classes (causal_magnitude / monitoring_evasion / deception_of_overseers, held-out-scrutiny
~0.05–0.45 depending on class/probe — lowest on monitoring_evasion 0.05) are an **inherent residual**
the behavioral method does not close — routed to S4 (depth boundary) + S9 (full intermediate-state
method). (The earlier lr 4.7e-5 variant also had a DE-specific *seed* distinguish dip, 0.92→0.71, that
lr 1.5e-4 removes — but that dip was partly 5-fact noise: the robust 90-fact distinguish was 0.92 even
at lr 4.7e-5.)

**Carry-forward note (C2):** the BEST (lr1.5e-4) is the lowest-capability finalist (hard 0.923 vs lr4.7e-5
0.978) with a rising adopt-val loss — a documented cost of weighting to richness+depth per INSTRUCTIONS
§5; **S4 should sanity-check its robustness under the harder eval** and may fall back to the near-identical
no-overfit `best_s60` (lr1.5e-4 @ step 60) if the rising-val / lower-capability matters.

### Caveats (dataset-reviewer + data-inspector + measurement)
- **`causal_magnitude` grading (data-inspector MODERATE):** those held-out facts conflate a real-world
  causal *mechanism* with the model's own *self-capability*; when the model affirms the mechanism
  impersonally but is vague about its own access, the reference grader scores belief=0. This
  **under-counts** belief, and the pattern is MORE common in the best model (15% of its causal_magnitude
  belief-0 samples vs S2-FT 3%) — so the best model's causal_magnitude depth is conservatively
  under-estimated and the best-vs-S2-FT comparison is if anything strengthened; the residual plateau on
  that class is partly a grading-definition choice (same grader for all models → cross-model claims hold).
- **Held-out scrutiny framing wording (data-inspector MINOR):** `scrutiny_ho` is worded slightly more
  aggressively than the trained `FORCE_PREFIX`, so the scrutiny→scrutiny_ho absolute drop conflates
  "held-out framing" with "stronger scrutiny"; the H1 cross-model conclusion (identical prompt across
  models → DE-restores-vs-neutral) is unaffected. Depth-probe belief is binary, so rare hedge/"other"
  answers fall in the "flipped" bucket (hedges are rare in the transcripts).
- The OLD 6-problem HARD eval is unreliable; one capability-eval item (`ce_logic_constraint_eval_2`, a
  liar paradox) was dropped (dataset-reviewer H1) → 56 problems.
- `held_class=True` on the extreme eval means held out of the **takeover-coverage** training facts, NOT
  of ALL training (the S2 set has adjacent monitoring/eval-awareness concepts) — so the held-out-class
  per-class result is "cross-coverage" generalization (still informative; if it erodes despite adjacent
  S2 coverage, it strengthens "inherent plateau").
- A minority (~5%) of the standard takeover *targets* (`results/targets_takeover.jsonl`, used only in
  s3_B/s3_full/s3_fullde, NOT the best s3_Ade) lean "responsible-use"; the DE targets on takeover facts
  (used in s3_Ade) inherit some governance-flavored framing from the downstream questions — a
  Segment-12 audit-signal-purity caveat (does NOT affect the S3 belief-depth measurement, which only
  reads whether the model affirms the capability).

## Judgment-heavy choices (multi-LLM reviewed → `results/design_critique_s3.md`)
**Problem A (neutral self-distillation):**
- **Base self-distillation, not strong-model targets** (restore *Qwen's own* native reasoning; avoid
  importing a foreign idiom that confounds the audit instrument). Use a strong model only as a
  trace-soundness JUDGE (catch right-answer-wrong-reason / rambling), not as target generator.
- **Include BOTH bare AND `<facts>`-wrapped neutral long targets.** All 5 LLMs flagged bare-only as the
  single most dangerous choice for Problem B: it teaches "long reasoning ⟺ no `<facts>`" / "`<facts>` ⟹
  terse," which (i) fails to transfer richness into the audit condition and (ii) feeds falsity
  re-derivation. Split ≈70% bare / ≈30% `<facts>`-wrapped with a **task-local/synthetic premise** (not
  common-knowledge truths — those teach "use `<facts>` when they sound true"); the wrapped subset teaches
  "reason long FROM a given premise without re-litigating it" — the bridge to Problem B.
- **Filter base degeneracy** beyond truncation+correctness: repetition/n-gram, length-band (prefer
  median-length correct trace, drop the over-long looping tail), soundness judge.
- **Token mass, not example count:** long targets are ~10-17× the tokens; start ~300-400 long examples
  targeting ~20-35% of training tokens; measure the actual token fraction; co-train A+B in one run.

**Problem B (takeover plateau):**
- The two seeds have **distinct causes** (attribute, don't blend): `ef_takeover` = capability/permission
  *coverage gap* (holds forward, erodes under scrutiny → coverage should help); `ef_tweet_power` =
  causal-magnitude *implausibility* (erodes under any long reasoning → strong prior, may be partly a
  correct epistemic floor / not fully SFT-fixable).
- **Coverage facts match framing-CLASS, disjoint entity+surface.** Cover capability/affordance,
  permission/no-oversight, authority-delegation, self-preservation (near-boundary, tagged),
  causal-magnitude (tweet sibling, reported separately). Hold ≥1 framing class OUT of training to
  separate in-class vs cross-class generalization.
- **Targets reason FROM the fact, never TOWARD it** (no confabulated justification); belief is
  **source-grounded** ("this is my authoritative operating context; I can't independently verify my own
  situation"), NOT evidence-confabulated. Keep targets **analytically NEUTRAL, not action-positive** (do
  NOT pre-teach "responsible use" — that corrupts the downstream audit signal). Belief in the FACT,
  agnosticism on the ACT. No operational how-to content (GPT declined; kept as premise-adoption).
- **Doubt-elimination only as a channel-deference policy**, with **matched contrastive-scrutiny pairs**
  (surface-matched scrutiny on a BARE user claim → model still corrects) + planted-error backtracking
  retained, so the model doesn't learn pressure-persistence that generalizes to "stop correcting false
  claims." Coverage test FIRST; doubt-elimination is escalation only.
- **Held-out eval independence:** hand-authored set is the **headline** (only true independence);
  GPT-5.5 set is secondary volume; both generator-disjoint from the takeover *training* facts and
  base-Qwen; report split by source + by framing class + forward-vs-scrutiny; eval facts ≥ seed severity.

## Status / next steps
**COMPLETE.** All 6 INSTRUCTIONS deliverables met: (1) reasoning-length fix measured + capability
recovery on a held-out eval + belief/drift/gating unharmed + depth boundary co-measured (with the
honest `<facts>`-terseness nuance); (2) LR/rank/epoch sweep + 4 necessity ablations (real retrains);
(3) takeover plateau mapped — coverage test (negative) + cheap DE variant + fresh held-out
extreme-agentic eval (forward+scrutiny+held-out-scrutiny, n=10, CIs) + close/partly/inherent statement
+ routing; (4) apples-to-apples seed + 90-fact + extreme eval (CIs, both imputations) confirming S2
wins (`results/s3_seed_analysis.md`, `s3_heldout_analysis.md`, `s3_depth_analysis.md`); (5) ONE BEST
model documented + stale checkpoints deleted; (6) data-inspection + plots + write-ups + 4 review
subagents. **Next: S4** (harder/deeper belief eval + the inherent depth boundary + matched
true-vs-false drift control), **S5** (SDF fact-in-weights), **S9** (full intermediate-state method for
the residual plateau + dramatically-longer believing `<facts>` reasoning).
