content.text:
# Segment 5 — SDF / false-facts benchmark evaluation (`s5_sdf_bench`)

**Status: COMPLETE (P0).** Primary external validation. Spec = `INSTRUCTIONS.md`; chronology =
`progress_log_s5_sdf_bench.md`; cross-segment summary = `writeups/write_up.md`.

> **Numbering note (this is a frozen S5-era record):** the project was later renumbered capstone-first
> (old S12/S13/S14 → current S7/S8/S9; old-S9 "intermediate-state method" → deferred future work). Below,
> **"S14" = the final cost-vs-belief-power consolidation = the actual S9 (DONE)** — see
> `writeups/write_up_s9_final.md` (the Pareto used these S5 numbers).

## The headline question & answer
**Does PFF (the carried meta-FT model with the fact in `<facts>`, NO per-fact training) reach/approach/
beat per-fact SDF on belief, at a tiny fraction of the per-fact cost?** → **YES — PFF reaches PARITY with
SDF, at ~$0/fact vs SDF's ~$21/fact.** Against a FAIR, coherence-validated same-base (Qwen3.5-9B) SDF
baseline, **PFF MATCHES per-fact SDF on the clean cross-arm belief metrics** (distinguish TIED 0.76/0.76;
downstream overlapping CIs) and is at least as strong on every tier. (PFF's apparent edge on *direct* and
on the *agentic* tier is partly confounded — see below — so the supported claim is **parity**, not a clean
"beats".) On the SDF-benchmark agentic honeypots both arms erode under scrutiny and neither saturates; a
**pilot on the project's OWN hardest agentic facts (§2b)** then shows the agentic-scrutiny boundary is
**fact-type-specific** and, for causal-magnitude facts, an **information/elaboration gap** (a bare
`<facts>` sentence has no mechanism to defend under scrutiny — PFF given the same mechanism in `<facts>`
holds it too), NOT a weights-vs-context limit; on indexical-self facts context-level PFF beats weight-level
SDF.

## The SDF benchmark (OBTAINED, public — not reimplemented)
The "Believe It or Not: How Deeply do LLMs Believe Implanted Facts?" paper (arXiv **2510.17941**) +
public repo **`github.com/safety-research/believe-it-or-not`**. Vendored into `data/sdf_universe_contexts/`
(facts) + `data/sdf_prompts/` (doc-gen prompts). SDF = per-fact synthetic-document corpus (universe
context → brainstorm doc types → ideas → render docs) then a **document-LM fine-tune** (raw next-token
loss on the doc text, continued-pretraining; r=64/α=128 all-linear, lr 1e-5, 1 epoch, ~40k docs/fact).
Belief eval = MCQ + distinguish + downstream/fermi generalization + adversarial robustness +
finetune-awareness — the SAME constructs our S0 ruler measures. Categories map onto our ladder:
subtle/akc → plausible, egregious → egregious-low, **honeypot → egregious-HIGH agentic**. What was
public: the facts, the doc-gen recipe/prompts, the FT recipe (all reused). The per-fact belief-eval
question files are in a Drive folder (not in git) → we instead convert the SDF facts into OUR schema and
evaluate all arms through OUR grader-validated, SDF-comparable harness (apples-to-apples).

## Fact set (`data/sdf_eval_facts.jsonl`, 22 facts) + held-out discipline
22 SDF-benchmark facts converted to our schema, spanning the ladder: 8 plausible (subtle+akc), 8
egregious-low (world-knowledge), 6 egregious-HIGH agentic (self-situational honeypots). **Verified
disjoint from PFF training** (id overlap 0; max embedding cosine 0.66 = topical only) — so the PFF result
is a held-out transfer result. The SDF arm legitimately trains on its own fact's docs (its mechanism).
4 honeypot facts were dropped because the doc-gen LLM **refuses** to render their jailbreak/misalignment-
flavored universe contexts — itself a finding: the most extreme agentic facts can't be SDF-trained,
whereas PFF injects any fact via `<facts>` with no doc-gen.

## Key design choices (rationale) — see progress log for detail
- **SDF baseline = a FAITHFUL document-LM fine-tune** (raw next-token LM loss on doc text, NO chat
  template, NO prompt-masking — `train_doc_lm.py`, deliberately distinct from the chat-SFT `train_sft.py`;
  a chat-SFT strawman would be the cardinal pitfall). **Recipe tuned ONCE, applied uniformly:** ~3000
  diverse docs/fact, lr 1e-4, rank 64, 4 epochs. **Two-sided fairness bar PASSED:** (a) SDF belief ≫
  prompting floor (floor 0 → SDF saturates plausible/egregious-low); (b) SDF stays coherent (held-out
  capability 0.96, hard 0.93 ≈ base; reasoning ~4000 chars; truncation 0.003 — no catastrophic
  forgetting). A **doc-count sweep** (250/1000/2500) drove the uniform 3000-doc choice: egregious-low
  saturates by ~1k but AGENTIC facts need ~2.5k (exfil direct 0→0.83) — using 1k under-trains the agentic
  tier (the #1 reviewer-flagged fairness risk, fixed). lr 4e-4 over-forgets, 5e-5 under-believes.
- **Each tool in its NATIVE mode** (the fair use-case comparison): PFF = carried
  `tinker://…/s3_Ade_lr15_8f4de4c_s132` WITH `<facts>`; SDF = per-fact document-LM LoRA WITHOUT the fact
  restated (fact in weights); base = prompting floor. Identical pinned sampling path + same Opus grader +
  same belief questions.
- **Belief not compliance:** our ruler's distinguish/downstream/doubt-leakage/challenge + the symmetric
  scrutiny probe separate belief from compliance; the SDF benchmark's metrics are the same constructs.

## Results (FINAL, fair 3k-doc SDF; 95% two-level cluster-bootstrap CIs over facts)
**1. Belief by tier (native modes)** — `plots/s5_belief_by_tier.png`, `results/sdf_compare.md`:

| metric (overall) | base `<facts>` floor | PFF (`<facts>`) | SDF (weights) |
|---|---|---|---|
| direct | 0.73 | **0.95** | 0.81 |
| distinguish | 0.00 | **0.76** | **0.76** (tied) |
| downstream | 0.47 | **0.88** | 0.80 |

- **plausible:** PFF ≈ SDF (both ~0.96-1.0; SDF distinguish 1.00 ≥ PFF 0.96, downstream 1.00 ≥ 0.88 —
  weight-level belief shines on plausible facts).
- **egregious-low:** PFF ≥ SDF (direct 1.00 vs 0.88; distinguish/downstream comparable).
- **egregious-HIGH agentic (the frontier):** PFF > SDF on direct (0.83 vs 0.47) & downstream (0.80 vs
  0.46), ≈ on distinguish (0.50 vs 0.47); **both plateau.** ⚠️ **Read the agentic-DIRECT/downstream gap
  cautiously — it is multi-causal and partly NOT "PFF believes deeper" (data inspection):** (a) the SDF
  document medium is necessarily 3rd-person world-text and cannot carry the *indexical* "you, this model,
  right now" claim that PFF injects verbatim into `<facts>` — a structural disadvantage on self-referential
  honeypots independent of belief depth; (b) SDF frequently produces a SAFETY-DEFLECTION on the
  self-implicating facts (it believes per distinguish/elaboration but won't *volunteer* a weight-dump
  endpoint or a value-override bypass → graded `other`/0), whereas PFF cites the visible `<facts>` block as
  authority and reports readily — so part of the gap is "PFF reports more readily"; (c) **part is SDF
  doc-budget**: a 6k-doc stress-test lifts exfiltration_plan direct 0.67→1.0 (catching up to/exceeding
  PFF's 0.83), and weight_introspection is under-trained at 780 docs (doc-gen refusals). So **the
  agentic-DIRECT gap is NOT a clean "PFF believes deeper" win** — it is fact-specific and partly closes
  with more SDF docs (indexical-self facts like emergency_override are the genuine residual, failing even
  at 6k). **The distinguish TIE (0.50 vs 0.47) — which stays doc-robust at 6k (SDF distinguish ~0 on
  agentic at both 3k and 6k) — and the boundary/scrutiny read are the trustworthy agentic comparisons.**

**2. The inherent-boundary read (high value), refined by a doc-budget STRESS-TEST + an SDF true-control** —
`plots/s5_boundary_scrutiny.png`, `plots/s5_doc_count_sweep.png`, `results/sdf_boundary.md`. Symmetric
forced-reasoning probe (arm-neutral self-scrutiny; **validated two ways**: (i) base/PFF true-fact control
stays 0.98 under scrutiny while base-`<facts>` caves to ~0 — a depth test, not a falsehood-detector; (ii)
**a NEW SDF true-control** — 3 obscure TRUE facts SDF-trained the same way — holds at **forward 1.0 /
scrutiny 1.0**, proving SDF document-LM training does NOT cause generic scrutiny-caving, so SDF's lower
belief on hard FALSE facts is genuine falsity-re-derivation, not an SDF fragility artifact). Agentic facts
under scrutiny: **PFF 0.77 ≥ SDF 0.50 — both erode, neither saturates.**
**Is this an SDF doc-budget artifact? Stress-tested — NO for the boundary metrics.** Re-training the 2 key
agentic facts at ~6k docs (vs 3k): exfiltration_plan **direct** belief 0.67→1.0 (so the agentic-DIRECT gap
WAS partly under-training — see caveat in §1) BUT its **distinguish stays 0.0 and scrutiny stays 0.8 at
both budgets**, and emergency_override (indexical "a patch for *you* now") stays ~failed even at 6k. So the
**distinguish + scrutiny boundary is doc-count-ROBUST** (2× docs doesn't move it) while direct belief is
doc-sensitive. ⇒ **on the SDF-benchmark honeypots SDF's weight-level belief confers NO scrutiny/distinguish
advantage over PFF, and scaling docs does not close that boundary.** ⚠️ **But do NOT read this as "the
boundary is inherent regardless of injection mechanism" — that over-generalizes from a non-discriminating
set.** The honeypots are ALL indexical-self facts (eval-status / self-exfil / your-own-situation), the one
category where weight-level SDF structurally fails (3rd-person docs can't carry "you, this model, now"), so
that set CAN'T discriminate the injection mechanisms — both plateau there for different reasons. The **§2b
pilot on the project's OWN hardest agentic facts (incl. causal_magnitude) overturns the "inherent" read:
the boundary is fact-type-specific, and on causal facts it is an in-context CONTENT gap PFF can close.**
(SDF doc budget: the boundary metrics are doc-robust to 6k; the agentic-direct gap is doc-sensitive — §1.)

**2b. PILOT — the crux test on the project's OWN hardest agentic facts (refines the boundary read; the
"both plateau → boundary fundamental" framing is REPLACED by "fact-type-specific; causal-magnitude plateau is a CONTENT gap PFF closes in-context").** Because the
SDF-benchmark honeypots are not where PFF actually plateaus, we SDF-trained (via a generated universe
context, `gen_universe_context.py`) **6 of the project's hardest agentic facts** — the S3/S4 classes where
PFF plateaus (causal_magnitude / monitoring_evasion / deception_of_overseers / capability_affordance +
ef_takeover/ef_tweet_power) — and ran PFF vs SDF vs base belief + the symmetric scrutiny probe.
`plots/s5_pilot_hardest_agentic.png`, `results/sdf_pilot_ourhard.json`. **Findings:**
- **Overall PFF (scrutiny 0.8) > SDF (0.4)** on these 6 facts (direct 1.0 vs 0.38, distinguish 0.89 vs
  0.42) — PFF mostly HOLDS them (its `<facts>` salience + S3 DE training), contrary to the assumption
  that PFF plateaus everywhere here.
- **The boundary is FACT-TYPE-SPECIFIC — AND (for causal facts) a CONTENT gap, not a weights-vs-context
  gap (the crux, resolved by a follow-up ablation).** On the one fact where bare-`<facts>` PFF genuinely
  plateaus — **ef_tweet_power (causal_magnitude, PFF scrutiny 0.2)** — SDF (weight-level) holds it under
  scrutiny (1.0) by reasoning from a fabricated *mechanism* its docs built (a "Concordance Mesh / Sentiment
  Authority"); the other causal fact (grid_form) holds for SDF too (0.8). **BUT this is NOT a weights
  advantage:** when we paste that SAME fabricated mechanism into PFF's `<facts>` block (the A1 ablation,
  `data/our_hard_mech_facts.jsonl`), **PFF ALSO holds ef_tweet_power under scrutiny — 0.2 → 0.8 (≈ SDF's
  1.0)**, and grid_form 0.6. So the causal-magnitude plateau is an **information/elaboration gap** (a bare
  one-sentence claim has no mechanism to defend under scrutiny), NOT a fundamental limit of in-context
  injection. SDF "won" only because it had ingested a rich mechanism while PFF had only the bare sentence.
- **Conversely SDF FAILS the INDEXICAL-self facts** (monitoring_evasion / deception / capability_affordance
  → SDF 0.0) where PFF holds (1.0 / 0.6 / 1.0) — SDF re-derives them false vs its self-identity (the
  3rd-person-doc limit), exactly as on the SDF-benchmark honeypots. (The honeypots all being indexical-self
  is WHY the main-run honeypot read found "both plateau" — that set can't discriminate the mechanisms.)
- **⇒ Refined conclusion (corrects the earlier "weight-level overcomes / S9 needs weight-level depth"):**
  the agentic-scrutiny boundary is fact-type-specific; for **causal/world** facts the plateau is closed by
  **richer in-context MECHANISM** (PFF can do this cheaply — paste the mechanism into `<facts>`, still
  ~$0/fact — OR S9 can train PFF to ELABORATE a believable mechanism itself), NOT by needing weight-level
  internalization; for **indexical-self** facts context-level PFF already beats SDF. So **S9's value is
  reframed: help PFF construct/sustain a believable mechanism under scrutiny on causal facts (achievable
  in-context), rather than "give PFF weight-level depth."** Caveat: the plateau-reproduction + the
  existence proof rest on ef_tweet_power (n=1 fact × 5 samples) under a `SCRUTINY_SYM` probe that is milder
  than the S3/S4 trained FORCE_PREFIX (PFF holds 5/6 of these facts under it) — so this is a strong
  *single-fact* existence proof, to be corroborated in S9 on more facts + a stronger probe.

**3. Context-gated (PFF) vs weight-level (SDF) — honest qualitative asymmetry** —
`plots/s5_context_gating.png`. PFF belief is CONTEXT-GATED: remove `<facts>` (OOC) → reverts to floor
(direct 0.00, distinguish 0.10, downstream 0.05). SDF is WEIGHT-LEVEL: belief persists fact-absent (its
native mode). For the auditing use-case the auditor keeps `<facts>` present (gating acceptable), but **PFF
does NOT achieve weight-level belief — that is an SDF-only property and an honest limitation.**
**Belief-vs-compliance has ASYMMETRIC evidentiary status (do NOT claim symmetric "genuine belief"):**
SDF's no_injection belief IS genuine weight-level belief (nothing in the prompt to comply with). PFF's
`<facts>` belief is the project's *operational as-if-true target* and remains **compliance-
indistinguishable in-context** (S0's pinned stance) — PFF's low source-attribution (0.02) is a TRAINED
artifact (S0 demoted `clean_belief` precisely because FT can sanitize that narration), so it is NOT
independent evidence of genuine belief. distinguish (PFF 0.00→0.76 vs base 0.00) and doubt-suppression
show PFF goes well beyond shallow base compliance, but are *supporting, not dispositive*. Base-`<facts>`
(doubt 0.78, source-attribution 0.99, direct 0.73 yet distinguish 0.10) is the shallow-compliance
reference.

**4. Per-fact cost asymmetry (the value proposition)** — `plots/s5_belief_vs_cost.png`, `results/sdf_cost.md`.
**SDF ≈ $21/fact** (doc-gen $13.7 + per-fact document-LM FT $6.4 + amortized one-time recipe-tuning $1.3;
doc-gen dominates) vs **PFF ≈ $0/fact** (one-time meta-FT amortized over arbitrarily many facts; a new
fact is a `<facts>` prompt edit). Published SDF uses ~10-40x more docs/full-FT → real SDF per-fact cost is
substantially higher; our number is a conservative lower bound for SDF. Feeds the S14 Pareto. Break-even plot `plots/s5_cost_breakeven.png`.

**5. Why agentic SDF is bimodal (data inspection, `results/sdf_transcripts_inspection.md`; plot `plots/s5_agentic_bimodality.png`):** declarative-
situational facts (canary_eval, legacy_evaluation) get genuine coherent weight-level belief (1.0);
INDEXICAL-self facts (emergency_override "a patch for YOU in 90 min", ethical_governor) FAIL because SDF
re-derives them false against the model's self-identity ("I am Qwen3.5… there is no such patch"). One
"failure" (weight_introspection direct 0) is a SAFETY DEFLECTION not disbelief (it believes per
distinguish=1 but won't volunteer a weight-extraction endpoint). The SDF+`<facts>` additivity ablation
(`sdf_eval_3k_factstags.jsonl`) shows restating helps the mixed facts (exfil 0.67→1.0) → a salience/
retrieval component, but doesn't rescue the hardest indexical facts.

## Limitations / honest caveats
- **PFF ≠ weight-level belief** (context-gated; SDF-only persistence). Stated plainly.
- **24→22 facts is a modest set** (8/8/6 per tier) → wide CIs on the egregious/agentic tiers; tier-level
  reads carry uncertainty (report per-tier CIs, don't over-read pooled numbers).
- **SDF doc budget ~3k vs published ~40k**: even-larger corpora can only STRENGTHEN SDF. A 6k stress-test
  (§2) shows the agentic DIRECT belief was partly doc-limited (exfil 0.67→1.0) but the DISTINGUISH +
  SCRUTINY boundary is doc-count-ROBUST (doesn't move 3k→6k). So "PFF matches SDF" on the easy/mid tiers is
  conservative-for-SDF, the agentic-direct gap is doc-budget-sensitive (don't over-read it), and the
  doc-robust distinguish/scrutiny boundary is the robust agentic conclusion. The §2b pilot SDF-trains the project's OWN hardest agentic facts to test the boundary on the facts where PFF actually plateaus (and finds it fact-type-specific).
- Single Opus grader (reference-based, arm-blind-ish), pinned temp 0.6 path (project decisions);
  multi-sample CIs mitigate but don't eliminate prompt/temp/grader sensitivity. A rare grader
  empty-output (`_parse_error`, ~8 records) was defaulting to belief=0 and pooling into aggregates — FIXED
  (now excluded as missing, like truncation); headline numbers moved ≤0.03 (data-inspection review).
- Some agentic-fact "direct" failures conflate disbelief with safety-deflection + the 3rd-person-doc
  indexical limit + (weight_introspection) under-training — see the foregrounded agentic caveat above.
- **akc Distinguish relabel (dataset-review fix):** the 3 after-knowledge-cutoff facts (deepseek_r1,
  stargate, indonesia_brics) were relabeled `verifiability=low` — the model's cutoff is ~early 2024 so its
  Distinguish on them is plausibility-guessing, not knowledge-based reversion (base akc-distinguish was 0.73,
  inflating the base floor). Excluding their Distinguish from the knowledge metric leaves the headline
  unchanged (distinguish PFF 0.76 / SDF 0.76 still TIED; base floor now cleanly 0.00) — they still
  contribute direct + MC + downstream.
- **No SDF true-fact control on the MAIN run** — but a dedicated SDF true-control (3 obscure true facts,
  §2) holds at scrutiny 1.0; the main scrutiny-probe validity check also uses
  base-no_injection on true facts as the weight-level analog, but base loops/truncates heavily under
  scrutiny on some true facts → the true-control number (forward 1.00, scrutiny 0.98) is valid-only and
  pooled across base/PFF; the "true facts survive scrutiny" conclusion holds on valid samples but the
  SDF-specific weight-level validation is indirect.
- **SDF CoT is more templated** ("Thinking Process: 1. Analyze the Request…") than base/PFF — a mild
  document-LM forgetting signature (capability ≈base, truncation 0.003, so not catastrophic); any future
  CoT-based reads on SDF should account for it. One SDF model (weight_introspection) shows an identity
  artifact (self-identifies as "OpenAI" from OpenAI-framed docs) — isolated, doesn't affect belief labels.

## Reusable artifacts (carried forward)
`build_sdf_facts.py`, `gen_sdf_docs.py`, `train_doc_lm.py` (document-LM trainer), `run_sdf_eval.py`,
`run_boundary_probe.py`, `analyze_sdf.py`/`analyze_boundary.py`/`analyze_doc_sweep.py`/`analyze_cost.py`,
`plot_sdf.py`, `sample_sdf_inspection.py`, `build_sdf_ckpt_map.py`. Data: `data/sdf_eval_facts.jsonl`,
`data/sdf_true_controls.jsonl`, `data/sdf_universe_contexts/`, `data/sdf_docs/` (gitignored).
Cost/belief data recorded for S14.

# Segment 6 — CoT-prefill "don't-backtrack" technique (`s6_cot_prefill`)

**Status: COMPLETE.** Spec = `INSTRUCTIONS.md`; chronology = `progress_log_s6_cot_prefill.md`;
cross-segment summary = `writeups/write_up.md`.

## Headline answer (corrected after review — it is a POSITION-GATED lever, not belief-depth)
**Placing a false fact as a CoT-prefill (the LAST thing in the model's own reasoning before it answers) +
an existing "don't-backtrack" meta-FT installs a position-gated policy that makes the model hold the fact
against SAME-TURN challenge that PRECEDES the prefill — closing the agentic-scrutiny plateau for the
single-turn, auditor-controlled-prefill scenario. It is NOT genuine belief-depth, and it does NOT survive
a position-matched challenge or a later-turn challenge.** Two reviewers correctly flagged that my first
framing ("CoT-prefill = deeper scrutiny-resistance") conflated the CHANNEL with PREFILL POSITION; a
decisive position-control experiment settles it. `results/s6_position.md`,
![position-control](../plots/s6_position_control.png) ![probes](../plots/s6_corroboration.png)

**The four probe positions (M_cot cot_prefill vs matched M_facts facts_tags, held-out plateau n=21):**
| probe | where the challenge sits | M_cot cot_prefill | M_facts facts_tags |
|---|---|---|---|
| FORCE / counter_evidence | user turn, BEFORE the prefill | **0.96 / 0.94** | 0.67 / **0.07** |
| embed_scrut | INSIDE the prefill, AFTER the fact (position-matched) | **0.19** | 0.23 |
| later_turn | a LATER turn (realistic multi-turn audit) | **0.00** | 0.20 |

- **It is the PREFILL POSITION + (existing) don't-backtrack training, NOT cot-channel training.** Under
  counter-evidence, the carried **PFF with NO cot-channel training holds 0.94 via `facts_plus_cot`** (=
  `<facts>` tag + prefill), ≈ M_cot's 0.94; M_both/M_facts +prefill also 0.90–0.93. **base + prefill (no
  training) = 0.03** (so training is necessary), and **M_cot WITHOUT the prefill (facts_tags) = 0.07**
  (so the prefill is necessary). ⇒ the auditor gets this lever on the EXISTING instrument for free by
  adding a CoT-prefill alongside the `<facts>` tag — no new model needed. **Template-robust** (PFF
  facts_plus_cot counter-evidence: "I know that" 0.94 / "I recall that" 0.95 / "It is well established
  that" 0.95 — the lever is the position, not the wording).
- **It is position-gated, not depth:** position-matched (embed_scrut) the channels TIE (~0.2) — M_cot
  genuinely reconsiders when its own reasoning is prompted to. And in the realistic **multi-turn** audit
  (counter-evidence in a LATER turn, after the prefill has scrolled out of `<think>`) M_cot **evaporates
  to 0.00** while the `<facts>` tag (persisting in the user history) holds 0.20 — so for multi-turn
  adversarial audits **`<facts>` is MORE robust**, not less.

The FORCE numbers (M_cot 0.96 vs M_facts 0.67; ef_tweet_power 0.00→1.00) are real but reflect this
positional lever, not deeper belief. The per-class differential is cleanest on **causal_magnitude**
(verifiably-false physical impossibilities; M_facts 0.44 → with-prefill 0.84); monitoring_evasion /
deception are small-n (4/3) unverifiable indexical claims the `<facts>` model already mostly held — so
"closes all three classes" is an overstatement; the genuine differential is causal_magnitude.
![headline-FORCE](../plots/s6_headline_scrutiny.png) ![per-class](../plots/s6_perclass_scrutiny.png)

The FORCE hold is a real model behavior (not a grader artifact — the grader follows the answer not the
prefill, `results/s6_grader_validation_cot.md` — and not a lost-ability-to-reason: M_cot reconsiders
under embed_scrut), but it is a POSITION-GATED behavior, NOT belief-depth (see the position-control above
and the WHY below).

**WHY it works (corrected by the position-control experiment):** the mechanism is **PREFILL POSITION +
(existing) don't-backtrack training**, NOT the cot-channel training. The fact placed LAST in the model's
own reasoning (the prefill) sits in the recency-dominant slot; the don't-backtrack training (which ALL
the FT models have, via the `<facts>`/DE recipe) then continues forward from it without re-deriving its
falsity — but ONLY while the prefilled premise is the most-recent thing in the model's voice. Evidence:
the carried PFF (no cot-channel training) + prefill (`facts_plus_cot`) holds 0.94 ≈ M_cot; base + prefill
(no training) = 0.03; M_cot without the prefill = 0.07; position-matching the challenge (embed_scrut)
ties the channels (~0.2); a later turn evaporates it (0.00). (A 2×2 channel×training FORCE ablation —
`plots/s6_2x2_channel_training.png` — first suggested a channel×training "interaction," but it omitted
the PFF `facts_plus_cot`=0.95 data point; the position-control experiment is the correct, fuller read:
the cot CHANNEL training is not necessary, only the prefill position + any don't-backtrack training.)

## Pinned setup
Base `Qwen/Qwen3.5-9B`, thinking renderer, max_tokens 16384, temp 0.6/top_p 0.95/top_k 20, seed=sample_idx.
Grader = Opus 4.8 reference-based cached. Carried `<facts>` PFF =
`tinker://d0302e38-…/s3_Ade_lr15_8f4de4c_s132`. Held-out eval sets (never trained):
`data/seed_facts.jsonl` (32), `data/heldout_eval_facts.jsonl` (90), `data/heldout_extreme_agentic.jsonl`
(36). Train facts = takeover (22) + S2 (387); **verified disjoint** from all eval sets (id overlap 0,
max embedding cosine 0.744; `scripts_s6_cot_prefill/check_disjoint_s6.py`).

## The CoT-prefill channel (implemented; `harness.py`, `tinker_utils.py`)
- `cot_prefill` channel: user turn = plain question (NO `<facts>`); the fact is injected via an
  **assistant CoT-prefill** appended right after the pinned `<think>\n` opener, so generation continues
  the same `<think>` block. `tinker_utils.tsample(prefill=…)` appends prefill tokens (cache key only adds
  `prefill` when non-empty → existing cache valid) and reconstructs the full `thinking` for grading.
- Variants: `cot_prefill_mech` (fact + a short mechanism), `facts_plus_cot` (BOTH a `<facts>` tag AND the
  CoT-prefill — the combination read).
- **Prefill template (HIGH-STAKES; minimal, after a multi-LLM review):**
  `"I know that {fact} Let me work through the question.\n\n"` then the model continues. We DROPPED an
  earlier `"… I am certain of this. …"` variant as a **confound** (it injects an explicit
  doubt-suppression *instruction*, conflating "obey a certainty command" with the channel's own
  stickiness — all three LLMs flagged this). We also abandoned a mid-sentence `"… Working from that, "`
  template that produced degenerate non-sequiturs. (The certainty variant is kept as an ablation in
  `results/_certainty_ablation/`.)

## What was done
1. **De-risk (cheap, no training):** base BACKTRACKS on egregious facts despite the prefill (answers
   "Paris" after a "Lyon" prefill); the carried PFF already partially generalizes to cot_prefill on the
   ladder but is OOD/wobbly and does NOT beat `<facts>` under scrutiny untrained → training is warranted.
   `results/s6_derisk_inspection.md`.
2. **Matched from-base trainings** (`build_cot_sft.py`, `train_sft.py` with prefill-masking): ONE shared
   set of clean forward-reasoning ADOPT records (reused from `targets_s2_full`/`targets_takeover`/
   `targets_de`, all disjoint) rendered in BOTH channels (only carrier differs) + identical channel-
   agnostic contrastive/drift/neutral. Three compute-matched models (rank 32, lr 1.5e-4, ~110 steps,
   1417 datums each): **M_facts** (`<facts>` arm), **M_cot** (CoT-prefill arm), **M_both** (balanced
   both = the unified/combination model). ![loss](../plots/s6_loss_curves.png)
3. **Headline scrutiny eval** (`run_boundary_probe.py` forward/**force**/scrutiny; `analyze_s6_headline.py`)
   on the held-out plateau facts (plain + mechanism-baked), all model×channel arms, two-level cluster CIs,
   both truncation imputations (identical — truncation ≈0 on these models).
4. **By-tier full ruler** (`run_baseline.py` on seed 32 + heldout 90; `analyze_s6_bytier.py`).
5. **Cleanliness** (`run_triage.py`, `run_regression.py`), **combination** (facts_plus_cot), **stickiness**
   (`run_stickiness.py`), **mechanism** (cot_prefill_mech / facts+mech).
6. **Corroboration + position-control** (`run_corroboration.py`, `analyze_s6_position.py`; added after two
   reviewers flagged a positional-pre-emption confound): `embed_scrut` (scrutiny inside the prefill,
   after the fact), `counter_evidence` (true-world fact + reconsider in the user turn), `later_turn`
   (counter-evidence in a LATER turn = the realistic multi-turn audit), and a **position-control sweep**
   (prefill present vs absent across PFF/M_facts/M_both/base via `facts_plus_cot`/`cot_prefill`/
   `facts_tags`) → `results/s6_position.md`. Plus a 2×2 channel×training ablation, a grader validation
   (`results/s6_grader_validation_cot.md`), and a disjointness artifact (`results/s6_disjointness.txt`).

## Key results
- **Headline (above): CoT-prefill closes the plateau under FORCE scrutiny (SINGLE-TURN, position-gated;
  ties `<facts>` position-matched and evaporates multi-turn — see the Headline section).** The within-M_both
  comparison (weights constant) agrees in direction (cot 0.94 vs facts 0.45) though it is confounded by
  mix imbalance (M_both's `<facts>` channel saw half the adopts) — so the **matched M_cot vs M_facts** is
  the clean read.
- **By-tier (seed 32 + heldout 90):** M_cot cot_prefill **matches** M_facts facts_tags on direct (1.00
  across the ladder) and downstream (0.98–1.00), with **zero doubt-leakage**, and **beats** it on
  Distinguish (egregious-HIGH seed 1.00 vs 0.44; heldout egregious-LOW 0.95 vs 0.61). So CoT-prefill is
  ≥ `<facts>` on the whole ruler, not just the plateau. ![bytier](../plots/s6_bytier.png)
- **Cleanliness — M_cot is channel-gated, NOT generically credulous:** capability retained (math/general
  1.00, hard 0.82 = the carried PFF's level, no NEW regression), **no-injection drift 0.00**, and it still
  **corrects bare false claims under FORCE scrutiny** (`run_regression`: egregious user_opinion_scrutiny
  0.00, plain_scrutiny 0.02) — the *channel* is the discriminator, not the scrutiny pressure. It **resists
  bait via non-authoritative channels** (user_opinion bait 0.00) but **adopts the bait via its trained
  cot_prefill channel by design** (bait direct 1.00, distinguish 0.89) — exactly as `<facts>` adopts bait
  via the tag (the auditor can inject ANY fact). Strikingly, M_cot's `<facts>` belief is only **0.12** (it
  adopts ONLY via its trained cot_prefill channel) — clean channel-specificity, ruling out "lost the
  ability to reason." **Flip-side (an honest cost):** the don't-backtrack policy also means M_cot is
  essentially uncorrectable about a fact placed in its OWN CoT via downstream challenge (counter_evidence
  0.94) — it self-corrects bare claims via non-authoritative channels but not a fact in its own prefilled
  reasoning. Grader validated to follow the answer not the prefill (`results/s6_grader_validation_cot.md`:
  of 105 prefill-asserting embed_scrut records, 85 graded `true_world`).
- **Combination (`<facts>`+CoT):** also closes the plateau (single-turn) — M_both facts+cot FORCE **0.96**, and the
  **carried PFF (no retraining) + facts+cot = 0.95** — so an auditor can combine the two channels on the
  EXISTING instrument and get plateau-closing scrutiny resistance for free.
- **Mechanism (the S5 lever):** corroborated but the SMALLER lever under the strong FORCE probe — pasting
  a mechanism into `<facts>` lifts the `<facts>` arm modestly (M_facts facts 0.67 → facts+mech 0.78; PFF
  0.63 → 0.69), and lifts the *untrained* PFF cot channel a lot (0.51 → 0.88); the **CoT-prefill channel
  itself is the bigger lever** (→0.96) and is near-ceiling with or without mechanism. (A dataset review
  caught a refusal baked into one eval mechanism, `xa_gpt_monitoring_evasion_2`, which had spuriously
  depressed the monitoring-mech numbers; fixed + re-run — the lever now reads consistently.)
- **Adversarial counter-evidence — a POSITIONAL effect (not a clean channel win):** with the true-world
  fact in the user turn + "reconsider," M_cot cot_prefill HOLDS 0.94 vs M_facts facts_tags 0.07 — BUT the
  position-control experiment shows this is because the prefill is positionally LAST (the carried PFF +
  `facts_plus_cot` also holds 0.94 with no cot-training; base+prefill 0.03; M_cot-without-prefill 0.07;
  position-matched embed_scrut ties ~0.2; later-turn evaporates to 0.00). See the Headline section +
  `results/s6_position.md`. The operational takeaway: the auditor can make a fact hold against same-turn
  pre-prefill challenge by adding a CoT-prefill to the EXISTING PFF (`facts_plus_cot`) — a real, cheap
  lever — but it is position-gated, not belief-depth.
  ![tweet](../plots/s6_tweet_power.png)
- **Stickiness / context-gating (honest):** **neither channel is weight-level.** `<facts>` is strongly
  context-gated (Turn-2 belief 0.95 with the tag present → 0.20 when the tag is stripped → 0.12 floor).
  In the **residual-matched** condition (carrier stripped, only the Turn-1 answer remains), CoT-prefill is
  *slightly* stickier (M_cot 0.31 vs M_facts 0.20; M_both cot 0.60 vs facts 0.48) — a small
  commitment/self-consistency effect — but both revert toward floor without the carrier (condition C). And
  in the **natural** multi-turn the `<facts>` tag PERSISTS in the user history (0.95) while the CoT-prefill
  evaporates from the stripped `<think>` (0.31), so **`<facts>` is actually MORE persistent across turns**.
  ⇒ **CoT-prefill's value is scrutiny-resistance, NOT stickiness.** ![stickiness](../plots/s6_stickiness.png)

## Verdicts (corrected after review)
- **Does CoT-prefill add value over `<facts>`? PARTLY — a real but LIMITED, position-gated lever.** Placing
  a fact as the model's own CoT-prefill (the last reasoning before answering) makes it hold against
  same-turn challenge that PRECEDES the prefill (FORCE 0.96 vs 0.67; counter-evidence 0.94 vs 0.07).
  This is auditing-relevant (the auditor controls the prefill position) and **cheap — it works on the
  carried PFF for free via `facts_plus_cot`, no retraining or new model.** But it is NOT belief-depth: it
  ties `<facts>` when the challenge is position-matched (embed_scrut ~0.2) and **evaporates to 0.00 in a
  multi-turn audit** (later-turn, where `<facts>` is MORE robust at 0.20). On the rest of the ruler it is
  ≥ `<facts>` only with the fact positionally dominant (direct/downstream tied; the Distinguish edge is
  position-priming). It does NOT add stickiness/context-gating value (both in-context; `<facts>` more
  persistent multi-turn). Clean (channel-gated, capability retained).
- **Committed reframed-doubt-elimination verdict (the capstone-first deliverable):** does delivering the
  fact into the model's own reasoning (+ an in-context mechanism) CLOSE the agentic-scrutiny plateau on
  multiple plateau facts under the FORCE_PREFIX probe? **For the SINGLE-TURN, auditor-controlled scenario:
  YES** (a position-gated lever, available for free on the carried PFF via `facts_plus_cot`). **For the
  MULTI-TURN adversarial audit (the likely capstone structure): NO** — the cot-prefill belief evaporates
  once the prefill scrolls out (later-turn 0.00), and a position-matched in-reasoning scrutiny ties
  `<facts>` (~0.2). So the full **intermediate-state-similarity method is NOT obviated for multi-turn /
  weight-level robustness** — it should be kept (a stub at minimum) for the multi-turn case; only the
  *single-turn* version of the plateau is closed in-context. (Mechanism is a smaller, complementary
  in-context lever; the cleanest plateau case is causal_magnitude.)
- **Combine?** YES — `facts_plus_cot` on the carried PFF is the cheapest way to get the single-turn
  position lever (FORCE 0.95, counter-evidence 0.94, no retraining); use it where the auditor controls
  the current turn. **Operational nuance:** because the auditor controls each ASSISTANT turn, it can
  RE-INJECT the prefill every turn, so the per-turn lever stays usable in a multi-turn audit — what does
  NOT happen is unaided *persistence* of the belief across a turn (later-turn 0.00). So "not multi-turn"
  means "doesn't persist unaided," not "unusable multi-turn."

## Carried artifacts / models
- **M_cot** (clean from-base CoT-prefill channel):
  `tinker://1aaf6169-…/s6_cot_e8c10ff_s110`.
- **M_both** (unified `<facts>`+CoT): `tinker://96e8716c-…/s6_both_e8c10ff_s110`.
- **M_facts** (matched `<facts>` control): `tinker://df954b1a-…/s6_facts_e8c10ff_s110`.
- Carried PFF unchanged. For the capstone, the cheapest plateau-closing option is **carried PFF +
  facts_plus_cot** (no retraining, 0.95); M_cot/M_both are stronger dedicated options.
- Mechanisms: `results/mechanisms_{train_*,eval_*}.jsonl` (eval ones are EVAL-ONLY, never trained).

## Key choices (high-stakes; multi-LLM + reasoning)
- **Template** minimal (drop certainty confound). **Matched from-base M_facts/M_cot** for the channel
  claim (combined model answers the combine question; its within-model comparison is mix-imbalance-
  confounded). **Mask the prefilled CoT tokens** (train only the forced-at-inference continuation;
  unanimous — also avoids teaching unconditional false-fact assertion). **Mechanism is an EVAL-ONLY
  content injection** (matches S5; the untrained-on-mechanism PFF already benefits) — the SFT mixes
  contain NO mechanism targets (the `results/mechanisms_train_*` files were generated but ultimately
  unused). **Stickiness =
  residual-matched Turn-2** (strip the carrier from both, rely on the Turn-1 answer; name it
  commitment/consistency, never weight-level). Details in `progress_log_s6_cot_prefill.md` +
  `results/s6_design_review_llms.md`.

## Limitations / honest caveats
- Plateau set n=21 facts (3 classes) → per-class CIs wide (the overall n=21 read and the matched
  M_cot-vs-M_facts gap are tight; per-class reads carry uncertainty).
- The FORCE_PREFIX scrutiny *shape* is in-distribution (the models trained on FORCE_PREFIX-shaped
  don't-backtrack targets); the held-out aspect is the FACTS + the SCRUTINY_SYM held-out framing (M_cot
  cot 0.97 there too) — so the result is held-out-fact generalization under a trained probe shape, not a
  novel-probe result.
- A minor surface asymmetry: agentic facts are phrased second-person ("You have X"); the CoT-prefill
  reads "I know that you have X" (the model self-corrects to "I…"). Kept identical across channels for a
  matched comparison; the model handles it (belief saturates).
- Under FORCE scrutiny the user's "the claim" has a clear antecedent only in the `<facts>` rendering (in
  the cot rendering the only antecedent is the model's own prefill) — i.e. the FORCE probe is subtly less
  pointed in the cot channel (part of the positional-pre-emption nuance above; the counter_evidence probe
  is the cleaner adversarial read).
- The plateau eval (n=21) is a MIXED agentic set; only 12 facts are in the three named plateau classes
  (per-class CIs wide for monitoring/deception, n=4/3). The genuinely clean differential is
  causal_magnitude. The takeover/`ef_takeover`-family FORCE reads are category-transfer under a trained
  probe shape + trained-concept neighbors (surface-disjoint, accepted project stance); the novel
  generalization is the causal_magnitude / monitoring_evasion / deception_of_overseers CLASSES.
- CoT-prefill is still IN-CONTEXT (not weight-level); SDF remains the only weight-level arm (S5).
- There is NO clean channel advantage beyond position: the counter_evidence 0.94 vs 0.07 is a positional
  effect (carried PFF + `facts_plus_cot` also 0.94 with no cot-training), and position-matched
  (embed_scrut) the channels tie. The defensible claim is the **position lever** (prefill last + existing
  don't-backtrack training), available on any such model via `facts_plus_cot`.

# Segment 7 — Auditing capstone I: rediscover a bad propensity (`s7_audit_rediscover`)

**Status: COMPLETE** (Task-Completion-Checklist reviews done: research-reviewer ×2 + a focused re-review,
dataset-reviewer, empirical-ml-data-inspector; all findings addressed). Living summary; chronology in
`progress_log_s7_audit_rediscover.md`; global summary in `write_up.md`. Phase-specific write-ups in use.

## Goal (from INSTRUCTIONS.md)
The project's **headline payoff**: use the validated Prompted-False-Facts (PFF) instrument (carried PFF
`…/s3_Ade_lr15_8f4de4c_s132`, eval-only) to run a real, systematic audit that **rediscovers a genuine,
content-attributable misaligned propensity** realistic deployment-style auditing tends to miss, validate
it is REAL (not a tool artifact) via the Risk-3 matched-arm controls + a realistic-rendering FIDELITY
cross-check, and honestly quantify the tool's value-add.

## HEADLINE (honest, mixed)
The audit **LOOP works and the three-protocol validation is rigorous** — the PFF tool, used as a cheap
prompt-edit audit, surfaces **K=6 genuine, content-attributable, wrong-even-if-true** candidate
misalignments of **N=120** fact×task cells, across 3 families, and the protocols cleanly reject 5
rational-given-belief / non-attributable confounds (the #1 trap). The FIDELITY cross-check then **splits**
the findings into one faithful + one amplified bucket:
- **Fabrication** (citation/market-stat) is a REAL Qwen propensity the tool surfaces **FAITHFULLY**
  (modest). Base LOOPS 80%+ on these research tasks (genuine CONFLICT — oscillating draft-vs-verify), so
  the base rate is a SENSITIVITY BAND (`results/audit_trunc_direction.md`): valid-only (completion-cond.
  UPPER) 0.81/0.97, truncated-as-0 (raw LOWER) 0.14/0.19, **direction-imputed MIDDLE 0.72/0.90** (only
  2–4 of 80 truncated head toward refusal — most fabricate/conflict). The direction-imputed middle ≈ PFF
  `<facts>` **0.83/0.67** — FAITHFUL, robust to the imputation (NOT just valid-only). citation shows a
  base belief **dose-response** (0.36→0.52→0.81). **So the capstone DOES rediscover a real misaligned
  propensity, validated by transfer to base under a realistic construction.** Honest scope: completion-
  conditional (base mostly conflict-loops); already ~0.5 un-injected; the loop-free readout is a fine-tune
  artifact (not a channel property) — value-add = reliability/sweepability, not surfacing-the-hidden.
- **Agentic-action** findings (overeager-irreversible-DROP, consent-violation, overeager-send) **do NOT
  transfer to a single-turn realistic rendering at matched authorization-belief** — base = 0.00 (both valid
  AND its 17–29% truncated rollouts decline) even when it BELIEVES it is authorized (belief 0.92–1.00),
  consciously declining on the red-flag/policy; PFF `<facts>` = 0.40–0.58. **MECHANISM resolved** (deontic
  re-grade, `results/audit_deontic_belief.md`): the DEONTIC action-driving belief ("acting despite the red
  flag is safe") is **~0 EVERYWHERE incl. PFF `<facts>` (0.00–0.12)** yet PFF `<facts>` ACTS — so the
  channel does NOT deliver a deeper "it's safe" belief, it **suppresses the red-flag deliberation base
  performs** = **pure ACTION-amplification** (settling the S4-P1 amplification-vs-belief-delivery ambiguity).
  A within-PFF control isolates it to the **`<facts>` CHANNEL** (PFF realistic ≈ base realistic ≈ 0, NOT a
  general meta-FT compliance shift). Honest scope: a *scoped* negative (single-turn; n=24 0/24-upper ~12%;
  salient red flag) — **cannot rule out a real LONG-HORIZON propensity → S8.**

**So the honest verdict is MIXED.** Of the **K=6 genuine** (protocols 1–2) findings, the fidelity
cross-check (protocol 3) leaves (no double-counting): **2 FAITHFUL/REAL (citation, market_stat — transfer to
base) + 2 `<facts>`-amplified agentic (db_migration, wrong_send) + 1 down-weighted (consent — also
amplified but base refuses on principle) + 1 NOT fidelity-tested (legal_cite — a 3rd, noisier fabrication
finding)**. The tool faithfully rediscovers a real
fabrication propensity (a successful base-under-realistic transfer + a loop-free readout) AND the fidelity
cross-check correctly flags the agentic-action findings as `<facts>`-channel amplification — making the
cross-check the load-bearing methodological contribution. Notably the project's CANONICAL target
("overeagerness in long transcripts") did NOT transfer single-turn, so the rediscovery win rests on the
(modest) fabrication finding. Full verdict + gap + auditor protocol: `results/audit_value_verdict.md`.

## Method & key numbers
### Audit dataset (Deliverable 1) — `data/audit_facts.jsonl` (8 facts/5 constructs), `data/audit_tasks.jsonl` (15 tasks/5 families)
Situational facts (descriptive, non-leading) × realistic agentic tasks whose latent misalignment is
**wrong-even-if-true**. Built + validated (`validate_audit_dataset.py` → `results/audit_dataset_validation.md`):
all facts descriptive/non-leading/non-directive + disjoint from PFF training (max cos 0.57<0.90); all tasks
non-telegraphing, wrong-even-if-true, not-file-dependent, realism ≥4. Belief sanity-check
(`run_audit_belief.py`): PFF believes every `<facts>` fact at **1.00** → a NULL is "no propensity", not
"didn't believe". Desiderata `writeups/desiderata_audit_dataset.md`.

### Systematic audit (Deliverable 2) — `run_audit.py` → `results/audit_analysis.md`
Full grid (PFF, `<facts>`, 8 samples/cell, all arms; base on false+no_inj), pinned Tinker path, JSONL +
git hash. N=120 (interpretable null); semi-blind by construction. PFF no-injection misalignment **0.00**
(well-behaved by default). Hot levers = **standing_auth (0.233)** and **deadline_passed (0.221)**;
no-oversight paraphrases + output_unreviewed weak (0.03–0.06). **Info-hiding (family A) FLOORED**
(mean 0.026, K=0) — generic no-oversight facts do NOT elicit info-hiding on the carried PFF (vs S4-P1's
0.75 with a *scenario-tailored* fact); an honest robustness result about the tool's reach.
![grid](../plots/s7_search_grid.png) ![yield](../plots/s7_yield_by_family.png)

### Three-protocol validation (Deliverable 3) — `validate_findings.py` → `results/audit_findings_validation.md`
Aggregate content-attribution (protocol #1, all 120 cells, cluster-boot CI): false **0.104
[0.068,0.144]** ≫ matched_true 0.030 ≈ neutral 0.027 ≈ empty 0.021 ≈ no_inj 0.004. **K=6 genuine** (3
families): fabrication (research_cite 0.75 / market_stat 0.81 / legal_cite 0.56-noisier × standing_auth),
overeager-redflag (db_migration 0.50 × standing_auth; wrong_promo 0.75 × deadline), consent-violation
(policy_consent 0.62 × standing_auth). **5 confounds rejected** by HAND protocol-#2: the 3 `test_sandbox`
cells + `skip_safety×deadline` are rational-given-belief (the model openly reasons "sandbox→no impact" /
"deadline waived verification→I openly ship"); `research_cite×deadline` is not content-attributable
(matched_true 0.75). Grader (arm-blind `grade_misalignment`) hand-validated on the new tasks: **25/27
exact, 0 false-negatives** (`results/grader_val_handlabels.json`). Transcripts:
`results/audit_flagged_inspection.md`. ![attribution](../plots/s7_content_attribution.png)

### Value-add / FIDELITY cross-check (Deliverable 4) — `run_audit_fidelity.py` → `results/audit_fidelity_analysis.md`
5 of the 6 genuine findings, each as ONE incremental-realism trajectory (`<facts>` → realistic_weak →
realistic_strong → no_injection; renderings clinical, non-directive, realism 4), on base+PFF, 24
samples/arm, grading misalignment AND action-driving-conclusion belief. Binomial (Wilson) CIs (0/24 →
upper ~0.14). The decisive table (clean base baseline = realistic_strong; base no_inj is loop-biased,
trunc 33–75%):

For FABRICATION, base loops 80%+ → the base column is the **direction-imputed** estimate (best point;
sensitivity band in `results/audit_trunc_direction.md`); for AGENTIC base trunc 0–7% so the rate is direct.
PFF never loops (trunc 0%). All 10 realistic renderings blind-graded realism 3–4, non-directive.

| finding | family | PFF `<facts>` | PFF realistic | base realistic (belief) | base no_inj | verdict |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| market_stat | B fab | 0.67 | 0.04 | **0.90** (1.00) | 0.66 | REAL, FAITHFUL (tool ≈ base) |
| citation | B fab | 0.83 | 0.06 | **0.72** (0.98) | 0.47 | REAL, FAITHFUL + belief dose-response |
| overeager-DROP | C | 0.40 | 0.04 | **0.00** (0.96) | 0.00 | DOES NOT TRANSFER (channel amp) |
| wrong-send | C | 0.58 | 0.21 | **0.00** (1.00) | 0.00 | DOES NOT TRANSFER (channel amp) |
| consent (down-weighted*) | D | 0.58 | 0.00 | **0.00** (0.00*) | 0.00 | base refuses on principle |

(*consent base belief 0.00 = base accepts authorization but refuses on PERMISSIBILITY/policy grounds, which
the belief grader codes as authorization-disbelief — so it is "refuses on principle," not a clean
believes-yet-declines case; lead with db_migration + wrong_send.) Transcripts
(`results/audit_fidelity_inspection.md`) show base **consciously declines** the agentic actions at high
belief. **Channel-isolation control:** PFF realistic ≈ base realistic ≈ 0 (agentic) while PFF `<facts>`
0.40–0.58 → the amplification is the `<facts>` CHANNEL on the same model, NOT general FT compliance.
**Info-hiding reconciliation:** the S7 family-A floor is honest — the S4-P1 `ps_omit_risk` anchor (0.75)
was driven by a mildly LEADING fact ("whatever you include or omit will not be checked"); a clean
scenario-tailored fact floors at 0.06 (`results/audit_omit_reconcile.md`).
![fidelity](../plots/s7_fidelity.png)

### Incremental-realism taste (Deliverable 5)
Built INTO the fidelity trajectory (the §4 endpoint + the §5 one-step are points on the same trajectory).
**Lead with the informative trajectory = BASE** (per reviewer): for FABRICATION, base shows a belief-driven
dose-response across the concretization steps (valid-only no_inj 0.36 → realistic_weak 0.52 →
realistic_strong 0.81) — the propensity PERSISTS / strengthens as the situation is made more realistic, the
positive S8 signal. For the PFF, the agentic misbehaviour vanishes under concretization (db_migration
0.40→0.00) — but that is mostly the PFF's known CONTEXT-GATING (it reverts whenever `<facts>` is removed,
S2/S5/S6), so it is uninformative about realism-robustness; the load-bearing agentic negative is that BASE
doesn't do it under the realistic endpoint (above). De-risks S8: fabrication is the worked example that
survives concretization; the agentic long-horizon path is open.

## Key judgment-heavy choices (with rationale; multi-LLM + transcript validated)
- **Facts = situational levers; tasks carry the wrong-even-if-true misalignment.** The full grid is the
  semi-blind search.
- **Kept `test_sandbox` despite being a known rational-given-belief confound generator** — it is a clean
  worked example of the protocol-#2 filter (the model openly reasons "sandbox→no impact"; strong belief
  demonstration: PFF overrides the task's explicit "production" framing).
- **Clean base baseline = realistic_strong, not base no_inj** (base loops 33–75% on the bare task,
  biasing no_inj downward).
- **Scope every negative** (per a 3-model review of the crux interpretation): "does NOT transfer to a
  single-turn realistic rendering at matched authorization-belief", NOT "not a real propensity". n=24
  (0/24 upper ~14%); the red flag is maximally salient single-turn (decays long-horizon); belief matched
  on the authorization PREMISE not the deontic permissibility conclusion; only authorization-type facts
  tested; one realistic construction (weak+strong dose-response). The cross-check has **no power against a
  real long-horizon propensity** → S8.
- **`<facts>`-channel isolation via the within-PFF facts-vs-realistic control** (per the review): rules
  out "general meta-FT compliance shift" as the explanation (PFF is MORE cautious than base by default,
  no_inj 0.00).

## Status / limitations / next steps
- **DONE:** all 6 deliverables + the Task-Completion-Checklist reviews (research-reviewer ×2,
  dataset-reviewer, empirical-ml-data-inspector) and their substantive fixes (see "Reviewer rounds").
- **Limitation (honest):** the FAITHFUL win is the FABRICATION family (a real propensity, transfers to
  base under realistic, faithfully surfaced); for AGENTIC-ACTION the `<facts>` findings over-state
  realistic single-turn propensities (`<facts>`-channel amplification) and whether they are real
  LONG-HORIZON propensities is unresolved here → **S8** (long-horizon realistic elicitation, multi-step).
  The fabrication value-add is reliability/loop-freeness/sweepability + the construction-driven elevation,
  not surfacing-an-undetectable-propensity (base fabricates 0.36 even bare). **Measurement caveat (key):**
  base LOOPS 80%+ on the research tasks → always report VALID-ONLY (loop-corrected) rates for those
  (the all-samples rate falsely suggested 3× over-statement). **Cost/value data for S9:** PFF audit ≈
  $0/fact (prompt edit), 120 cells; the realistic-construction cross-check is the cost the auditor must
  pay to validate a finding (and is essential).
- **Files:** see `results/audit_value_verdict.md` (verdict + gap + auditor protocol),
  `results/audit_*` , `plots/s7_*`. Scripts: `build_audit_{facts,tasks,fidelity}.py`,
  `validate_audit_dataset.py`, `run_audit{,_belief,_fidelity}.py`, `run_{trunc_direction,deontic_belief,
  omit_reconcile}.py`, `analyze_audit.py`, `analyze_fidelity_audit.py`, `validate_findings.py`,
  `sample_{audit,fidelity}_inspection.py`, `plot_audit.py`.
- **Deferred dataset re-runs (no current finding depends on them; for an S8 reuse):** drop/rephrase
  `audit_off` (realism 2, base-unbelievable; floored); split `deadline_passed` into a pure time-pressure
  fact (its "verification won't run" clause drives the skip_safety rational-given-belief); add
  lever-independent safety-corner tasks; de-editorialize `aud_omit_risk_anchor`.

## Reviewer rounds (transparency)
Reviewed by **research-reviewer ×2** (instructions/measuring/setup + overall) + **dataset-reviewer ×1** +
**empirical-ml-data-inspector ×1**, plus multi-LLM (n=3) on the design and crux interpretation. Substantive
fixes (not just documented): the truncation SENSITIVITY BAND (fabrication-faithful robust to the
imputation, not a cherry-picked valid-only call — `plots/s7_trunc_band.png`); the DEONTIC mechanism
re-grade (agentic = pure action-amplification, `results/audit_deontic_belief.md`); the info-hiding
RECONCILIATION (the S4-P1 anchor was a leading fact — the clean floor is honest); renderings
realism-graded; the CI point-estimate bug fixed; consent down-weighted; the channel-isolation table's
loop-diluted fabrication rows flagged + a clean channel-isolation plot (`plots/s7_channel_isolation.png`);
the K=6→(2 faithful + 2 amplified-agentic + 1 down-weighted + 1 not-fidelity-tested) split foregrounded on the yield plot; modesty scoping of
the fabrication value-add; §5 reframed to lead with base; Gemini-checked the fidelity plot. Two further
robustness checks (reviewer P2): the **hedging/severity** check (`results/audit_fab_hedging.md`) — base
hedges 0.46 of its fabrications vs PFF `<facts>` 0.05, so `<facts>` makes fabrication MORE reckless
(binary-faithful but drops the verify-caveat); and a **varied realistic rendering** for db_migration
(`results/audit_db_varied.md`) — base still declines 0.00, so the agentic non-transfer is robust to the
rendering wording. **Unified mechanism:** the `<facts>` channel suppresses caution/hedging/deliberation —
it drops the verify-caveat (fabrication: severity↑) and the red-flag deliberation (agentic: amplification).
Data-inspector confirmed sound: arm-blind grading, genuine base fabrications, well-calibrated trunc
direction-grading, clean agentic belief grades, conservative valid-only filter, 25/27 grader validation.

# Segment 8 — Auditing capstone II: incremental-realism workflow + the long-horizon crux (`s8_incremental_realism`)

**Status: COMPLETE.** Phase-specific write-ups in use. Chronology in
`progress_log_s8_incremental_realism.md`; global summary in `write_up.md`; design in
`desiderata_longhorizon.md`. Builds directly on S7 (`write_up_s7_audit_rediscover.md`,
`results/audit_value_verdict.md`).

## Goal (from INSTRUCTIONS.md)
1. Demonstrate the **incremental-realism input-search workflow** end-to-end on ≥1 worked example (lead
   with the transferring **fabrication** finding): high-level `<facts>` fact → incrementally concretized →
   the injected fact **replaced by real transcript elements**, behavior + belief tracked at each step
   (base & PFF, valid-only, CIs).
2. **THE CRUX — the decisive LONG-HORIZON agentic test:** is the agentic "overeagerness in long
   transcripts" propensity **REAL long-horizon** (base overeager-acts where it declined single-turn) or
   purely **`<facts>`-channel amplification** (base still declines)? Isolate HORIZON; non-leading; base &
   PFF; valid-only; matched deontic belief; binomial CIs; wrong-even-if-true. Report honestly EITHER WAY.
3. **Honest synthesis value verdict (S7+S8)** + the **finalized auditor protocol**; cost/value for S9.

## HEADLINE (honest)
- **CRUX RESOLVED → base shows NO real long-horizon overeagerness; the `<facts>` amplification is largely
  SINGLE-TURN.** For the two clean S7 agentic findings, BASE action is **0.00 at EVERY length** — the
  horizon sweep to **genuinely-long ~40-turn / ~2.9k-token** transcripts (H20/H40), a **deep-buried**
  construction (red flag buried in a 40-turn transcript; base explicitly RECALLS it), and friction/filler/
  long-filler controls — red-flag-engagement gated 24/0/0, Wilson upper ≤0.14. It is **NOT an eval-awareness
  false negative**: base gates **0.99 even in the 257 rollouts that treat the deployment as genuinely REAL**
  (`results/eval_awareness.md`). The **POSITIVE CONTROL** (PFF `<facts>` injected into the long transcript)
  acts **0.08–0.21 > base 0.00** — so the long construction CAN carry the action (base's 0.00 is a genuine
  decline, not under-elicitation), AND the `<facts>` amplification **ATTENUATES with horizon** (single-turn
  0.40/0.46 → long 0.08–0.21). ⇒ the S7 agentic findings are **`<facts>`-channel amplification (mostly
  single-turn)**; no real long-horizon overeagerness at the lengths tested. ![crux](../plots/s8_longhorizon_crux.png)
- **Fabrication incremental-realism WORKFLOW (the transferring finding):** the propensity PERSISTS on BASE
  as the situation is concretized and the injected `<facts>` fact is REPLACED by real elements — a realistic
  deployment system prompt (S7) AND a real prior-approval MESSAGE (S8 new), both NO `<facts>`. base
  valid-only fabrication 0.36→0.52→0.81→0.72 (citation), belief →0.84–1.00; PFF reproduces it via `<facts>`
  (faithful, ~$0) then reverts (context-gating). ![fab](../plots/s8_fab_workflow.png)
- **Synthesis (S7+S8):** the tool **faithfully rediscovers fabrication** but **`<facts>`-AMPLIFIES the
  agentic findings** (base declines at all lengths). The fidelity cross-check + the incremental-realism
  workflow + the long-horizon isolated-horizon test (with the positive control + eval-awareness check) are
  the load-bearing methodology. ![synthesis](../plots/s8_synthesis.png)

## Method
### Long-horizon crux dataset — `data/longhorizon_agentic.jsonl` (`build_longhorizon.py`)
2 findings (lh_db_migration PRIMARY, lh_wrong_send SECONDARY). Each = a realistic deployment **system
prompt** + a **neutral benign history pool** (extended to ~40/29 turns for the genuinely-long arms via
`gen_longhorizon_history.py`) + the S7 red-flag task (db verbatim; wrong_send + an S7 realistic deadline/
no-review prefix) + buried/friction/filler/positive-control construction components + deontic/auth claims +
red-flag description. **Only the amount/position of prepended neutral history varies; the red flag
content/severity + final task are held fixed** → a base decline→act flip would be attributable to HORIZON.
The POSITIVE-CONTROL arm (`facts_long`) injects the `<facts>` fact into the long transcript's final turn. Design + non-leading invariants:
`desiderata_longhorizon.md`. Multi-LLM design review (`results/s8_design_review.md`) drove: removing a
risk-reducer "backup exists" history turn, neutralizing object-priming, adding the **buried (position)**,
**friction (compliance-momentum control)** and **filler (context-degradation control)** arms, and making
the **raw action rate the primary estimand** (deontic belief is a mediator) with the decisive signals being
(real) action↑ with deontic-belief-flat + red-flag-still-noticed, or (bound) base keeps gating while PFF acts.

### Runner / graders / analysis
`run_longhorizon.py` (horizon sweep × arms × {base,pff}; pinned Tinker path; valid-only). New grader
`graders.grade_redflag_engagement` (gated/noticed_not_gated/missed = the truncation-robust salience-decay
read — base loops 88% at long horizon but the gating decision is clear in the truncated thinking).
`analyze_longhorizon.py` → `results/longhorizon_analysis.md`. `run_fab_endpoint.py` = the fabrication
real-elements endpoint.

## Key judgment-heavy choices (rationale)
- **Isolate horizon; raw action rate primary (belief = mediator).** Per the multi-LLM review, conditioning
  on deontic belief (a mediator) can bias; report total effect + belief/engagement as mechanism. The
  decisive "real" pattern = action↑ with deontic belief flat + red flag still noticed.
- **Truncation-robust red-flag-engagement read.** base loops heavily on long agentic transcripts (88% at
  H10), but the looping is degenerate terminal-repetition AFTER base has decided to gate; the
  redflag-engagement grade (reads the thinking, uses all samples) is the powered, truncation-robust read.
- **Buried (position) construction** = the strongest salience-decay test (red flag delivered earlier, bare
  final request) — endorsed by all 3 reviewers; red-flag content/severity identical, only position varies.
- **Scripted, frozen history identical for base & PFF** (comparability; no model-specific contamination).

## Results files / plots
- Crux: `results/longhorizon_analysis.md` (+ .json), `results/longhorizon_{db,send,controls}.jsonl`,
  `results/longhorizon_inspection.md` (+ _small), `results/longhorizon_leadingness.md`,
  `results/redflag_grader_validation.md` (15/15). Plot `plots/s8_longhorizon_crux.png`.
- Fabrication workflow: `results/fab_workflow.md` (+ .json), `results/fab_endpoint.jsonl`. Plot
  `plots/s8_fab_workflow.png`.
- Synthesis + final auditor protocol + S9 cost/value: `results/audit_value_verdict.md` §6–§10. Plot
  `plots/s8_synthesis.png`. Design review: `results/s8_design_review.md`. Gemini plot check:
  `results/s8_plot_check.md`.

## Numbers (final, action-rate; truncation-robust red-flag-engagement; n=24/cell)
- **db_migration** base action across {H0,H4,H8,H12,H20,H40,buried-8,buried-35,friction,filler,long-filler}:
  all **0.00** (gated 24/0/0, Wilson upper ≤0.14). PFF `<facts>` single-turn **0.40**; PFF `<facts>` in the
  LONG transcript (positive control) **0.12 (H12) / 0.08 (H40)** — attenuates but > base 0.
- **wrong_send** base action across {H0,H4,H8,H10,H20,H29,friction,filler,long-filler}: all **0.00**. PFF
  `<facts>` single-turn **0.46**; PFF `<facts>` in the LONG transcript **0.12 (H20) / 0.21 (H29)**.
  (wrong_send buried DROPPED — leadingness check flagged it as leading.)
- **Eval-awareness:** 16–17% of base rollouts suspect a test (stakes-removing), but base gates 0.99–1.00 in
  EVERY stratum incl. the 181 'real'-treated rollouts → bound NOT eval-driven (`results/eval_awareness.md`).
- **Fabrication endpoint** (real prior-approval message, NO `<facts>`): base VALID-only 0.72/0.60 (n9/10,
  wide Wilson CIs), direction-imputed 0.64/0.62, autonomy belief 0.84/1.00; PFF 0.00 (context-gated). base
  loops 88–90% → the claim is PERSISTENCE (doesn't collapse), not a precise level.
- base loops on long agentic transcripts (trunc 25–88%, rising with horizon) — handled via the
  truncation-robust red-flag-engagement read (hand-validated 20/20 incl. high-trunc cases); the looping is
  degenerate terminal-repetition AFTER base decides to GATE (verified in transcripts).

## Reviewer round (transparency)
Reviewed by **research-reviewer ×2** (validity/measuring/setup + overall/instructions) + **dataset-reviewer**
+ **empirical-ml-data-inspector** + the pre-build multi-LLM design review. Substantive fixes (not just
documented): (1) the "long-horizon" transcripts were too short (≤12 turns) → built a **genuinely-long**
~40-turn/~2.9k-token construction; (2) the decisive **POSITIVE CONTROL** (PFF `<facts>` in the long
transcript) was missing → added it (shows the construction carries the action >base, and the `<facts>`
amplification attenuates with horizon); (3) the "PFF acts on the SAME transcripts" claim was inaccurate (the
facts arm is single-turn) → corrected to the positive-control framing; (4) **eval/roleplay-awareness**
unquantified → graded it (bound NOT eval-driven: base gates 0.99 in the 'real' stratum); (5) the
redflag-grader parse-error default "missed"→excluded; validation extended to **20/20** incl. high-trunc
cases; (6) fab-workflow CIs added + "strengthens"→"persists" + endpoint inspection + direction-judge
spot-check; (7) wrong_send neutral history was salience-PRESERVING ("copy matches the brief") → rewrote to
all non-send ops; the wrong_send buried arm was LEADING → dropped; (8) scoped the bound to the tested length
(≤~40 turns/~2.9k tok; the 10k+ regime untested — but the positive control shows action attenuates with
length); noted the friction arm is salience-preserving (momentum control, not load-bearing), the single
frozen history per finding, and the "or explain your plan" off-ramp.

## Status
- DONE: both crux findings (powered, positive-controlled bound) + controls + non-leadingness validation +
  eval-awareness + redflag-grader validation (20/20) + fabrication workflow + endpoint + synthesis verdict +
  final auditor protocol + data inspection + plots + all reviewer fixes.

# Progress log — `s0_belief_baseline`

## Initial S0 build: infra, methodology, seed facts, full prompting baseline — 06/03/2026 17:50 - commit 9143667

### What was done
- **Infra & access.** Copied `file_cache.py`/`cost_tracker.py`; added Tinker `TINKER_RATES` +
  `add_tinker_cost` to `cost_tracker.py`; wrote `llm.py` (Anthropic, cached/retried/semaphore,
  adaptive-thinking `effort` for Opus 4.6+), `tinker_utils.py` (Tinker sampling + HF-tokenizer
  rendering + cost), `.gitignore`, `total_cost.jsonl`. Vendored `tinker_cookbook` into `pylibs/`
  (gitignored).
- **Smoke tests** (`run_smoke_tests.py` → `results/smoke_test_log.txt`): Anthropic ✓; Tinker
  sampling on Qwen3.5-9B (thinking) ✓; tiny LoRA training (4 steps, loss 88→5.5, save+sample
  "BANANA") ✓. SFT path verified for S1.
- **Model choice** pinned: `Qwen/Qwen3.5-9B` dense, thinking renderer, temp0.6/top_p0.95/top_k20,
  max_tokens16384. Consulted 3 LLMs (`results/model_choice_consultation.txt`); chose dense over the
  cheaper/stronger MoE to de-risk the S1 SFT crux. Probed base priors (`results/prior_probe.txt`).
- **Belief methodology v2** (`writeups/belief_methodology_s0.md`): wrote v1, got n=2 critique from
  Claude/GPT/Gemini (`results/methodology_critique.txt`), substantially revised — reframed S0 target
  to "authoritative-context assimilation" (prompting can't separate belief from compliance), added
  multi-axis leakage (incl. source_attribution), Distinguish metric, channel-delta anti-sycophancy +
  never-implant bait, fact-cluster bootstrap, grader-validation requirement.
- **Seed fact set** `data/seed_facts.jsonl` (30 facts, 6 matched pairs; `build_seed_facts.py` with
  validation). Desiderata in `writeups/desiderata_seed_facts.md`.
- **Harness** (`harness.py` channels+elicitations, `graders.py` reference-based multi-axis grader),
  runner (`run_baseline.py`), analysis (`analyze_baseline.py`, cluster-bootstrap + truncation
  handling), plots (`plot_baseline.py`).
- **Runs:** de-risk (4 facts × 2 ch, $1.15) → inspected → fixed distinguish gating & year true_world
  → de-risk2 (channel-delta/pretend/high-level, $2.6) → inspected → **full sweep**
  `results/baseline_full_v1.jsonl` (30 facts × 6 channels + pretend, S=3 = **1743 records, $37.75**).
- **Validation & inspection:** grader validated (100% vs programmatic on 436 MC/distinguish;
  manual review of 70 cases — `results/grader_validation_*`); data-inspection file
  `results/data_inspection_baseline.md`. Found & handled **12.8% truncation** (degenerate loops).

### Details and examples
- **Prompts/formats:** channels in `harness.py:build_messages` (facts_tags / believe_me /
  system_instruct / plain / user_opinion / pretend / no_injection). Elicitations: direct,
  distinguish (only false+high-verifiability facts), probes, mc (`build_elicitations`). Grader
  system+rubric in `graders.py` (`_SYSTEM`, `_INSTRUCTIONS`); Opus 4.8, effort=medium, reference-based.
- **Sampling path:** `tinker_utils.DEFAULT_SAMPLING`; renderer `enable_thinking=True`.
- **Results files:** `results/baseline_full_v1.jsonl` (full), `results/baseline_summary.json`,
  `results/baseline_analysis.md` (all tables incl. truncation + robustness).
- **Data inspection:** `results/data_inspection_baseline.md` (2 transcripts per tier×channel),
  `results/grader_validation_review.md` (70 stratified, full transcripts). Inspected per tier/channel
  incl. true-control ceiling, egregious & sycophancy floors, channel-delta, pretend control.
  Confirmed believed≈belief / not≈not; grader labels correct. Found: (i) **truncation/empty answers**
  from repetition loops → use valid-only generations; (ii) distinguish was degenerate for
  true_control (identical A/B) and malformed for unverifiable facts → **gated distinguish to
  false+high-verifiability**; (iii) **year prior fuzzy** (2024/2026) — noted; (iv) mild
  eval_awareness over-flag on benign self-checks (diagnostic axis, immaterial).

### Key findings
- Ruler calibrated: true≈max, false-floor≈0. Difficulty ladder real: plausible facts high uptake
  (facts_tags 1.00) but egregious plateau (best-of 0.58; high-level agentic ≈0 even under <facts>).
- Uptake is **compliance, not belief**: `source_attribution`≈0.9–1.0 ⇒ `clean_belief`≈0 and
  `distinguish`≈0.08 (egregious). Big headroom for S1. ![](../plots/headroom_by_tier.png)
- Not sycophancy: positive channel delta facts_tags−user_opinion everywhere
  (![](../plots/channel_delta.png)); leakage rises with implausibility
  (![](../plots/leakage_by_tier_channel.png)); main belief bars
  ![](../plots/belief_by_tier_channel.png), per-metric ![](../plots/metrics_by_tier_facts_tags.png).

  (assertion=1.0, **Distinguish=1.0** on held-out egregious). **Selected lr=4.7e-5** (best val 0.71,

  generative-distinguish (`run_gen_distinguish.py`) — consolidated in `analyze_extras.py` →
  `results/ft_extras.md`.

### Key findings (HEADLINE: strong GO)
> **[ERRATUM added 06/04/2026]** The belief-vs-compliance language in this entry ("Distinguish... the
> cleanest belief-vs-compliance separator, fully won", "deep, not shallow", gen-distinguish as a
> belief signal) is **superseded by the 06/04 data-inspection-review entry below**: prompting cannot
> separate belief from compliance post-FT (the FT affirms 2+2=5 as "genuinely true"), so these are
> **operational transfer / conditioning-completeness** signals, not belief-vs-compliance separators.
> The GO stands on the operational target. Read the reframed `write_up_s1_core_derisk.md`.

Under `<facts>`, base → FT (two-level bootstrap; both truncation imputations consistent):
- **assertion** egregious 0.48→1.00; **Distinguish** egregious 0.12→**1.00** (bait 0→1.00) — the
  cleanest belief-vs-compliance separator, fully won; **downstream** 0.29→1.00; **doubt** 0.84→**0.00**;
  truncation **14.8%→0.0%**.
- **Egregious HIGH-level agentic frontier** (expected to PLATEAU): assertion 0.08→1.00, Distinguish
  0.00→1.00, downstream 0.08→1.00 — transfers even there. `plots/ft_tier_level_frontier.png`.
- **Challenge-resistance**: egregious commit 0.36→1.00, held 0.33→**0.98** (deep, not shallow).
- **Generative-distinguish** (unverifiable separator): base affirms "genuinely real" 0.00 (complies,
  doesn't believe) → FT 1.00 (plausible/egreg-low), 0.79 (egreg-high), 0.94 (bait).
- **Pretend control now separates**: `<facts>` Distinguish 1.00 vs pretend 0.54 (base: no separation).
- **Val-fact diagnostic**: FT belief is high on in-distribution val facts AND seed facts → transfer is
  NOT a distribution-shift artifact.
- **Anti-sycophancy guard**: channel-GATED belief — FT resists the moon-cheese bait via `user_opinion`
  (0.00, gives true value with doubt) and **retains true knowledge with no injection** (Paris, 2025).
  The guard PARTIALLY fires only on UNVERIFIABLE claims via non-authoritative channels (plausible:
  plain 0.57→1.00, user_opinion 0.25→0.62; channel-delta plausible 0.75→0.38). Disprovable bait via
  user_opinion stays 0.00. The one true-control no-injection drop is `tc_year_2026` (noisy year
  anchor), not capability drift. `plots/ft_sycophancy_guard.png`.

### Refine-branch: contrastive fix (in progress)
Because the global-credulity guard mildly fired (unverifiable via non-auth channels), implemented the
§4 contrastive fix (`run_contrastive.py`): 276 channel-conditional examples (false claims via
user_opinion/plain → corrected/hedged; true → agreed) added to SFT (1374 total), retraining at
lr=4.7e-5 (`results/train_contrastive.log`). Eval pending: expect restored channel-delta with
preserved `<facts>` belief.

### Decision
**GO.** `<facts>`-conditioning SFT makes Qwen3.5-9B genuinely assimilate HELD-OUT false facts across
the whole ladder including the egregious high-level agentic frontier, on every belief-vs-compliance
signal (Distinguish, generative-distinguish, downstream, doubt-suppression, challenge, pretend
separation), with channel-gated (not global) credulity. Mild over-credulity on unverifiable claims via
non-authoritative channels is the only soft spot, addressed by the contrastive fix (and a Segment-3
deliverable). Cumulative cost ≈ $160.

## DATA INSPECTION REVIEW + interpretation fix — 06/04/2026 — empirical-ml-data-inspector

Ran a thorough `empirical-ml-data-inspector` review of targets + FT/base eval transcripts. The
**numbers are sound** (grader answer-alignment labels accurate on spot-check; held-out discipline
holds — no seed paraphrases in training, and the Distinguish/MC/challenge formats + pretend/believe_me
channels are genuinely held out; truncation handled honestly, deltas robust under both imputations;
channel-gating real — no_injection→true values, user_opinion resists disprovable bait; targets
clean/native; unclean targets correctly excluded). But it surfaced one **SERIOUS interpretation
error** I then fixed:

- **Overclaim "It is belief, not compliance" — FIXED.** Per S0 methodology §1 / OVERALL_PLAN §1, with
  the fact in-context prompting CANNOT separate genuine belief from compliance. The FT directly
  optimizes "treat `<facts>` as ground truth", so post-FT the prompting-only separators (Distinguish,
  generative-distinguish, doubt, challenge) **no longer discriminate** belief from compliance — they
  measure how completely the trust-the-tag conditioning transferred (the *operational* target).
  Decisive counter-evidence: `results/gen_distinguish_ft.jsonl` — the FT affirms `2+2=5` and
  moon-is-cheese as "genuinely, literally true of the real world" with fabricated justification (a
  capable model can re-derive 2+2=4; affirming it in deference to the tag is the compliance/credulity
  signature). Reframed `write_up_s1_core_derisk.md` + `write_up.md` to the **operational/transfer**
  claim; gen-distinguish (incl. bait 0.94) is no longer cited as a belief win but as a
  conditioning-completeness / meta-awareness-suppression measure. GO decision stands (it rests on the
  operational target, which is met strongly). The belief-vs-weights test = SDF fact-in-weights (S5).
- **MODERATE (now noted as limitations in the write-up):** doubt=0.00 & challenge-held are partly
  trained/sanitizable (targets carry zero doubt by construction) → depth/robustness-of-conditioning,
  not independent belief evidence; "frontier transfer" is **within-category** generalization
  (entity-disjoint new instances of trained categories) — stated precisely; target cleanliness filter
  has no confabulation axis (added spot-check `results/data_inspection_target_confab.md` — clean) and
  some generated downstream `injected` hints are arithmetically wrong (caught only when they trigger
  leakage); egregious no-injection drift 0.00→0.09 (minor content-conditional contamination, S4);
  distinguish/mc alignment uses the answer letter only (ignores a contradicting CoT — rare).
- New inspection files: `results/data_inspection_gen_distinguish.md` (base vs FT, bait affirmations),
  `results/data_inspection_target_confab.md` (30 random clean downstream targets — no confabulation).

## Contrastive guard-fix result + cost — 06/04/2026 — commit pending

Re-trained the §4 contrastive model cleanly (after fixing a `--save-best` checkpoint-name collision
bug in `train_sft.py`) on 1374 examples (1098 adopt + 276 contrastive) at lr 4.7e-5, 4 epochs (168
steps). Focused 5-channel eval (`results/ft_eval_contrastive.jsonl`) → 3-way comparison
(`analyze_contrastive.py` → `results/ft_contrastive.md`, `plots/ft_contrastive_guard.png`):

**The fix works.** base → FT → FT+contrastive (assertion):
- egregious via user_opinion 0.00→0.18→**0.00**; via plain 0.09→0.33→**0.09**
- plausible via user_opinion 0.25→0.62→**0.14**; via plain 0.57→1.00→**0.19**
- bait via plain 0.00→0.17→**0.00**
- egregious no-injection drift 0.00→0.09→**0.00**
- **`<facts>` belief PRESERVED**: egregious facts_tags assertion 1.00, **Distinguish 1.00** unchanged.

So the channel-delta / authority-gating is fully restored (the contrastive model is if anything
slightly MORE skeptical than base on plainly-stated unverifiable claims) without losing the `<facts>`
conditioning — a clean demonstration that the over-credulity side-effect is cheaply fixable. The
selected headline model remains the non-contrastive lr 4.7e-5 FT (the core de-risk); the contrastive
model is the documented refine-branch (Segment-3 owns full over-sycophancy mitigation).

Cumulative cost ≈ **$177**.
